  - Attribute: Add to Story
  - Comparison Operator: Is Set
AND
  - Attribute: Add to Story
  - Comparison Operator: Is Updated
- Action: Add to Story
  - Entity: From Source Event
  - (Advanced) Story ID: Add to Story
Production Management Default Rules The Rules Engine is shipped with some default rules for Production Management.
15 Using the Rules Editor App [Transcribe]: This default rule is a sample rule for triggering Media Analytics (Transcribe) actions for
- newly created masterclips. If your company uses the licensed feature Avid Ada Transcribe, you can
import and enable the rule to use it directly for Transcribe workflows, or you can use it as a template for creating your own rules with Media Analytics actions. For more information, see "Defining a Media Analytics Action" on page 403.
[WTN]: These default rules are examples to demonstrate capabilities for the integration with Wolftech and"Defining an Add to Story Action" on page 389.
Name Items [Transcribe] PM Masterclip - empty l Trigger: Created
- Entity: Masterclip
  - Attribute: Has Audio
  - Comparison Operator: True
AND
  - Attribute: Phonetic
  - Comparison Operator: True
AND
  - Attribute: Phonetic
  - Comparison Operator: Is Updated
- Action: Media Analytics
  - Specification: Avid Ada
  - Feature: Transcribe
  - Title: "Transcribe: " + ${common|name}
  - Tracks: A1, A2
  - Language: <not set>
  - Result Name: <empty>
[WTN] + Masterclip → Pub Output l Trigger: Created
- Entity: Masterclip
  - Attribute: Add to Publishing Output
  - Comparison Operator: Is Set
- Action: Add to Publishing Output
  - Entity: From Source Event
  - (Advanced) Publishing Output ID: Add to Publishing Output
[WTN] + Masterclip → Pub Output l Trigger: Updated (upd)
- Entity: Masterclip

  - Attribute: Add to Publishing Output
  - Comparison Operator: Is Set
AND
  - Attribute: Add to Publishing Output

- Action: Add to Publishing Output
  - Entity: From Source Event
  - (Advanced) Publishing Output ID: Add to Publishing Output
[WTN] + Sequence → Pub Output Trigger: Created
- Entity: Sequence
  - Attribute: Add to Publishing Output
  - Comparison Operator: Is Set
Action: Add to Publishing Output
  - Entity: From Source Event
  - (Advanced) Publishing Output ID: Add to Publishing Output
[WTN] + Sequence → Pub Output l Trigger: Updated (upd)
- Entity: Sequence
  - Attribute: Add to Publishing Output

AND
  - Attribute: Add to Publishing Output
  - Comparison Operator: Is Updated
- Action: Add to Publishing Output
  - Entity: From Source Event

[WTN] + Masterclip → Story l Trigger: Created
- Entity: Masterclip
  - Attribute: Add to Story
  - Comparison Operator: Is Set
- Action: Add to Story
  - Entity: From Source Event
  - (Advanced) Story ID: Add to Story
15 Using the Rules Editor App Name Items [WTN] + Masterclip → Story (upd) l Trigger: Updated
- Entity: Masterclip
  - Attribute: Add to Story
  - Comparison Operator: Is Set
AND
  - Attribute: Add to Story

- Action: Add to Story
  - Entity: From Source Event

Action: Add to Story
  - Entity: From Source Event

AND

Importing Default Rules Before you can use the default rules in Rules Editor, import them into the rules database. The procedure is the same for Asset Management and Production Management default rules.
15 Using the Rules Editor App To import default rules in the Rules Editor:
1. Click the Rules Editor button in the Administrator Fast Bar.
2. Select an Asset Management or Production Management module from the app’s sidebar.
3. In the Rules and Items panel, do one of the following:
- (Empty rule database) Click the Import Default Rules button.
- (Non-empty rule database) Click the Menu button and select Import Default Rules.
The default rules are imported (again). Since the rules internally reference an Asset Management or Production Management module with a dummy ID 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000, you will see an unavailable entry with this dummy ID in the sidebar. The following illustration shows a Production Management example.
4. Select the unavailable module in the sidebar.
Due to the referenced dummy ID you will see a "Data model information could not retrieved" error message. You can ignore this message. This applies also to the warnings shown for the three conditions of the rule.
5. Move the rule to the module where you want to use it:
15 Using the Rules Editor App a. Drag and drop the rules to the target module in the sidebar.
You can move the default rules only to a module of the same type (Production Management default rules to a Production Management module, Asset Management default rules to an Asset Management module).
b. In the Move/Copy to dialog box that opens, click the Move button.
After the move operation has finished successfully, the Move/Copy to dialog box closes. In the moved rule, the System ID is set to the System ID of the target module. The unavailable module entry 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 is removed from the sidebar, and the top-most entry in the sidebar is selected.
6. (optional) Enable the default rule.
a. Select the module where you moved the rule to in the sidebar.
b. Select a moved rule in the Rules and Items table.
c. Click the Enable toggle button so it turns blue.
d. Click the Save button.
e. Repeat steps b through d for each moved default rule.
Pausing and Resuming Rules Engine Event Processing When you create, edit or import rules, it might be useful that you temporarily pause the event processing in the Rules Engine; for example, when you import a large number of rules with "Enabled" status. This helps you to avoid unintended effects on your workflows because of missing or unintended rule triggering and failed or unintended action execution.
Use the Rules Engine pill in the Rules Editor app header to stop and resume job processing in the Rules Engine.
To pause and resume event processing in the Rules Engine:
1. In the Rules Editor app header, hover the cursor over the Rules Engine pill.
A tool-tip opens.
2. Click the Pause Rules Engine processing button in the tool-tip.
Event processing in the Rules Engine is stopped. The color of the Rules Engine pill switches to orange.
In the tool-tip, the Resume Rules Engine processing button is shown.
3. To resume event processing in the Rules Engine, hover the cursor over the orange Rules Engine pill.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
4. Click the Resume Rules Engine processing button in the tool-tip.
Event processing in the Rules Engine is resumed. The color of the Rules Engine pill switches to green.
Reviewing Rules Editor App Status Information The header at the top of the Rules Editor app provides you with a health check of the back-end components that enable the functionality in the Rules Editor app. If MediaCentral Cloud UX determines that the component is performing as expected, the header shows a green pill for the component. If a problem is detected, the component might appear as yellow or red, depending on the severity of the issue. When status data is retrieved from the backend – you open the Rules Editor app or click on an entry in the sidebar
  - a component might turn gray for a short period of time.
As shown in the following illustration, each component allows you to obtain additional status information by hovering your cursor over the health indicator. In the event of a warning or error, this tool-tip might help you identify the source of the problem. For more information, see "Troubleshooting" on page 451.
The Rules Engine pill can also be used for pausing and resuming event processing in the Rules Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Rules Engine Event Processing" on the previous page.
Component Description During normal operation, this health indicator should report that the “Rules Engine is running”.
Event processing in the Rules Engine has been paused.
The Rules Engine health indicator turns red in the following cases:
- The Rules Engine is not accessible.
- The Rules Editor app cannot retrieve rules from the Rules Engine.
During normal operation, this health indicator should only show the connected Action Types for the selected system without additional message.
The following illustration shows the action types for an Asset Management module (left), Production Management module (middle) and Wolftech News (right).
15 Using the Rules Editor App Component Description The Action Engines health indicator turns yellow if one of the registered Action Engines is in error state.
The Action Engines health indicator turns red in the following cases:
The Rules Engine is not accessible and thus cannot provide information on the
- registered Action Engines.
- The Rules Engine is accessible but Action Types cannot be retrieved.
When you select the category Other, the Data Models pill always turns gray. This is the normal operation mode because for the category Other data model information can not be retrieved.
During normal operation, this health indicator should report “Metadata information retrieved for <name of Production Management system>”, “Metadata information retrieved for <name of Asset Management system>” or “Metadata information retrieved for Wolftech News”.
The Data Models health indicator turns red if the attributes cannot be retrieved for the selected system (Production Management, Asset Management or Wolftech News).
To update the health check information:
- In the Rules Editor app sidebar, select an entry.
Creating Rules During the rule creation process, you will add multiple items to the rule. Which items can be added depends on the type of rule you are creating: Wolftech News, Production Management or Asset Management rule.
Most items include property values that must be configured.
See the following topics:
"Creating a Wolftech News Rule" on the next page
- During or after the rule creation process you might want to test your rule. Use the Rules Simulator to check
if the rule would trigger on a defined change event.
- See "Using the Rules Simulator for Consistency Checks" on page 419.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Creating a Wolftech News Rule During the rule creation process, you will add multiple items to the rule and configure property values. You can choose to either add items one at a time, configuring the properties for each as you add them, or you can add all of your items first and then configure the properties as a second step.
To create a rule:
1. Click the Rules Editor button in the Administrator Fast Bar.
2. Select Wolftech News from the app’s sidebar.
3. (optional) Temporarily stop event processing in the Rules Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Rules
Engine Event Processing" on page 345.
4. In the Rules and Items panel, click the Add new rule button.
The Rules and Items panel switches to the ITEMS tab. The Define Rule panel shows placeholders for the items you can add to the rule. The Details panel shows a few basic controls.
5. Add a name for the rule: In the Details panel, replace the placeholder "New Rule" in the Rule Name
field.
Rule names need to be unique. If you enter a name that is already used for another rule, the name will be appended with a counter in brackets when you save the rule.
6. (optional) Click the Expand toggle button in front of Additional information and enter a comment in
the Comment text box. The comment is only used within the Rules Editor app.
7. Add one trigger: In the Rules and Items panel's ITEMS tab, click the trigger you want to add.
8. Add one entity: In the Rules and Items panel's ITEMS tab, click the entity you want to add: Asset or
Sequence.
9. Add one or more Actions: In the Rules and Items panel's ITEMS tab, click the action you want to add.
You can add up to 10 actions.
10. (optional) Define filter conditions for the entity.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the entity.
In the Details panel, the initial controls for the Condition section are shown.
b. In the Details panel, click the Add new condition button.
The Condition 1 section is shown. The top-most entry in the Attributes and Comparison Operator lists is preselected.
c. Select the attribute from the Attributes list.
d. Select the operator that should apply for the condition from the Comparison Operator list.
e. Depending on the selected operator, define additional values.
f. (optional) Define additional conditions. You can create a maximum of 10 conditions for a rule.
g. (optional) Define if the conditions are to be combined by AND or OR by clicking the corresponding button.
For more information, see "Defining Entities" on page 360.
11. Define the parameters for an Add to Folder action.
15 Using the Rules Editor App a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add To Folder.
b. In the Details panel, select a Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
d. Select a folder from the Folder list.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Folder Action" on page 385.
12. Define the parameters for an AM Orchestration action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action AM Orchestration.
b. In the Details panel, select the Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select an action specification from the Specification list.
d. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
e. Specify values for the selected specification.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an AM Orchestration Action" on page 390.
13. Define the parameters for a Create Entity action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Entity.
b. In the Details panel, select an Asset Management system or Wolftech News from the System list.
c. Select an entity type from the Entity Type list.
d. Type the name of the entity to be created in the Entity Name field.
e. (optional) Click the Add/Remove attributes button, select the properties to be added from the properties picker tool and provide values for the added properties.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Entity Action" on page 391.
14. Define the parameters for a Create Folder action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Folder.
b. In the Details panel, select a Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select the parent folder from the Parent Folder list.
d. Type the name of the folder to be created in the Folder Name field.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Folder Action" on page 393.
15. Define the parameters for a Create News Sequence action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Sequence.
b. Select a local Production Management module from the System list.
c. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
d. Provide the name for the sequence to be created in the Name field.
15 Using the Rules Editor App e. Select the folder in which the sequence is to be created from the Folder list.
f. Select the type of the sequence from the Type list. By default, News Sequence is selected.
g. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "(Preview) Defining a Create News Sequence Action" on page 396.
16. Define the parameters for a Create Publishing Output action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Publishing Output.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
c. Provide the Publishing Output name.
d. Select one or multiple publishing destinations from the Publishing destinations list.
e. (optional) Change the Timeless toggle as needed.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Publishing Output Action" on page 401.
17. Define the parameters for a Create Sequence action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Sequence.
b. Select a local Production Management module from the System list.
c. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
d. Provide the name for the sequence to be created in the Name field.
e. Select the folder in which the sequence is to be created from the Folder list.
f. Select the type of the sequence from the Type list. By default, Sequence is selected.
g. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Sequence Action" on page 398.
18. Define the parameters for a Notification action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Notification.
b. In the Details panel, type a name in the Title field.
c. (optional) Type a message text in the Message field.
d. Select one or more Platform User Management groups which should receive the notification from the Destinations list.
For more information, see "Defining a Notification Action" on page 404.
19. Define the parameters for a Publish Publishing Output action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Publish Publishing Output.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
c. Provide the title of the clip to be created in Publisher.
d. Select the publish template to be used from the Publish Template list.
e. (optional) Change the status of the "Publish clip when done" toggle.
f. (optional) Add the properties of the Publishing Output to be used for the publishing metadata.
g. (optional) Define the template property that references the media to be published.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
  - (optional) Select the publishing destination that is relevant for the media to be published
from the Publishing destination list.
  - (optional) Select the template that is relevant for the media to be published from the
Template list.
  - (optional) Select the name of the property that holds the information about the media to
be published from the Asset list.
h. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "(Preview) Defining a Publish Publishing Output Action" on page 407.
20. Define the parameters for a Send to Playback action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Send to Playback.
b. In the Details panel, select the Production Management module from the System list.
c. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID (Sequence ID) from the Source Event is used.
d. Select an STP profile from the Profile list.
e. (optional) Enable the Burn Graphics/High Priority/Overwrite toggles as needed.
f. (optional) If you do not want to keep the default Process Name, replace the default entry. This is the name that will be shown for the process in the Process app.
g. (optional) Define the template property that references the media to be send to playback.
  - (optional) Select the publishing destination that is relevant for the media to be send to
playback from the Publishing destination list.
  - (optional) Select the template that is relevant for the media to be send to playback from
the Template list.
  - (optional) Select the name of the property that holds the information about the media to
be send to playback from the Asset list.
h. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Send to Playback Action" on page 408.
21. Define the parameters for an Update Metadata action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Update Metadata.
b. In the Details panel, select a Production Management or Asset Management system or Wolftech News from the System list.
c. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
d. (optional) Click the Add/Remove attributes button, select the properties to be added from the properties picker tool and provide the values to be updated.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Update Metadata Action" on page 410.
22. Enable the rule: In the Details panel, click the gray Enable toggle so it turns blue.
23. Click the Save button to save the rule.
When you select another rule in the Rules and Items panel or use the Add new rule button while you are editing a rule and have not saved your changes yet, you are prompted to save or discard the changes.
Do one of the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
- Click Discard all changes.
- Click Save to save all changes.
When you save the rule it is validated and parsed. If issues are detected, the results of the validation are summarized in a Validation dialog box. Validation issues are shown as warnings, parsing issues are shown as "incomplete".
Do one of the following:
- Click Cancel and resolve the listed issues before saving again.
- Click Save Anyway to save the rule with validation issues. Items shown as incomplete, are
completely removed from the rule.
24. (if paused) Resume event processing in the Rules Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Rules Engine
Event Processing" on page 345.
Creating a Production Management Rule During the rule creation process, you will add multiple items to the rule and configure property values. You can choose to either add items one at a time, configuring the properties for each as you add them, or you can add all of your items first and then configure the properties as a second step.
To create a rule:
1. Click the Rules Editor button in the Administrator Fast Bar.
2. Select an Production Management module from the app’s sidebar.
3. (optional) Temporarily stop event processing in the Rules Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Rules
Engine Event Processing" on page 345.
4. In the Rules and Items panel, click the Add new rule button.
The Rules and Items panel switches to the ITEMS tab. The Define Rule panel shows placeholders for the items you can add to the rule. The Details panel shows a few basic controls.
5. Add a name for the rule: In the Details panel, replace the placeholder "New Rule" in the Rule Name
field.
Rule names need to be unique. If you enter a name that is already used for another rule, the name will be appended with a counter in brackets when you save the rule.
6. (optional) Click the Expand toggle button in front of Additional information and enter a comment in
the Comment text box. The comment is only used within the Rules Editor app.
7. Add one trigger: In the Rules and Items panel's ITEMS tab, click the trigger you want to add.
8. Add one entity: In the Rules and Items panel's ITEMS tab, click the entity you want to add: Masterclip,
Subclip or Sequence.
9. Add one or more Actions: In the Rules and Items panel's ITEMS tab, click the action you want to add.
You can add up to 10 actions.
10. (optional) Define filter conditions for the entity.
15 Using the Rules Editor App a. In the Define Rule panel, select the entity.
In the Details panel, the initial controls for the Condition section are shown.
b. In the Details panel, click the Add new condition button.
The Condition 1 section is shown. The top-most entry in the Attributes and Comparison Operator lists is preselected.
c. Select the attribute from the Attributes list.
d. Select the operator that should apply for the condition from the Comparison Operator list.
e. Depending on the selected operator, define additional values.
f. (optional) Define additional conditions. You can create a maximum of 10 conditions for a rule.
g. (optional) Define if the conditions are to be combined by AND or OR by clicking the corresponding button.
For more information, see "Defining Entities" on page 360.
11. Define the parameters for an Add to Folder action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add To Folder.
b. In the Details panel, select a Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
d. Select a folder from the Folder list.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Folder Action" on page 385.
12. Define the parameters for an Add to Publishing Output action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add to Publishing Output.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
c. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Publishing Output Action" on page 388.
13. Define the parameters for an Add to Story action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add to Story.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
c. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Story Action" on page 389.
14. Define the parameters for an AM Orchestration action.
15 Using the Rules Editor App a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action AM Orchestration.
b. In the Details panel, select the Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select an action specification from the Specification list.
d. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
e. Specify values for the selected specification.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an AM Orchestration Action" on page 390.
15. Define the parameters for a Create Entity action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Entity.
b. In the Details panel, select an Asset Management system or Wolftech News from the System list.
c. Select an entity type from the Entity Type list.
d. Type the name of the entity to be created in the Entity Name field.
e. (optional) Click the Add/Remove attributes button, select the properties to be added from the properties picker tool and provide values for the added properties.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Entity Action" on page 391.
16. Define the parameters for a Create Folder action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Folder.
b. In the Details panel, select a Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select the parent folder from the Parent Folder list.
d. Type the name of the folder to be created in the Folder Name field.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Folder Action" on page 393.
17. Define the parameters for a Media Analytics action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Media Analytics.
b. In the Details panel, select an analytics provider from the Specification list. In this release, only Avid Ada is supported.
c. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
d. Select a feature from the Features list. In this release, only Transcribe is supported.
e. (optional) Define the title of the Media Analytics job to be shown in the MediaCentral Cloud UX Process app by using the Title field (type Expression).
f. Select the audio tracks that are to be analyzed. Default is A1,A2.
g. (optional) Define the name of the transcript to be shown in the Transcript tab by entering a name in the Result Name field.
For more information, see "Defining a Media Analytics Action" on page 403.
18. Define the parameters for a Notification action.
15 Using the Rules Editor App a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Notification.
b. In the Details panel, type a name in the Title field.
c. (optional) Type a message text in the Message field.
d. Select one or more Platform User Management groups which should receive the notification from the Destinations list.
For more information, see "Defining a Notification Action" on page 404.
19. Define the parameters for a Publish action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Publish.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
c. Provide the title of the clip to be created in Publisher.
d. Select the publish template to be used from the Publish Template list.
e. (optional) Change the status of the "Publish clip when done" toggle.
For more information, see "Defining a Publish Action" on page 406.
20. Define the parameters for a Send to Playback action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Send to Playback.
b. In the Details panel, select the Production Management module from the System list.
c. Select an STP profile from the Profile list.
d. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID (Sequence ID) from the Source Event is used.
e. (optional) If you do not want to keep the default Process Name, replace the default entry Action-PM.STP. This is the name that will be shown for the process in the Process app.
f. (optional) Enable the Burn Graphics/High Priority/Overwrite toggles as needed.
g. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Send to Playback Action" on page 408.
21. Define the parameters for an Update Metadata action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Update Metadata.
b. In the Details panel, select a Production Management or Asset Management system or Wolftech News from the System list.
c. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
d. (optional) Click the Add/Remove attributes button, select the properties to be added from the properties picker tool and provide the values to be updated.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Update Metadata Action" on page 410.
22. Enable the rule: In the Details panel, click the gray Enable toggle so it turns blue.
23. Click the Save button to save the rule.
When you select another rule in the Rules and Items panel or use the Add new rule button while you are editing a rule and have not saved your changes yet, you are prompted to save or discard the changes.
Do one of the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
- Click Discard all changes.
- Click Save to save all changes.
When you save the rule it is validated and parsed. If issues are detected, the results of the validation are summarized in a Validation dialog box. Validation issues are shown as warnings, parsing issues are shown as "incomplete".
Do one of the following:
- Click Cancel and resolve the listed issues before saving again.
- Click Save Anyway to save the rule with validation issues. Items shown as incomplete, are
completely removed from the rule.
24. (if paused) Resume event processing in the Rules Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Rules Engine
Event Processing" on page 345.
Creating an Asset Management Rule During the rule creation process, you will add multiple items to the rule and configure property values. You can choose to either add items one at a time, configuring the properties for each as you add them, or you can add all of your items first and then configure the properties as a second step.
To create a rule:
1. Click the Rules Editor button in the Administrator Fast Bar.
2. Select an Asset Management module from the app’s sidebar.
3. (optional) Temporarily stop event processing in the Rules Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Rules
Engine Event Processing" on page 345.
4. In the Rules and Items panel, click the Add new rule button.
The Rules and Items panel switches to the ITEMS tab. The Define Rule panel shows placeholders for the items you can add to the rule. The Details panel shows a few basic controls.
5. Add a name for the rule: In the Details panel, replace the placeholder "New Rule" in the Rule Name
field.
Rule names need to be unique. If you enter a name that is already used for another rule, the name will be appended with a counter in brackets when you save the rule.
6. (optional) Click the Expand toggle button in front of Additional information and enter a comment in
the Comment text box. The comment is only used within the Rules Editor app.
7. Add one trigger: In the Rules and Items panel's ITEMS tab, click the trigger you want to add.
8. Add one entity: In the Rules and Items panel's ITEMS tab, click the entity you want to add: Asset or
Sequence.
9. Add one or more Actions: In the Rules and Items panel's ITEMS tab, click the action you want to add.
You can add up to 10 actions.
10. Define the asset type for the entity.
15 Using the Rules Editor App a. In the Define Rule panel, select the entity.
In the Details panel, the Asset section is shown.
b. Click the Show all link.
c. In the Asset Type fly-out, select all desired asset types.
d. Click the Apply button (√).
For more information, see "(Asset Management) Defining the Asset Type" on page 380.
11. (optional) Define filter conditions for the entity.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the entity.
In the Details panel, the initial controls for the Condition section are shown.
b. In the Details panel, click the Add new condition button.
The Condition 1 section is shown. The top-most entry in the Attributes and Comparison Operator lists is preselected.
c. Select the attribute from the Attributes list.
d. Select the operator that should apply for the condition from the Comparison Operator list.
e. Depending on the selected operator, define additional values.
f. (optional) Define additional conditions. You can create a maximum of 10 conditions for a rule.
g. (optional) Define if the conditions are to be combined by AND or OR by clicking the corresponding button.
For more information, see "Defining Entities" on page 360.
12. Define the parameters for an Add to Folder action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add To Folder.
b. In the Details panel, select a Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
d. Select a folder from the Folder list.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Folder Action" on page 385.
13. Define the parameters for an Add to Publishing Output action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add to Publishing Output.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
c. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Publishing Output Action" on page 388.
14. Define the parameters for an Add to Story action.
15 Using the Rules Editor App a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add to Story.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
c. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Story Action" on page 389.
15. Define the parameters for an AM Orchestration action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action AM Orchestration.
b. In the Details panel, select the Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select an action specification from the Specification list.
d. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
e. Specify values for the selected specification.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an AM Orchestration Action" on page 390.
16. Define the parameters for a Create Entity action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Entity.
b. In the Details panel, select an Asset Management system or Wolftech News from the System list.
c. Select an entity type from the Entity Type list.
d. Type the name of the entity to be created in the Entity Name field.
e. (optional) Click the Add/Remove attributes button, select the properties to be added from the properties picker tool and provide values for the added properties.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Entity Action" on page 391.
17. Define the parameters for a Create Folder action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Folder.
b. In the Details panel, select a Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select the parent folder from the Parent Folder list.
d. Type the name of the folder to be created in the Folder Name field.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Folder Action" on page 393.
18. Define the parameters for a Notification action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Notification.
b. In the Details panel, type a name in the Title field.
c. (optional) Type a message text in the Message field.
d. Select one or more Platform User Management groups which should receive the notification from the Destinations list.
For more information, see "Defining a Notification Action" on page 404.
19. Define the parameters for a Publish action.
15 Using the Rules Editor App a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Publish.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
c. Provide the title of the clip to be created in Publisher.
d. Select the publish template to be used from the Publish Template list.
e. (optional) Change the status of the "Publish clip when done" toggle.
For more information, see "Defining a Publish Action" on page 406.
20. Define the parameters for an Update Metadata action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Update Metadata.
b. In the Details panel, select a Production Management or Asset Management system or Wolftech News from the System list.
c. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
d. (optional) Click the Add/Remove attributes button, select the properties to be added from the properties picker tool and provide the values to be updated.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Update Metadata Action" on page 410.
21. Enable the rule: In the Details panel, click the gray Enable toggle so it turns blue.
22. Click the Save button to save the rule.
When you select another rule in the Rules and Items panel or use the Add new rule button while you are editing a rule and have not saved your changes yet, you are prompted to save or discard the changes.
Do one of the following:
- Click Discard all changes.
- Click Save to save all changes.
When you save the rule it is validated and parsed. If issues are detected, the results of the validation are summarized in a Validation dialog box. Validation issues are shown as warnings, parsing issues are shown as "incomplete".
Do one of the following:
- Click Cancel and resolve the listed issues before saving again.
- Click Save Anyway to save the rule with validation issues. Items shown as incomplete, are
completely removed from the rule.
23. (if paused) Resume event processing in the Rules Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Rules Engine
Event Processing" on page 345.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Defining Entities For any entity selected in the Define Rule panel, you can define filters (conditions). Defining conditions conditions for an entity. When you define more than one condition you can decide how the individual conditions are to be combined; by default, conditions are combined by AND.
Some operators are very simple and require you to only select the operator. Others let you define more complex conditions. See the following topics:
- "Conditions: Attributes and Operators" below
For an Asset Management entity selected in the Define Rule panel, you can additionally define the asset type. See the following topic:
- Conditions: Attributes and Operators
When you define conditions for the entity, you select attributes and comparison operators. Depending on the attribute type different operators are supported. Since each module provides its own data model with different attribute types and attributes, the following sections list the attribute types and supported operators for each rule/system type:
- "Wolftech News" below
Wolftech News Defining conditions is supported for all entity types. When Wolftech News is selected, the Rules Editor retrieves the data model from the backend.
> **Note:** All attributes that are available for the selected entity type are shown in the Attributes list. Therefore,
you will see different attributes for the entities Story, Publishing Output and Pitch.
In the Attribute selection list, attributes are shown as follows:
First, static attributes and common attributes (Description and Name; for Publishing Output
- additionally Duration, Duration (Frames) and Sequence Requested) are alphabetically listed.
- Then, custom attributes – marked with the suffix "(Custom)" – are alphabetically listed.
Depending on the attribute type different operators are supported, as listed in the following table.

Asset Management Defining conditions is supported for all entity types. When an Asset Management system is selected, the Rules Editor retrieves the data model from the backend.
> **Note:** All attributes of all asset and sequence types that can be used for defining conditions are shown in the
Attributes list at the same time.
In the Attribute selection list, attributes are shown as follows:
First, static attributes (Ada Summary Status, Ada Transcript Status, Add to Publishing Output, Add to
- Story, Has Audio, Has Video, Incomplete) and common attributes are together alphabetically listed.
- Then, custom attributes – marked with the suffix "(Custom)" – are alphabetically listed.
The following attributes are not supported: Duration, Date, Time, Timecode, Float, Masterdata, Thesaurus, multi-value (except multi-value Legallist) and multi-value compound.
Depending on the attribute type different operators are supported, as listed in the following table.

1 The default Asset Management data model does not provide a multi-value legal list attribute that is used in an object or EDL class. Using multi-value legal list attributes requires customization.
Supported only for properties that are included in the "watched properties" list. See "Attributes That Support Update Events ("Watched Properties")" below.
Attributes That Support Update Events ("Watched Properties") To support Update events, the Change Event Agent keeps track of specific "watched properties". Update events include the old value for watched properties (if the value has changed), but are skipped if none of the watched properties have changed.
The following attributes are marked as "watched properties":
Ada Summary Status ("ada_summary_status")
- Ada Transcript Status ("ada_transcript_status")
- Add to Publishing Output ("add_to_wtn_pubpoint")
- Add to Story ("add_to_wtn_story")
- Description ("description")
- Has Audio ("has_audio")
- Has Video ("has_video")
- Incomplete ("incomplete")
- Name ("name")
- Phonetic ("phonetic)
- Using the Is Set and Is Not Set Operator
Operator Supported Attribute Types Is Set Wolftech News: All
- l
Is not Set Production Management: All
- l
Asset Management: All
- The Is Set/Is not Set operators are very simple and require you to only select the operator and the attribute
for which the operator should apply.
The Is Set/Is not Set operators check if the value for the attribute is set/not set.
15 Using the Rules Editor App To use the Is Set and Is not Set operators:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an entity.
2. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- Click the Add new condition button to use the operator for a new condition.
- Go to an existing condition to replace the used operator.
3. Select an attribute from the Attributes list.
4. Select the Is Set or the Is not Set operator from the Comparison Operator list.
Using the Is Updated and Is Not Updated Operator Operator Supported Attribute Types
- Is Updated l Wolftech News: All
All1
- Is not Updated l Production Management:
Asset Management: All1 Although the Is Updated/Is not Updated operators are shown for all attributes, usage of the Is Updated/Is not Updated operators is only supported for attributes that are included in the "watched properties" list. See "Attributes That Support Update Events ("Watched Properties")" on the previous page.
The Is Updated/Is not Updated operators are very simple and require you to only select the operator and the attribute for which the operator should apply.
The Is Updated/Is not Updated operators check if the value for the attribute is updated/not updated.
To use the Is Updated and Is not Updated operators:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an entity.
2. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- Click the Add new condition button to use the operator for a new condition.
- Go to an existing condition to replace the used operator.
3. Select an attribute from the Attributes list.
4. Select the Is Updated or the Is not Updated operator from the Comparison Operator list.
Using the True and False Operator Operator Supported Attribute Types
- True l Wolftech News: Boolean
- False l Production Management: Boolean, Phonetic
- Asset Management: Boolean, Phonetic
The True/False operators are very simple and require you to only select the operator and the attribute for which the operator should apply.
The True/False operators check if the value for the attribute is "True"/"False".
15 Using the Rules Editor App To use the True and False operators:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an entity.
2. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- Click the Add new condition button to use the operator for a new condition.
- Go to an existing condition to replace the used operator.
3. Select a Boolean attribute or Phonetic from the Attributes list.
4. Select the True or the False operator from the Comparison Operator list.
Using the Is Any of and Is None of Operator (Single Value Attribute) Operator Type Attribute Types Sample Attributes
- Is Any of Wolftech News l Text l Name, Description
- Is None l Taxonomy (single- l Publishing Platform (Custom)
of value thesaurus) Production l Text l Name, Description, Programme Management Title (Custom)
- Number l Duration
List –
- l
Asset l Text l Creator, Description, Main Title Management (Custom)
- Number (Integer) l Audio Track Count, (Custom),
Canvas Height (Custom)
- Legallist l Asset Type (Custom), Atmosphere
(Custom) The Is Any of/Is None of operators check if
- at least one of the provided values is found in the attribute value (as an exact match).
- none of the provided values is found in the attribute value.
Depending on the type of the selected attribute, different input controls are displayed: a Value text "pill", a Value list, or a Value taxonomy selection control.
For attributes of type Text and Number For these attribute types, you type values that should apply in text boxes (pills).
To use the Is Any of and Is None of operators:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an entity.
2. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- Click the Add new condition button to use the operator for a new condition.
- Go to an existing condition to replace the used operator.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
3. Select an attribute of type Paragraph, Text or Number from the Attributes list.
4. Select the Is Any of or the Is None of operator from the Comparison Operator list.
5. Click the + (Add value) button.
An input pill is shown.
6. In the Value pill, type the value that should apply and then click outside the pill.
Values are not case sensitive.
7. (optional) To add another value, click the + (Add value) button and type the value in the Value pill.
You can add a maximum of 5 values. When you try to add a sixth value a message will appear.
8. (optional) To remove a value, click the x (Remove value) button in the Value pill.
For attributes of type List (Production Management) and Legallist (Asset Management) For these attribute types, you do not type values but select them from the configured lists.
To use the Is Any of and Is None of operators:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an entity.
2. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- Click the Add new condition button to use the operator for a new condition.
- Go to an existing condition to replace the used operator.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
3. Select an attribute of type Priority or Status, List, or Legallist from the Attributes list.
4. Select the Is Any of or the Is None of operator from the Comparison Operator list.
5. Click the drop-down arrow at the right side of the Value list.
6. In the list that opens, select one or more values.
7. Click outside the list.
8. (optional) To remove a value, open the Value list, deactivate the check box of a selected value and
click outside the list.
For attributes of type single value thesaurus (Wolftech News) For these attribute type, you do not type values but select them from the taxonomy control.
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an entity.
2. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- Click the Add new condition button to use the operator for a new condition.
- Go to an existing condition to replace the used operator.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
3. Select a single-value thesaurus from the Attributes list.
4. Select one of the above mentioned operators from the Comparison Operator list.
5. Add one or multiple values:
a. Click the + (Add value) button.
A taxonomy selection control opens. Branches show a ">" to the right side; selecting a branch navigates to its values. Note that you cannot add a branch.
b. Do one of the following:
- Select a value.
- Select a branch, then select a value.
- Start typing in the filter box to filter the taxonomy to display only entries that match
your text, and then select the entry from the filtered list. If this is a branch, select one of its values.
The selected value is shown. When you over the cursor over the pill, a tool-tip shows the value and its parent branch.
c. (optional) To add additional values, repeat steps a and b.
6. (optional) To remove a value, click the x (Remove value) button in the Value pill.
Using Operators for Multi-Value Taxonomies Operator Type Attribute Types Sample Attributes
- Equals (in any Wolftech News l List (multi-value) l Genres (Custom),
order) Offices (Custom)
- Taxonomy (multi-
Does not Equal value thesaurus) Publishing
- l
15 Using the Rules Editor App Operator Type Attribute Types Sample Attributes (in any order) Destinations (Custom)
- Includes
Production l – l –
- Does not Include
Management Asset Management l Legallist (multi- l – value) The Equals (in any order)/Does not Equal (in any order) operators check if the list of specified values equals/does not equal the list of values in the attribute (ignoring the order).
The Includes/Does not Include operators check if at least a part of the specified value(s) is included/not included in the attribute value. Note the following:
If you add more than one value to an Equals/Does not Equal operator, ALL of the selected values
- must match for the condition evaluate to true.
If you add more than one value to a Includes/Does not Include operator, you can decide if ANY or ALL
- values must match for the condition evaluate to true.
For attributes of type multi-value list (Wolftech News, Asset Management) For this attribute types, you select values from the configured lists.
To use the Equals and Includes operators for a multi-value list attribute:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an entity.
2. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- Click the Add new condition button to use the operator for a new condition.
- Go to an existing condition to replace the used operator.
3. Select an attribute of type multi-value list from the Attributes list.
4. Select one of the above mentioned operators from the Comparison Operator list.
5. Click the drop-down arrow at the right side of the Value list.
6. In the list that opens, select one or more values.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
7. Click outside the list.
8. (For Includes/Does not Include) Define how many values must match for the condition evaluate to
true: Click the Any or All button.
9. (optional) To remove a value, open the Value list, deactivate the check box of a selected value and
click outside the list.
For attributes of type multi-value thesaurus (Wolftech News) For this attribute type, you select values from a thesaurus selection control.
To use the Equals and Includes operators for a multi-value thesaurus attribute:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an entity.
2. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- Click the Add new condition button to use the operator for a new condition.
- Go to an existing condition to replace the used operator.
3. Select a multi-value thesaurus from the Attributes list.
4. Select one of the above mentioned operators from the Comparison Operator list.
5. Add one or multiple values:
a. Click the + (Add value) button.
A taxonomy selection control opens. Branches show a ">" to the right side; selecting a branch navigates to its values. Note that you cannot add a branch.
15 Using the Rules Editor App b. Do one of the following:
- Select a value.
- Select a branch, then select a value.
- Start typing in the filter box to filter the taxonomy to display only entries that match
your text, and then select the entry from the filtered list. If this is a branch, select one of its values.
The selected value is shown. When you over the cursor over the pill, a tool-tip shows the value and its parent branch.
c. (optional) To add additional values, repeat steps a and b.
6. (For Includes/Does not Include) Define how many values must match for the condition evaluate to
true: Click the Any or All button.
7. (optional) To remove a value, click the x (Remove value) button in the Value pill.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Using the Contains and Does not Contain Operator (Single Value Attribute) Operator Type Attribute Types Sample Attributes Contains Wolftech News Text Name, Description
- l l
Does not
- Production l Text l Name, Description, Programme
Contain Management Title (Custom) Asset Management l Text l Creator, Description, Main Title (Custom) The Contains/Does not Contain operators check if at least a part of the specified value is contained/not contained in the attribute value.
If you add more than one value to a Contains or Does not Contain operator, you can select how many values must match for the condition evaluate to true: ANY or ALL.
To use the Contains and Does not Contain operators:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an entity.
2. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- Click the Add new condition button to use the operator for a new condition.
- Go to an existing condition to replace the used operator.
3. Select an attribute of type Text from the Attributes list.
4. Select the Contains or the Does not Contain operator from the Comparison Operator list.
5. Click the + (Add value) button.
An input pill is shown.
6. In the Value pill, type the value that should apply and then click outside the pill.
Values are not case sensitive.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
7. (optional) To add another value, click the + (Add value) button and type the value in the Value pill.
You can add a maximum of 5 values. When you try to add a sixth value a message will appear.
8. Define how many values must match for the condition evaluate to true: Click the Any or All button.
9. (optional) To remove a value, click the x (Remove value) button in the Value pill.
Using the Starts With and Ends With Operator Operator Type Attribute Types Sample Attributes
- Starts Wolftech News l Text l Name, Description
With Production l Text l Name, Description, Programme Title
- Ends With Management (Custom)
Asset Management Text Creator, Description, Main Title
- l
(Custom) The Starts With/Ends With operators check if the attribute value starts/ends with the specified value.
To use the Starts With and Ends With operators:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an entity.
2. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- Click the Add new condition button to use the operator for a new condition.
- Go to an existing condition to replace the used operator.
3. Select an attribute of type Text from the Attributes list.
4. Select the Starts With or the Ends With operator from the Comparison Operator list.
5. In the Value text box, type the value that should apply and then click outside the text box.
This value is not case sensitive.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Using Date-Related Operators Operator Type Attribute Types Sample Attributes Before Wolftech News Date Published At (Custom)
- l l
Before or
- Equal
- After Production l DateTime l Created, Modified, Creation
Management Date (Custom)
- After or Equal
- Between
- Today
Asset Management l DateTime l Created, Modified, Air Date
- Tomorrow
(Custom)
- Yesterday
- Relative
Date-related operators check if the attribute value matches the defined date.
To use the date-related operators:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an entity.
2. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- Click the Add new condition button to use the operator for a new condition.
- Go to an existing condition to replace the used operator.
3. Select an attribute of type Date Time from the Attributes list.
4. To define a date limit, do the following:
a. Select one of the following from the Comparison Operator list:
- Before
- Before or Equal
- After
- After or Equal
b. Click on the date in the Value field and select a date from the calendar picktool.
15 Using the Rules Editor App c. (optional) Type the hour and minute value.
For attributes of type Date, hour and minute values are not supported.
5. To define a date range, do the following:
a. Select Between from the Comparison Operator list:
b. Click on the left date in the Value field and select a starting date from the calendar picktool.
c. (optional) Type the hour and minute value.
For attributes of type Date, hour and minute values are not supported.
d. Click on the right date in the Value field and select an ending date from the calendar picktool.
e. (optional) Type the hour and minute value.
6. To use a predefined date value, select one of the following from the Comparison Operator list:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
- Today
- Tomorrow
- Yesterday
7. To define a custom relative date, do the following:
a. Select Relative from the Comparison Operator list.
b. Define the operator for the relative date: Click the Last or Next button.
c. Define the unit: Click the Minutes, Hours, or Days button.
For attributes of type Date, only the Days unit is supported.
d. Define the value: Click in the Value box and type a value or use the up/down arrows at the right side to increase/decrease the value.
Using Number-Related Operators Operator Type Attribute Types Sample Attributes Less than Wolftech News Number Duration (frames)
- l l
Less or
- Equal Production l Number l Duration
Management
- Greater
- Greater or
Asset Management l Number l Audio Track Count, (Custom), Equal (Integer) Canvas Height (Custom)
- Between
Number-related operators check if the attribute value matches the defined number.
To use the number-related operators:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an entity.
2. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- Click the Add new condition button to use the operator for a new condition.
- Go to an existing condition to replace the used operator.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
3. Select an attribute of type Number (Integer) from the Attributes list.
4. To define a number limit, do the following:
a. Select one of the following from the Comparison Operator list:
- Less than
- Less or Equal
- Greater
- Greater or Equal
b. Click in the Value field and do one of the following:
- Type a positive or negative number.
- Use the ArrowUp and ArrowDown controls to define a positive or negative number.
- To clear the input, highlight the value and press the Del or Backspace key.
The maximal value is 100000000000 (positive or negative).
5. To define a range, do the following:
a. Select Between from the Comparison Operator list.
b. Click in the left Value field and type a starting number.
c. Press the Tab key or click in the right Value field and type an ending number.
(Asset Management) Defining the Asset Type When you add the entity Asset or Sequence to the rule, the Details panel will show
- all asset types ("object classes") or
- all sequence types ("EDL classes")
defined in the data model of the selected Asset Management system.
You need to restrict the rule execution to selected asset or sequence types, as described in the following procedure. If you do not select at least one asset or sequence type, a "No asset types selected for entity" warning will be shown.
In a default data model, only one sequence type named "EDL" is available. As long as your data model does not contain customized EDL classes, you can directly enable the EDL sequence type. If you use customized sequence types, you might want to apply the below described process for the entity Sequence.
15 Using the Rules Editor App To define the asset type:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the entity Asset.
In the Details panel, the Asset section is shown.
2. Click the Show all link.
An Asset Type fly-out window opens. It shows all object classes retrieved from the data model of the selected Asset Management system.
3. In the Asset Type fly-out, select all desired asset types.
4. Click the Apply button (√).
In the Define Rule panel, the names of the selected asset types are shown on the entity card. In the 15 Using the Rules Editor App Details panel's Asset section, a blue counter shows the number of selected asset types.
Defining Actions Define the parameters of the action to be executed in the action-specific section of the Details panel.
Depending on the rule type different actions are supported, as listed in the following table.
Rule Type Supported Actions Wolftech News Add to Folder
- AM Orchestration
- Create Entity
- Create Folder
- Create Publishing Output
- Create News Sequence
- Create Sequence
- Notification
- Publish Publishing Output
- Send to Playback
- Update Metadata
- Production Management l Add to Folder
- Add to Publishing Output
- Add to Story
- AM Orchestration
- Create Entity
- Create Folder
- Media Analytics (master clips only)
- Notification
- Publish
- Send to Playback
- Update Metadata
Asset Management l Add to Folder 15 Using the Rules Editor App Rule Type Supported Actions
- Add to Publishing Output
- Add to Story
- AM Orchestration
- Create Entity
- Create Folder
- Notification
- Publish
- Update Metadata
See the following topics:
- Action Engine calls against the downstream system run under a dedicated identity (account). You can
modify the default impersonation setting if needed. See the following topic:
For the input fields of the action specifications, you have specific options. See the following topics:
If you use an expression, you might want to test the expression against a defined change event. See "Using the Rules Simulator for Consistency Checks" on page 419.
Actions: Configuration and Back-End Workflow The Rules Engine retrieves all available Action Engines. For each Action Engine, the Rules Editor retrieves the Action Types to populate the list of available actions in the Rules Editor UI. An Action Type provides basic information of an action along with a list of system types plus entity types the action can be applied to. If an action depends on systems, a system selection is displayed in the Rules Editor app’s Details panel for the Action.
15 Using the Rules Editor App When the user selects a system, the Rules Editor retrieves the Action Specification(s) for the selected system and action type. For some actions types, such as Send to Playback, always a single Action Specification is returned; for others, such as AM Orchestration, several Action Specifications can be returned. In this case, the user will select one of the provided Actions Specifications.
An Action Specification defines the visual elements by providing labels, value types, and potentially default values. With this information, the Rules Editor can display the layout of the Action’s section in the Details panel. Optionally, an Action Specification may also contain a list of supported Entity Types, which indicate the entities that can be combined in the same rule with the given Action. This helps the Rules Editor to validate compatibility between Actions and Entities.
When a user selects a control representing a dynamic property (such as Profile), the Rules Editor retrieves the related properties. In a case of STP profiles, not only the profile names but also their attributes (Device, Resolution, …) get retrieved.
After a user populated all mandatory fields, the Rules Editor is able to save the Action configuration in the rule. The Rules Editor only validates the mandatory flag of properties but does not validate dependent fields, for example, if some of Boolean values (check boxes) need to appear in certain combinations only.
Such invalid combinations can only be detected by the Action Engines, when the Action is executed.
When matching a rule the Rules Engine reads the related Action Configuration, resolves expressions and sends a corresponding Action Message to the responsible Action Engine. The Action executes the action, for example by creating an STP job or Asset Management process.
Action Specifications An Action Specification defines the visual elements by providing labels, value types, and potentially default values. With this information, the Rules Editor can display the layout of the Action’s section in the Details panel.
For all action types – except for AM Orchestration –, a predefined action specification is provided by Avid.
These action specifications cannot be altered. The Create Entity and Update Metadata action specifications additionally provide a properties picker tool that lets you dynamically add properties to the template.
For actions of type AM Orchestration, predefined action specifications are not provided by Avid. In case of AM Orchestration, the Action Engine generates dynamically action specifications for each applicable Asset Management process. The name of such an action specification corresponds to the Asset Management process label and gets displayed in the Specification drop-down list. AM Orchestration action specifications need to be configured. The following are required to create an action specification:
- A process model (created in Process Modeler, uploaded and activated in the Process Engine).
- All scripts that are required by the process model (uploaded into the script repository in
Orchestration Monitor).
The corresponding process class with attributes in Datamodel Administrator.
- A specific Action (process creation) template defined in Datamodel Administrator.
  - Template name: ACTION_<process class name>
  - Supported properties: boolean, duration, float, integer, text, timecode, legal list
Not supported: date, datetime, time; multi-value, multi-value compound; thesaurus, master data The corresponding process creation right (Processes/Create_<process class name>) assigned in Asset
- Management User Manager to the user groups that are to be allowed to create the process.
For more information, see "Creating Action Templates" in the Avid MediaCentral | Asset Management Process Reference.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Auto-Fixing Changed Action Specifications When you select a rule in the Rules and Items panel's RULES tab and changes in an action specification of the rule are detected (for example, a property has been added to or removed from the action specification) an “Action Specification(s) Changed” window will be displayed. The window lists all changes, as shown in the following example.
The rule will be automatically adapted and saved. After that, the action specification in the rule is aligned again. However, additional manual steps might be necessary to make the action valid, such as setting a new mandatory parameter.
Click OK to close the “Action Specification(s) Changed” window.
Defining an Add to Folder Action Action Supported Entities Supported Target Modules Add to Folder l all Wolftech News l local and remote Production Management modules
- all Production Management
local and remote Asset Management
- all Asset Management
modules Using the Add to Folder action you can define a rule that triggers adding the specified entity to the specified folder in the Production Management or Asset Management module.
> **Note:** However the entity is resolved (From Source Event, referenced property, literal value, expression), its
source system has to match the selected system in the action. For Add to Folder in a Wolftech News rule, this means that only an Asset Management or Production Management asset that is referenced in the Wolftech News entity can be added to the selected folder in Asset Management/Production Management.
Generic Configuration Workflow The following procedure describes a generic configuration workflow, which means that you provide input for fields of type "Literal" (default). See "Defining the Input Type: Literal, Property or Expression" on page 414.
15 Using the Rules Editor App To define an Add to Folder action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add to Folder.
In the Details panel, the Add to Folder section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, select the Production Management or Asset Management module from the
System list. The System list shows all connected local and remote Production Management and Asset Management modules.
The template defined by the action specification is loaded.
3. The Entity field defines how the entity (the asset to be added) is retrieved. The default setting From
Source Event secures that the entity Id of the asset specified by the rule is retrieved. You can change the Entity field value as follows, but consider leaving the default value.
(optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
4. To select a folder, do the following:
a. Click on the Folder list.
The list shows all top-level folders of the selected module as shown in the following illustration.
b. Select a parent folder from the Folder list.
The Folder control shows the folders of the selected top-level folder.
15 Using the Rules Editor App c. Select a subfolder from the control. You can use the Filter control to filter the list of folders.
The control shows one of the following:
  - The subfolders of the selected folder.
  - The No values found message if the folder does not contain subfolders.
d. To navigate back, do on of the following:
- To navigate back to the top-level folders, click the X button in the control.
- To navigate upwards in the folder hierarchy, click behind the last folder name and delete
the subfolder name(s) by pressing the backspace key.
e. To confirm your selection, click outside the Folder selection control.
The control is closed and the selected folder is set as Folder.
f. To replace a set folder, click on the Folder list, then click the X button in the control and select another folder.
5. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 414.
6. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Sample Configuration The following procedure describes a sample configuration for the following use case: When a new masterclip is created in a Production Management module, this masterclip is to be added to the folder Projects/Platform Automation/New on the same Production Management module.
15 Using the Rules Editor App (Sample workflow) To define an Add to Folder action:
1. Select a Production Management module in the sidebar.
2. Create or open a rule that includes the following:
  - Trigger: Created
  - Entity: Masterclip
  - Action: Add to Folder
3. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add to Folder.
In the Details panel, the Add to Folder section is shown.
4. In the Details panel, select the Production Management module from the System list.
5. Do not change the default value of the Entity field (From Source Event).
6. To define the Folder to which the masterclip is to be added, do the following:
a. Click on the Folder list.
b. Select Projects.
c. Select Projects/Platform Automation.
d. Select Projects/Platform Automation/New.
e. To confirm your selection, click outside the Folder selection control.
The control is closed and the selected folder is set as Folder.
7. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Defining an Add to Publishing Output Action Action Supported Entities Add to Publishing Output l all Production Management
- all Asset Management
Using the Add to Publishing Output action you can define a rule that triggers adding a Production Management or Asset Management asset, which references a Publishing Output in its Add to Publishing Output (addToWtnPubpoint) property, to the Publishing Output board. This action is usually used by an ingest operation.
To define an Add to Publishing Output action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add to Publishing Output.
In the Details panel, the Add to Publishing Output section is shown. The template defined by the action specification is directly loaded.
2. The Entity field defines how the entity (the asset to be added) is retrieved. The default setting From
Source Event secures that the entity Id of the asset specified by the rule is retrieved. You can change the Entity field value as follows, but consider leaving the default value.
15 Using the Rules Editor App (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
3. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify an advanced and the identity impersonation
setting if needed.
  - Publishing Output ID: The MediaCentral Entity ID of the publishing output to which the asset
(entity) is to be added. By default, the property Add to Publishing Output is selected. Do not change this property.
  - Impersonation setting: For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting
for Action Engine Calls" on page 414.
4. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Defining an Add to Story Action Action Supported Entities Add to Story l all Asset Management
- all Production Management
Using the Add to Story action you can define a rule that triggers adding an Asset Management or Production Management asset, which references a Wolftech News Story in its Add to Story (addToWtnStory) property, to the board of a Wolftech News Story. This action is usually used by an ingest operation.
To define an Add to Story action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add to Story.
In the Details panel, the Add to Story section is shown. The template defined by the action specification is directly loaded.
2. The Entity field defines how the entity (the asset to be added) is retrieved. The default setting From
Source Event secures that the entity Id of the asset specified by the rule is retrieved. You can change the Entity field value as follows, but consider leaving the default value.
(optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
3. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify an advanced and the identity impersonation
setting if needed.
  - Story ID: The MediaCentral Entity ID of the story to which the asset (entity) is to be added. By
default, the property Add to Story is selected. Do not change this property.
  - Impersonation setting: For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting
for Action Engine Calls" on page 414.
4. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Defining an AM Orchestration Action Action Supported Entities Supported Target Module AM Orchestration l all Wolftech News l local Asset Management module
- all Production Management
- all Asset Management
In case of AM Orchestration, the Action Engine dynamically generates action specifications for each applicable Asset Management process – each process for which a specific Action (process creation) template has been defined in Datamodel Administrator. The names of these action specifications correspond to the Asset Management process labels and get displayed in the Specification drop-down list.
Which fields are shown depends on the Action template configured for the specification. The following procedure therefore only details the general steps that are required for all AM Orchestration actions.
The attributes that are shown when clicking the Advanced toggle button, are not part of the Action template created in Datamodel Administrator but are added by the Action Engine to the action specification.
> **Note:** In this release, only actions for local modules are supported.
The following procedure describes how to provide input for fields of type "Literal" (default). See "Defining the Input Type: Literal, Property or Expression" on page 414.
To define an AM Orchestration action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action AM Orchestration.
In the Details panel, the AM Orchestration section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, select the Asset Management module from the System list.
The Specification list is shown.
