15 Using the Rules Editor App
3. Select an action specification from the Specification list.
4. Specify values for the selected specification.
Which fields are shown depends on the Action template configured for the selected specification.
5. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID
from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
- Select Not Set if no entity is to be attached to the action. If the Not Set option is shown is
determined by the min entities/max entities setting of the process that will be started.
6. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 414.
7. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Defining a Create Entity Action Action Supported Target Modules Supported Target Entities Create Entity Wolftech News Wolftech News Story, Pitch
- l
local and remote Asset Management all Asset Management
- l
modules Using the Create Entity action you can define a rule that triggers the creation of an entity (metadata object) in the database of the selected Asset Management module or in Wolftech News.
Although you can define the Create Entity action for any Production Management rule and entity, creating entities in any Production Management database is not supported.
This action lets you dynamically add properties once the template defined by the action specification is loaded. A properties picker tool provides the following:
All properties of all asset and sequence types (object and EDL classes) defined in the data model of
- the selected Asset Management module.
All properties of all asset types defined in the data model of Wolftech News.
- You can add the properties and provide their values as you want them to be populated on the new entity.
Note the following limitations:
15 Using the Rules Editor App The properties picker tool is not restricted to the properties of the selected asset type but shows all
- properties of all asset types defined in the data model of the respective system; misconfiguration is
not prevented.
- Independent of system type, read-only attributes will be filtered out and not provided in the
properties picker. This applies also to compound properties and multi-value properties (except list properties and thesaurus properties of system type Wolftech, which are supported).
The section only provides text fields or lists for providing input for added properties. You need to know
- which kind of value (for example, milliseconds, frames, SMPTE TC) the system expects for the used
property. Refer to the CTMS properties documentation at https://developer.avid.com/ctms/api/aa/resources/attributes.html.
Generic Configuration Workflow The following procedure describes a generic configuration workflow, which means that you provide input for fields of type "Literal" (default). See "Defining the Input Type: Literal, Property or Expression" on page 414.
To define a Create Entity action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Entity.
In the Details panel, the Create Entity section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, select a system from the System list.
The System list shows all connected local and remote Asset Management modules and Wolftech News.
The template defined by the action specification is loaded. Note the Add/Remove button that is shown at the bottom right corner of the section.
3. Select an entity type from the Entity Type list.
The list shows the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
  - For a selected Asset Management module: All asset and sequence types (object and EDL
classes) defined in the data model of the selected Asset Management module.
  - For Wolftech News: Story and Pitch.
4. Type the name of the entity to be created in the Entity Name field.
The name you provide will populate the common Name attribute of the CTMS interface, which will be mapped to the Maintitle property of the created asset or sequence or Name property of the created Wolftech News entity.
5. Add properties:
a. Click the Add/Remove attributes button shown at the bottom right side of the Details panel.
b. In the property picker tool that opens, check the check boxes of the properties you want to add.
You can use the Filter control at the top of the property picker to filter the list of displayed properties.
c. Click outside the property picker tool.
d. Provide values for the added properties.
6. (optional) Remove added properties:
a. Click the Add/Remove attributes button shown at the bottom right side of the Details panel.
b. In the property picker tool that opens, deselect the check boxes of the added properties.
c. Click outside the property picker tool.
Alternatively, right-click an added property and select Remove from the context-menu that opens.
7. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 414.
8. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Sample Configuration A sample configuration will be provided in context of future enhancements.
Defining a Create Folder Action Action Supported Entities Supported Target Modules Create Folder l all Wolftech News l local and remote Production Management modules
- all Production Management
- local and remote Asset Management
- all Asset Management
modules Using the Create Folder action you can define a rule that triggers the creation of a folder in the database of the selected Production Management or Asset Management module. In case the folder with the name specified in the rule already exists, no action is performed.
Note the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App If you have selected a Parent Folder and switch to another System, the parent folder will be cleared
- and reset to initial state.
- If a folder saved in the Action has become invalid, the Parent Folder control shows the corresponding
message; the tool-tip of the warning icon in the Action header shows the parentFolderId of the invalid folder.
Generic Configuration Workflow The following procedure describes a generic configuration workflow, which means that you provide input for fields of type "Literal" (default). See "Defining the Input Type: Literal, Property or Expression" on page 414.
To define a Create Folder action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Folder.
In the Details panel, the Create Folder section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, select the Production Management or Asset Management module from the
System list. The System list shows all connected local and remote Production Management and Asset Management modules.
The template defined by the action specification is loaded.
3. To select a folder, do the following:
a. Select the parent folder from the Parent Folder list. The list shows all top-level folders of the selected module. The following illustration shows the top-level folders of a selected Production Management module.
15 Using the Rules Editor App The Parent Folder control shows the folders of the selected top-level folder.
b. Select a subfolder from the control. You can use the Filter control to filter the list of folders.
The control shows one of the following:
  - The subfolders of the selected folder.
  - The No values found message if the folder does not contain subfolders.
c. To navigate back, do on of the following:
- To navigate back to the top-level folders, click the X button in the control.
- To navigate upwards in the folder hierarchy, click behind the last folder name and delete
the subfolder name(s) by pressing the backspace key.
d. To confirm your selection, click outside the Parent Folder control.
The control is closed and the selected folder is set as Parent Folder.
e. To replace a set parent folder, click on the Parent Folder list, then click the X button in the control and select another folder.
4. Type the name of the folder to be created in the Folder Name field.
5. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 414.
6. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Sample Configuration A sample configuration will provided in context of future enhancements.
15 Using the Rules Editor App (Preview) Defining a Create News Sequence Action Action Supported Entities Supported Targets Create News l Wolftech Publishing Output l local Production Management Sequence module Using the Create News Sequence action you can define a rule that triggers the creation of a Production Management Sequence based on a Wolftech News Publishing Output, which references a Newsroom Management story.
Precondition:
The Publishing Output contains a link to a Newsroom Management story.
- The Newsroom Management story does not contain a link to a Production Management sequence.
- If the Newsroom Management story does not contain a link to a Production Management sequence, the
following is done:
- A sequence is created in the specified or in the global folder of the selected Production Management
system.
The Production Management sequence is linked to the Newsroom Management story.
- The Production Management sequence is added to the board of the Publishing Output.
- If the Newsroom Management story already contains a link to a Production Management sequence or the
Publishing Output does not reference a Newsroom Management story, the Create News Sequence action will terminate at run-time with a corresponding error message.
To define a Create News Sequence action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Sequence.
In the Details panel, the Create Sequence section is shown.
2. Select the Production Management module from the System list.
The template defined by the action specification is loaded.
3. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID
from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
4. Provide the name of the sequence to be created: Select a property from the Name list.
By default, the field is of type Property and the "Name" property is preselected; thus, the value of the Name property will be used as sequence name.
5. (optional) Select the folder in which the sequence is to be created. If you do not select a folder, the
sequence will be created in the default save location for script sequences ("Location Script Sequence"). See "Configuring the Production Management Module" on page 236.
a. Click on the Folder list.
The list shows all top-level folders of the selected module as shown in the following illustration.
b. Select a parent folder from the Folder list.
The Folder control shows the folders of the selected top-level folder.
c. Select a subfolder from the control. You can use the Filter control to filter the list of folders.
The control shows one of the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
  - The subfolders of the selected folder.
  - The No values found message if the folder does not contain subfolders.
d. To navigate back, do on of the following:
- To navigate back to the top-level folders, click the X button in the control.
- To navigate upwards in the folder hierarchy, click behind the last folder name and delete
the subfolder name(s) by pressing the backspace key.
e. To confirm your selection, click outside the Parent Folder control.
The control is closed and the selected folder is set as Folder.
f. To replace a set parent folder, click on the Folder list, then click the X button in the control and select another folder.
6. (optional) Select the type of the sequence to be created from the Type list. By default, News
Sequence is set.
- Sequence
- News Sequence
7. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 414.
8. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Defining a Create Sequence Action Action Supported Entities Supported Targets Create Sequence l Wolftech Publishing Output l local Production Management module Using the Create Sequence action you can define a rule that triggers the creation of a Production Management Sequence in a specified folder of the selected Production Management system, based on a Wolftech News Publishing Output. The sequence will be added to the board of the Publishing Output.
Only if the rule should react on the "create sequence" button in the Wolftech app, make sure that the configuration of the Publishing Output entity has the following Conditions in the rule:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
  - Attribute: Sequence Requested
  - Comparison Operator: Is Updated
  - Attribute: Sequence Requested
  - Comparison Operator: True
To define a Create Sequence action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Sequence.
In the Details panel, the Create Sequence section is shown.
2. Select the Production Management module from the System list.
The template defined by the action specification is loaded.
3. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID
from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
4. Provide the name of the sequence to be created: Select a property from the Name list.
By default, the field is of type Property and the "Name" property is preselected; thus, the value of the Name property will be used as sequence name.
5. To select a folder, do the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App a. Click on the Folder list.
The list shows all top-level folders of the selected module as shown in the following illustration.
b. Select a parent folder from the Folder list.
The Folder control shows the folders of the selected top-level folder.
c. Select a subfolder from the control. You can use the Filter control to filter the list of folders.
The control shows one of the following:
  - The subfolders of the selected folder.
  - The No values found message if the folder does not contain subfolders.
d. To navigate back, do on of the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
- To navigate back to the top-level folders, click the X button in the control.
- To navigate upwards in the folder hierarchy, click behind the last folder name and delete
the subfolder name(s) by pressing the backspace key.
e. To confirm your selection, click outside the Folder selection control.
The control is closed and the selected folder is set as Folder.
f. To replace a set folder, click on the Folder list, then click the X button in the control and select another folder.
6. Select the type of the sequence to be created from the Type list. By default, Sequence is set.
- Sequence
- News Sequence
7. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 414.
8. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Defining a Create Publishing Output Action Action Supported Entities Create Publishing Output Wolftech Story
- Using the Create Publishing Output action you can define a rule that triggers the creation of a publishing
output for a story in Wolftech News.
To define a Create Publishing Output action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Publishing Output.
In the Details panel, the Create Publishing Output section is shown.
2. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID
from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
3. Provide the name of the Publishing Output to be created.
15 Using the Rules Editor App By default, the Publishing Output field is of type Property and the "Name" property is preselected; thus, the value of the Name property will be used as publishing output name.
4. Add one or multiple publish destinations to be used for the publishing output creation:
a. Click the + (Add value) button.
A taxonomy selection control opens. Publishing folders show a ">" to the right side; selecting a publishing folder navigates to its publishing destinations. Note that you cannot add a publishing folder.
b. Do one of the following:
- Select a publishing destination.
- Select a publishing folder, then select a publishing destination.
- Start typing in the filter box to filter the taxonomy to display only entries that match
your text, and then select the entry from the filtered list. If this is a publishing folder, select one of its publishing destinations.
The selected publishing destination is shown as value of Publishing destinations.
c. (optional) To add additional publishing destinations, repeat steps a and b.
In case of multiple publishing destinations, the destinations must be assigned to the same subplatform (Wolftech News policy).
5. (optional) Change the Timeless toggle as needed.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
- Enabled: The publishing output is created without a publishing date.
- Disabled (default): The next available date is queried and set during publishing output
creation.
6. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 414.
7. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Defining a Media Analytics Action Action Supported Entities Media Analytics l Production Management master clips Using the Media Analytics action you can define a rule that triggers a Media Analytics job, concrete: a Transcribe operation.
Note the following:
- Only Production Management master clips are supported.
- Only the provider Avid Ada and the feature Transcribe are supported.
Triggering Transcribe jobs with Avid Ada requires an Avid Ada Transcribe (formally named MediaCentral | STT Base Package) license.
Since the Transcribe license is a quota license that tracks the number of hours of transcribed audio, formulate the rule carefully. Consider defining Conditions for the entity in the rule. For an example, see the Conditions defined in the default Transcribe rule, as described in "Importing Default Rules" on page 343.
To define a Media Analytics action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Media Analytics.
In the Details panel, the Media Analytics section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, select an analytics provider from the Specification list: In this release, only Avid
Ada is supported.
3. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID
from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
4. Select the features that are to be processed from the Features list. In this release, only Transcribe is
supported.
5. Define the title of the Media Analytics job to be shown in the MediaCentral Cloud UX Process app by
using the Title field (type Expression). If you do not change the default, the Media Analytics job title will be Analytics: <name of analyzed asset> To change the default expression, do the following:
a. Hover the cursor over the Title field and click the Edit expression button that is shown.
b. In the Edit Expression - Title dialog box edit the expression. For example, change Analytics to Transcribe.
c. Click OK to close the dialog box.
The changed expression is shown as content of the Title field.
6. Select the audio tracks that are to be processed from the Tracks list. The list shows the maximum
number of 16 audio tracks (A1-A16). By default, A1 and A2 are preselected.
> **Note:** If you select more than one audio track, the tracks will be mixed down into one track for
transcription and a transcript for the mixed down audio track is created.
7. Do not use the Language list, which is reserved for future use.
In this release, the media analytics jobs run with language auto detection.
8. Enter a name for the transcript in the Result Name field. This name will be shown as transcript name
in the Transcript tab. If you do not provide a result name, the transcript will be named according to the audio track(s) for which it is created.
9. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Defining a Notification Action Action Supported Entities Notification l all Wolftech News
- all Production Management
- all Asset Management
15 Using the Rules Editor App Using the (platform) Notification action you can configure notifications and define which user groups of the MediaCentral platform User Management (IAM) should receive the notifications.
> **Note:** By default, up to 10 groups can be added to an individual notification action. This number might be
altered by the setting GROUP_MAX_SIZE of the configMap "avid-push-notifications-notification- service-core". See "Configuring the Maximum of Groups for Notification Actions" on page 602.
When the rule applies and a platform Notification is sent, the configured users will receive a Platform notification, as shown in the following example. By default, this notification includes a deep link related to the triggering asset of the rule: When a user clicks the notification, the default app of the asset opens in MediaCentral Cloud UX. For more information, see "The Notifications App" in the MediaCentral Cloud UX User's Guide. Alternatively, an activity notification email can be send to the users.
Sending platform notifications and emails can be enabled and disabled for each rule type in MediaCentral Cloud UX > User Settings > Notifications by using the "Production Management rule applies", "Asset Management rule applies" and "Wolftech News rule applies" toggle buttons in the "Platform Automation" section. Note that you cannot enable/disable rules for individual Production Management or Asset Management systems. You can also enable/disable sending notifications that do not include a deep link to an asset by using the "System independent job applies" toggle button. For more information, see "Configuring Notifications" in the MediaCentral Cloud UX User's Guide.
The following procedure describes how to provide input for fields of type "Literal" (default). See "Defining the Input Type: Literal, Property or Expression" on page 414.
To define a Notification action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Notification.
In the Details panel, the Notification section is shown.
2. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID
from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
- Select Not Set if no entity is to be attached to the action. In this case, the notification will not
include a deep link related to the triggering asset.
3. In the Details panel, type a name in the Title field.
4. (optional) Type a message text in the Message field.
5. Select one or more groups which should receive notifications from the Destinations list. By default,
you can select up to 10 groups.
The Destinations list shows all user groups that are retrieved from the MediaCentral IAM.
6. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Defining a Publish Action Action Supported Entities Publish l Production Management: Masterclips, Subclips, Sequences
- Asset Management: Video-type assets, EDLs, Images
Using the Publish action you can define a rule that triggers publishing with Publisher without using the MediaCentral Publisher app. The following can be published:
Production Management: master clips, subclips, sequences
- Asset Management: video-type assets, EDLs, images
- To define a Publish action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Publish.
In the Details panel, the Publish section is shown.
2. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID
from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
3. Provide the title of the clip to be created in Publisher.
By default, the field is of type Property and the "Name" property is preselected; thus, the value of the Name property will be used as clip title in Publisher.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Note that the Name property can be overwritten by a value set for the title on the used publish template in Publisher (field TITLE: DEFAULT VALUE in the Publisher app).
4. Select the publish template to be used from the Publish Template list.
The list shows all templates of type Video and Image.
5. (optional) Change the status of the "Publish clip when done" toggle.
- If toggled on, the asset is published to the defined targets of the selected template after it is
imported to the Publisher backend.
- If toggled off (default), the asset is not published after it is imported to the Publisher backend.
6. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
(Preview) Defining a Publish Publishing Output Action Action Supported Entities Publish Publishing Output l Wolftech Publishing Output Using the Publish Publishing Output action you can define a rule that triggers publishing of defined media attached to the Wolftech News publishing output with Publisher - without using the MediaCentral Publisher app.
Note the following regarding defined media:
- If the Publishing Output is linked to a Newsroom Management story, the action will query the
Production Management sequence ID from the Newsroom Management story, and the Production Management sequence will be published to Publisher. You do not need to configure the "Publishing destination" in the action.
- The publishing Editor is using a metadata template, which contains a dedicated property with a
reference to the media. The action will query this property and will publish the related media to Publisher. You need to configure the "Publishing destination" in the action (see step 7 in the following procedure).
To define a Publish Publishing Output action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Publish Publishing Output.
In the Details panel, the Publish Publishing Output section is shown.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
2. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID
from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
3. Provide the title of the clip to be created in Publisher.
By default, the field is of type Property and the "Name" property is preselected; thus, the value of the Name property will be used as clip title in Publisher.
Note that the Name can be overwritten by a value set for the title on the used publish template in Publisher (field TITLE: DEFAULT VALUE in the Publisher app).
4. Select the publish template to be used from the Publish Template list.
The list shows all templates of type Video and Image.
5. (optional) Change the status of the "Publish clip when done" toggle.
- If toggled on, the asset is published to the defined targets of the selected template after it is
imported to the Publisher backend.
- If toggled off (default), the asset is not published after it is imported to the Publisher backend.
6. Select the publishing output properties that are to be added to the publishing metadata from the
Properties list.
7. (optional, only required if no Newsroom Management story is linked on the Publishing Output, or
more technical: the ExternalStoryId property does not reference a Newsroom Management Story) Define the name of the publishing template property holding the entity global ID:
a. Select the publishing destination that is relevant for the media to be published from the Publishing destination list.
b. Select the template that is relevant for the media to be published from the Template list. Which templates are shown depends on the selected Publishing destination.
c. Select the name of the property that holds the link to the media to be published from the Asset list. Which properties are shown depends on the selected template.
8. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 414.
9. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Defining a Send to Playback Action Action Supported Entities Supported Target System Send to Playback (Preview) Wolftech News Publishing local Production Management
- l
Output module
- all Production Management
Using the Send to Playback action you can define a rule that triggers sending the asset to a playback destination. The action Send to Playback can be used for Production Management and Wolftech News rules.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Production Management: The Action pm-stp of the Action Engine STP triggers the Send to Playback
- activity for the asset specified by the Entity property (by default, the asset of the source event).
- (Preview) Wolftech News: The action performs a Send to Playback of a defined media attached to the
Publishing Output. Its purpose is to retrieve the asset entity ID used for the STP activity by evaluating the linked entities of the incoming publishing output asset (either a link to a sequence/clip or a Newsroom Management Story, which itself can contain a link to a sequence). The action then uses the Action pm-stp of the Action Engine STP to finally trigger the Send to Playback activity.
> **Note:** In this release, only actions for one local system are supported.
To define a Send to Playback action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Send to Playback.
In the Details panel, the Send to Playback section is shown.
For the Send to Playback action added to a Wolftech News rule you will see a preview flag.
2. Select the Production Management module from the System list.
3. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID
from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
4. Select an STP profile from the Profile list.
The list shows all profiles that are created for the system in the Workflow Settings app, Send to Playback section. For more information, see "Working with the Send to Playback Settings" on page 242.
Data in the read-only fields Device, Resolution, Video, Audio, and Workspace is read from the selected profile.
5. (optional) Enable the following toggles if needed:
  - Burn Graphics: Enable the toggle if your MediaCentral Cloud UX system is integrated with a
graphics management system, and you are sending an asset to playback that includes timed graphic elements that are to be embedded (“burn-in”) into the video stream.
  - High Priority: Enable the toggle if you need the asset to be transferred as quickly as possible to
15 Using the Rules Editor App the playback device.
  - Overwrite: Enable the toggle if you want to overwrite any asset with the same VideoID already
sent to the playback device.
6. (optional) If you do not want to keep the default Process Name, replace the default entry Action-
PM.STP (Production Management) or Action-WTNews.STP (Wolftech News). This is the name that will be shown for the process in the Process app.
7. (optional, Wolftech News only) Define the template property that references the media to be send to
playback:
a. Select the publishing destination that is relevant for the media to be send to playback from the Publishing destination list.
b. Select the template that is relevant for the media to be send to playback from the Template list. Which templates are shown depends on the selected Publishing destination.
c. Select the name of the property that holds the link to the media to be sent to playback from the Asset list. The list shows the names of the publishing template properties that hold a global ID related to a Production Management asset. Which assets are shown depends on the selected template.
8. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 414.
9. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Defining an Update Metadata Action Action Supported Entities Supported Target Systems Update Metadata l all Wolftech News l Wolftech News
- all Production Management l local and remote Production Management
modules
- all Asset Management
local and remote Asset Management
- modules
Using the Update Metadata action you can define a rule that triggers the update of selected properties with specified values in the database of a local or remote Production Management or Asset Management module or in Wolftech News.
This action lets you dynamically add properties once the template defined by the action specification is loaded. A properties picker tool provides all properties of all asset types (object classes) defined in the data model of the selected Asset Management module resp. all properties of the selected Production Management module or Wolftech News. You can add the properties and provide their values as you want them to be updated on the entity.
Note the following limitations:
- The properties picker tool is not restricted to the properties of the selected asset type but shows all
properties of all asset types defined in the data model of the respective system; misconfiguration is not prevented.
Independent of system type, read-only attributes will be filtered out and not provided in the
- properties picker. This applies also to compound properties and multi-value properties (except list
properties and thesaurus properties of system type Wolftech, which are supported).
- Description property in Wolftech News entities: a description can be set/updated for Story and Pitch
entities but not for a Publishing Output entity.
15 Using the Rules Editor App The section only provides text fields or lists for providing input for added properties. You need to know
- which kind of value (for example, milliseconds, frames, SMPTE TC) the system expects for the used
property. Refer to the CTMS properties documentation at https://developer.avid.com/ctms/api/aa/resources/attributes.html.
> **Note:** The Asset Management or Production Management system you select is just to provide you a list of
applicable metadata properties for better guidance at configuration time. But the MediaCentral entity ID referenced or provided in the rule might refer to a different system where these properties are not available. Design the action in a way that ensures that the desired metadata of the entity gets updated in the correct system.
Generic Configuration Workflow The following procedure describes a generic configuration workflow, which means that you provide input for fields of type "Literal" (default). See "Defining the Input Type: Literal, Property or Expression" on page 414.
To define an Update Metadata action:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Update Metadata.
In the Details panel, the Update Metadata section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, select a system from the System list.
The System list shows all connected local and remote Production Management and Asset Management modules and Wolftech News.
The template defined by the action specification is loaded. Note the Add/Remove button that is shown at the bottom right corner of the section.
3. The Entity field defines how the entity (the asset for which metadata is to be updated) is retrieved.
The default setting From Source Event secures that the entity Id of the asset specified by the rule is retrieved. You can change the Entity field value as follows, but consider leaving the default value.
15 Using the Rules Editor App (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
Right-click the Entity label and do one of the following:
- Select Literal and enter an entity ID.
- Select Property and then select a property to resolve the entity ID(s) from.
- Select Expression, then provide an expression to resolve the entity ID(s) on run-time.
- Select From Source Event. The entity ID from the Source Event is used. This is the default value.
4. Add properties:
a. Click the Add/Remove attributes button shown at the bottom right side of the Details panel.
b. In the property picker tool that opens, check the check boxes of the properties you want to add.
You can use the Filter control at the top of the property picker to filter the list of displayed properties.
c. Click outside the property picker tool.
d. Provide values for the added properties.
5. (optional) Remove added properties:
a. Click the Add/Remove attributes button shown at the bottom right side of the Details panel.
b. In the property picker tool that opens, deselect the check boxes of the added properties.
c. Click outside the property picker tool.
Alternatively, right-click an added property and select Remove from the context-menu that opens.
6. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 414.
7. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Sample Configuration The following procedure describes a sample configuration for the following use case: When a masterclip is created on the Production Management system MUN-IE, its Comments property should be filled with information on the name of the master clip and when it was created and by whom.
Adding the property Comments and reconfiguring it to input type Expression are required.
(Sample workflow) To define an Update Metadata action:
1. Select a Production Management system in the sidebar. In this sample workflow, the system is named
MUN-IE.
2. Create or open a rule that includes the following:
  - Trigger: Created
  - Entity: Masterclip
  - Action: Update Metadata
3. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Update Metadata.
In the Details panel, the Update Metadata section is shown.
4. In the Details panel, select a Production Management system from the System list. Make sure that the
source and target system are identical (MUN-IE in this sample workflow).
15 Using the Rules Editor App
5. Do not change the default value of the Entity field (From Source Event).
6. To add and define the Comments property:
a. Click the Add/Remove attributes button shown at the bottom right side of the Details panel.
b. In the property picker tool that opens, select the check box of the Comments property.
You can use the Filter control at the top of the property picker to filter the list of displayed properties.
c. Click outside the property picker tool.
d. Right-click the Comments field and select Expression from the context menu.
e. Hover the cursor over the Comments field and click the Edit expression button that is shown.
f. In the Edit Expression - Comments dialog box enter or paste the following expression:
"Entity '" + ${common|name} + "' created by '" + ${common|creator} + "' at '" + ${common|created} + "'" g. Click OK to close the dialog box.
The Update Metadata section will look like the following:
7. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls Action Engine calls against the system defined by the Entity ID run under a dedicated identity (account). By default, the user identity of the trigger (change event) is used. You can change this default behavior, as follows. The impersonation setting is provided for most actions, except for Media Analytics, Notifications and Publish.
To change the default impersonation setting:
1. In the Define Rule panel, select an action.
2. In the Details panel, click the Advanced toggle button shown in the action header.
3. Modify the identity impersonation setting by selecting one of the following:
- System
The Action Engine uses the System Identity (service account) to trigger actions on the selected system.
- Trigger (default)
The Action Engine uses the user identity stored in the change event to trigger actions on the selected system.
Enable the Fallback to System Identity toggle for cases where the change event does not provide a user identity. Then, the system identity is used as a fallback. If the Fallback to System Identity toggle is disabled, the Action will fail if the change event does not provide a user identity.
- User
Select a MediaCentral User Management (IAM) user from the User list.
The Action Engine uses the specified user account to trigger actions on the selected system.
4. Save the rule.
Defining the Input Type: Literal, Property or Expression In contrast to the input fields in other sections of the Details panel, the actions section allows you to switch the input type of the displayed fields.
Sample Use Case for Different Input Types Example: For a notification, the Message field by default is a text field; here you can type a message that is to be shown as notification text. If you do not want to provide a "hard-coded" message, you can change the Message field to the following types:
15 Using the Rules Editor App "Property" and assign a property whose value will be shown as notification message text.
- "Expression" and formulate an expression that injects the value of a specified attribute as contents of
the notification message. An expression allows also to combine hard-coded text with values from attributes.
In the following sample Notification action, all input types are used:
- "Title" is of type "Expression" and combines an expression that injects the Priority value with a text
message "Please Review" as the title of the notification.
- "Messages" is of type "Property" and a property attribute "Comments (Custom)" is selected. The
value of the Comments (Custom) attribute will be shown as message text.
"Destinations" is of type Literal and - as defined by the action specification - one or more groups
- which should receive notifications can be selected from the list.
Switching the Input Type When you hover the cursor over an input field or its label, to the left side of the label an icon indicates the input type:
Literal
- Property
- Expression
- By default, input fields are shown as type "Literal." This means that the input control defined in the action
specification is shown for each field. You can change the input type for a field by using the field's context menu.
In some cases, the context menu shows an additional entry:
If a default value is available for a field, the field label is displayed in italic font. In this case, the
- context menu additionally shows the option "Reset to Default" (for example, for the toggles and
Process Name field in a Send to Playback action).
15 Using the Rules Editor App Double-clicking an italic label resets the field to its default value and type.
- In actions that are applied to an entity (such as Add to Folder, Update Metadata or Send to
Playback), the context menu of the "Entity" field shows the additional option "From Source Event" as default value.
- In actions where you can dynamically add properties (Create Entity and Update Metadata), the
context menu of any added property shows the "Remove" option.
> **Note:** The option to switch the input type is only available if the action specification supports this feature.
When you change the input type of a field, you are not prevented from creating an invalid input.
To change the input type:
1. Right-click on a label or input field.
2. Select the input type from the context menu.
The currently applied input type is dimmed in the context menu.
  - Literal: The input control defined in the action specification for the field is used.
  - Property: The input field switches to a drop-down list. You can select a data model attribute
from the list.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
  - Expression: The input field switches to a read-only box. To enter an expression, click the Edit
expression button to the right. See "Entering an Expression" below.
To restore the original input type and default value:
- Right-click on an italic label and select Reset to Default from the context menu.
- Double-click on an italic label.
Using Expressions Expressions let you inject the value of a specified attribute as contents of another attribute.
Entering an Expression Before you can enter an expression, you must switch the input type of the desired field to type "Expression." To enter an expression:
1. Right-click on a label or input field.
2. Select Expression from the context menu.
The input type for the field is set to Expression. An "Open dialog to enter an expression" placeholder is shown in the field.
3. Hover the cursor over the field.
To the right side, an "Edit expression" button is shown.
4. Click the Edit expression button.
The Edit Expression - <field name> dialog box opens.
5. Enter or paste an expression in the field, as shown in the following example.
You can test the entered expression in the Rules Simulator, or formulate and test an expression in the Rules Simulator and then paste it to the Edit Expression dialog box.
a. Click the Open Simulator button.
The Rules Simulator opens. If an expression was entered in the Edit Expression dialog box, it is inserted in the Rules Simulator's Expression Simulator pane.
b. Enter and evaluate, or directly evaluate the expression in the Rules Simulator.
15 Using the Rules Editor App c. Minimize the Simulator or resize the Simulator window so that the Edit Expression dialog box is not hidden.
d. In the Edit Expression dialog box, click the Paste from Simulator button.
The evaluated expression is inserted into the Edit Expression dialog box.
For more information, see "Evaluating an Expression" on page 423.
6. Click OK.
The Edit Expression dialog box is closed. The expression is shown in the field. If the expression text is too long, its display is cut and ends in an ellipsis. Hover the cursor over the field to show the entire expression in a tool-tip.
7. To remove an expression:
a. Hover the cursor over the field and click the Edit expression button.
b. In the Edit Expression dialog box, delete the expression text and click OK.
Expressions: General Examples The following table lists some general expression examples that can be used in Action fields.
Sample Expressions Meaning ${common|name} Fetch the value of the common property Name from the change event ${custom|myprop} Fetch the value of the custom property "myprop" from the change event "job for [" + ${common|title} + "]" Combined string expression ${custom|myprop} == "abc" ? "ABC" : "something else" Expression with preprocessing token ${custom|myprop.curr} + " - " + ${custom|otherprop} "Changed from " + ${custom|myprop.curr} + " -> " + ${custom|myprop.prev} ((string)${status}) == "Ready for Review" && If the status is Ready For Review and the due ((DateTime?)${custom|dueDateTime})<= date and time is 15 minutes or less, set the DateTime.Now.AddMinutes(15)?"Urgent":"Normal" urgency to normal Expression Context Change event access The change event is available as variable "this". The properties of the change event can also be accessed implicitly without the "this." prefix.
Execution context The internal execution context (ExpressionContext) is available as variable _context.
Custom Expression Functions In the Rules Engine context there are additional functions available for expressions.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Functions Meaning array(<item1>, <item2> ...) Convert a list of values to an flattened array. Examples:
- array(${custom|mylist})?[0]}
- array(${custom|mylist})?.FirstOrDefault()}
createArray(<item1>, <item2> ...) Create an array from items in the expression (if an item is an array itself it will not be flattened).
entityId(object obj) Convert value to an EntityId. If the value is a string, it is parsed as EntityId.
If value is a structure, it should contain the properties entityType, systemType, systemId, type, id. Example:
- entityId("<some.entity.id>")
coid(object obj) Convert value to an CommonObjectId. If the value is a string, it is parsed as CommonObjectId. If value is a structure, it should contain the properties systemType, systemId, type, id. Example:
- coid("<some.coid>")
json(object obj) Convert object to json string. Example:
- json(${custom|myprop})
String Extension Methods Methods Meaning <input>.Matches(<pattern>) Regular expression match <input>.Like(<pattern>) Wildcard match <input>.StringContains(<substr1>) String match <input>.StringContains([<substr1>, <substr2> ...]) String match Using the Rules Simulator for Consistency Checks The Rules Editor provides a "Rules Simulator" utility that lets you to test or debug rule initiation and expressions. Using the Rules Simulator, you can do the following:
- Define the change event against which expressions and rules are to be evaluated.
- Test expressions used in the rule against the defined change event.
- Test if a certain rule or any rule in the Rule DB trigger on the defined change event.
See the following topics:
"The Rules Simulator Window" on the next page 15 Using the Rules Editor App The Rules Simulator Window Unlike most other areas of the MediaCentral Cloud UX user interface, the Rules Simulator tool is a floating window that appears initially at the bottom-right corner of the user interface. You can move the window around your screen, or close and reopen it at your leisure to evaluate other rules or expressions. When you reopen the tool, the system displays the window in its last known position. You can also vertically resize the window to change the visible area.
The Rules Simulator window is divided between four primary areas, as described in the following illustration and table.
Pane Description 1 Toolbar The toolbar is a "drag handler" that allows you to move the window and includes the Minimize application button to close the window.
2 Tabs The EXPRESSIONS and RULES tabs let you toggle between the Expression and Rule pane on the right-hand side of the window. By default, the Expression pane is shown.
3 Change Event pane The Change Event pane is always shown. Use the Change Event pane to define a change event against which expressions and rules are to be evaluated. The Change Event pane shows the following:
- Change Event Template selector
- JSON display and formatting panel
- JSON formatting errors below the JSON display and formatting panel
- Clear and Format JSON buttons
For more information, see "Defining a Change Event" on page 422.
4 Expression/Rule Depending on the selected tab, the Expression or Rule pane is shown on the pane right-hand side of the Rules Simulator window.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Pane Description Use the Expression pane to formulate or paste a used expression and to evaluate the expression against the change event. The Expression pane shows the following:
- Expression formulation panel. If the Rules Simulator was opened from an
Edit Expression dialog box, the expression is already inserted.
- Simulate button
- Clear button to remove the contents from the Expression formulation
and Simulation Results panels Simulation Result panel
- For more information, see "Evaluating an Expression" on page 423.
Use the Rule pane to provide a Rule ID and to evaluate the rule against the change event. You can also evaluate all rules in the rules DB. The Rule pane shows the following:
Rule Name field (populated automatically, read-only)
- Rule ID field to paste a copied Rule ID. If the rule is opened via drag and
- drop, the Rule ID will be filled automatically.
- Evaluate all rules available in database toggle
- Evaluate button
- Evaluation Result panel
For more information, see "Evaluating Rules" on page 425.
To open the Rules Simulator window:
- Click the Open Simulator button on the Rules Editor toolbar.
- Open an action where you used an expression, then open the Edit Expression dialog box for the field
of type Expression and in the dialog box click the Open Simulator button. See "Using Expressions" on page 417.
The window appears initially at the bottom-right corner of the user interface. If you already opened the Rules Simulator during your current session and changed its position, the window appears at this position. If opened from the Edit Expression dialog box, the expression is already inserted in the Rules Simulator.
To move the Rules Simulator window:
- Click on the toolbar and move the window to change its position.
The window retains its position for the length of your current user session.
To resize the Rules Simulator window:
- Click and drag the top or the bottom of the window to change its size.
The window retains this size for the length of your current user session.
You cannot expand the size of the window on a horizontal scale.
15 Using the Rules Editor App To resize the panes in the Simulator window:
- Click the vertical border between two panes and drag it to the left or right to change their size.
The window retains the pane size for the length of your current user session.
To close the Rules Simulator window:
- Click the Minimize application button on the Rules Simulator toolbar.
The window is closed. Contents, size and position are maintained for the length of your user session and are restored when you re-open the window. When you close the browser, the system removes all content from the Rules Simulator window.
Defining a Change Event Rules always trigger based on events. Therefore, you first must define a change event against which you then can evaluate an expression or a rule. You have the following options to define a change event:
Use a change event template provided in the Rules Simulator and modify it in the Change Event pane.
- Use a Kafka Client to retrieve the JSON of a change event and paste it to the Change Event pane.
- The Rules Simulator's Change Event Template list provides a created, deleted and updated template for the
following:
- interplay-mam (video)
- interplay-pam (masterclip)
- wolftech-news (pubpoint)
- wolftech-news (story)
To define a change event:
1. Click the Open Simulator button on the Rules Editor toolbar.
The Rules Simulator window opens.
2. Do one of the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
- Select a change event template from the Change Event pane's Change Event Template list.
- Paste a copied change event JSON to the JSON display and formatting panel.
3. Edit the JSON in the JSON display and formatting panel as needed.
For example, adapt Ids in the template to the ones used in your system; for Wolftech News templates, you also might want to add custom properties.
Once input is provided or changed, the Rules Simulator validates the JSON schema when you click outside the JSON display and formatting panel. Errors are shown in red below the JSON display and formatting panel, as illustrated in the following example for change event schema errors.
If you violated the JSON structure, a Syntax Error is shown. Change event schema messages are hidden until the JSON syntax error is repaired. After that, the hidden event schema messages are shown again.
4. (optional) If you pasted a change event into the JSON display and formatting panel, click the Format
JSON button to format the input in a valid JSON format.
5. (optional) To remove the contents from the Change Event pane, click the Clear button.
Evaluating an Expression Defining expressions in actions is a very powerful but not easy to use option. Therefore, the Rules Simulator lets you evaluate expressions against a defined change event in its Expression Simulator pane.
You have several options to insert an expression to be evaluated in the Expression pane:
- Type an expression.
- Copy and paste an expression.
- Directly insert an expression by opening the Rules Simulator from the Edit Expression dialog box in an
action.
Vice versa, you can also copy an evaluated expression in the Expression pane and then paste it into the Edit Expression dialog box of an action's Expression field. See "Using Expressions" on page 417.
15 Using the Rules Editor App To manually enter and evaluate an expression:
1. Click the Open Simulator button on the Rules Editor toolbar.
The Rules Simulator window opens.
2. In the Rules Simulator's Change Event pane, define the change event against which the expression is
to be evaluated. See "Defining a Change Event" on page 422.
3. (if needed) Click the EXPRESSIONS tab to show the Expression pane.
4. Insert an expression in the Expression pane. In the following example, this.entityId.
5. Click the Simulate button.
The results of the evaluation are shown in the "Simulation Result" panel. In error case, an error message opens.
6. (optional) To remove the contents from the Expression pane, click the Clear button.
To evaluate an expression already used in an Edit Expression dialog box:
1. Open an action that uses an expression in the Rules Editor's Details pane.
2. In the Details pane, hover the cursor over the field of type Expression and click the "Edit expression"
button that is shown.
The Edit Expression dialog box opens.
3. In the Edit Expression dialog box, click the Open Simulator button.
The Rules Simulator window opens. The expression is already entered in the Expression pane.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
4. In the Rules Simulator's Change Event pane, define the change event against which the expression is
to be evaluated. See "Defining a Change Event" on page 422.
5. Click the Simulate button.
The results of the evaluation are shown in the "Simulation Result" panel.
In error case, an Expression Evaluation Error message opens.
6. (optional) To remove the contents from the Expression pane, click the Clear button.
Evaluating Rules The Rules Simulator lets you evaluate rules against a defined change event. The evaluation checks if the rule triggers on the event. You have the following options:
- Evaluate a single rule against the defined change event.
- Evaluate all rules in the Rule DB against the defined change event.
15 Using the Rules Editor App To evaluate an individual rule:
1. Open the Rules Editor app.
2. Select a system/rule type from the sidebar.
3. Do one of the following:
- To open the rule via drag and drop:
a. Open the Rules Simulator window.
b. In the Rules Simulator window, click the RULES tab to show the Rule pane.
c. Move or minimize the Rules Simulator window so that you see the Rules Editor's RULES tab.
d. In the Rules Editor, select a rule in the RULES tab's rules table and drag and drop it to the Rules Simulator's Rule pane.
- To open the rule by using the Rule ID:
a. Select a rule in the Rules Editor's RULES tab.
b. In the Rules Editor's Details pane, expand the Additional information section and copy the Rule ID.
c. Open the Rules Simulator.
d. (if needed) In the Rules Simulator window, click the RULES tab to show the Rule pane.
e. Paste the copied ID into the Rule pane's Rule ID field.
4. In the Rules Simulator's Change Event pane, define the change event against which the rule is to be
evaluated. See "Defining a Change Event" on page 422.
5. Click the Evaluate button.
15 Using the Rules Editor App If the rule matches, its JSON is shown in the Evaluation Result panel.
In error case, an error message opens.
To evaluate the full Rule DB:
1. Open the Rules Editor app.
2. Select a system/rule type from the sidebar.
3. Open the Rules Simulator window.
4. In the Rules Simulator's Change Event pane, define the change event against which the Rules DB is to
be evaluated. See "Defining a Change Event" on page 422.
5. In the Rules Simulator window, click the RULES tab to show the Rule pane.
6. In the Rule pane, click the "Evaluate all rules available in the database" toggle button so it turns blue.
7. Click the Evaluate button.
The results of the evaluation – the JSON of all rules that match – are shown in the Evaluation Result panel. In error case, an error message opens.
Editing a Rule You can edit the items and properties of a rule at any time. The following process details all editing options for all rule types.
To edit a rule:
1. (optional) Temporarily stop event processing in the Rules Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Rules
Engine Event Processing" on page 345.
2. Select the rule type from the app’s sidebar.
3. In the Rules and Items panel's RULES tab, select the rule to be edited.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
4. Change the name of the rule: In the Details pane, replace the current name in the Rule Name field.
Rule names need to be unique. If you enter a name that is already used for another rule, the name will be appended with a counter in brackets when you save the rule.
5. Click the Expand toggle button in front of Additional information and change the comment in the
Comment text box.
6. Change the status of the rule: In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- To enable a disabled rule, click the gray Enable toggle so it turns blue.
- To disable an enabled rule, click the blue Enable toggle so it turns gray.
7. Replace the trigger.
a. In the Define Rule panel, hover the cursor over the trigger, click the Delete button that is shown.
b. Confirm the Delete Trigger security prompt by clicking Delete.
c. In the Rules and Items panel's ITEMS tab, click the trigger you want to add.
8. Replace the entity.
a. In the Define Rule panel, hover the cursor over the entity and click the Delete button that is shown.
b. Confirm the Delete Entity security prompt by clicking Delete.
c. In the Rules and Items panel's ITEMS tab, click the entity you want to add.
9. (Asset Management) Change the asset type for the entity.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the entity.
In the Details panel, the Asset section is shown.
b. Click the Show all link.
c. In the Asset Type fly-out, select other asset types.
d. Click the Apply button (√).
10. Change the conditions of the entity.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the entity.
b. In the Details panel, expand all conditions.
c. Change the parameters of a condition. You have the following options:
15 Using the Rules Editor App a. Change the attribute.
b. Change the comparison operator.
c. Add an additional value, change the value or delete a value (if supported by the selected operator).
d. Change the match type (if supported by the selected operator).
d. Move a condition:
a. Hover the cursor over the condition header.
b. Click the Move up or Move down button that is shown. Condition 1 only shows the Move down button, the last condition only shows the Move up button.
The condition is moved, and the condition counter is adapted accordingly.
e. Remove a condition:
a. Hover the cursor over the condition header.
b. Click the Delete condition button that is shown.
c. Click Delete in the security prompt that is shown.
f. Change the way how all conditions are to be combined by clicking the AND or OR button.
For more information, see "Defining Entities" on page 360.
11. Add an action.
a. In the Rules and Items panel's ITEMS tab, click the action you want to add.
b. Define at least the mandatory properties of the action.
12. Delete an action.
15 Using the Rules Editor App a. In the Define Rule panel, hover the cursor over the action.
b. Click the Delete button that is shown.
c. Click Delete in the security prompt.
13. Change the parameters for an Add to Folder action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add To Folder.
b. In the Details panel, select another Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
d. Select another folder from the Folder list.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Folder Action" on page 385.
14. (Asset Management, Production Management) Change the parameters for an Add to Publishing
Output action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add to Publishing Output.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
c. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Publishing Output Action" on page 388.
15. (Asset Management, Production Management) Change the parameters for an Add to Story action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Add to Story.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
c. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Story Action" on page 389.
16. Change the parameters for an AM Orchestration action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action AM Orchestration.
b. In the Details panel, select another Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select another action specification from the Specification list.
15 Using the Rules Editor App d. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
e. Specify values for the selected specification.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an AM Orchestration Action" on page 390.
17. Change the parameters for a Create Entity action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Entity.
b. In the Details panel, select another Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select another asset or sequence type from the Entity Type list.
d. Provide another entity name in the Entity Name field.
e. Change the values of added properties.
f. Add properties and values.
g. Remove added properties.
h. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Entity Action" on page 391.
18. Change the parameters for a Create Folder action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Folder.
b. In the Details panel, select another Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select another parent folder from the Parent Folder list.
d. Type the name of another folder to be created in the Folder Name field.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Folder Action" on page 393.
19. (Wolftech News) Change the parameters for a Create News Sequence action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Sequence.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
c. Change the name for the sequence to be created in the Name field.
d. Select another folder in which the sequence is to be created from the Folder list.
e. Select another sequence type from the Type list.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "(Preview) Defining a Create News Sequence Action" on page 396.
20. (Wolftech News) Change the parameters for a Create Publishing Output action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Publishing Output.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
c. Provide another Publishing Output name.
d. Select one or multiple publishing destinations from the Publishing destinations list.
e. Change the Timeless toggle as needed.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Publishing Output Action" on page 401.
21. (Wolftech News) Change the parameters for a Create Sequence action.
15 Using the Rules Editor App a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Create Sequence.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
c. Change the name for the sequence to be created in the Name field.
d. Select another folder in which the sequence is to be created from the Folder list.
e. Select another sequence type from the Type list.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Sequence Action" on page 398.
22. (Production Management) Change the parameters of a Media Analytics action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Media Analytics.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
c. Change the title of the Media Analytics job to be shown in the MediaCentral Cloud UX Process app by using the Title field (type Expression).
d. Select other audio tracks from the Tracks list.
e. Change the name of the transcript to be shown in the Transcript tab by entering a new name in the Result Name field.
For more information, see "Defining a Media Analytics Action" on page 403.
23. Change the parameters for a Notification action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Notification.
b. In the Details panel, type another name in the Title field.
c. Type another message text in the Message field.
d. Select one or more Platform User Management groups which should receive the notification from the Destinations list.
For more information, see "Defining a Notification Action" on page 404.
24. (Production Management, Asset Management) Change the parameters for a Publish action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Publish.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
c. Change the title of the Publisher clip in the Name field.
d. Select another publish template to be used from the Publish Template list.
e. Change the status of the "Publish clip when done" toggle.
For more information, see "Defining a Publish Action" on page 406.
25. (Wolftech News) Change the parameters for a Publish Publishing Output action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Publish Publishing Output.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved.
c. Change the title of the Publisher clip in the Name field.
d. Select another publish template to be used from the Publish Template list.
e. Change the status of the "Publish clip when done" toggle.
f. Change the properties of the Publishing Output to be used for the publishing metadata.
g. (optional) Change the publishing destination settings.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
  - Select another publishing destination from the Publishing destination list.
  - Select another template that is relevant for the media to be published from the Template
list.
  - Select another property that holds the information about the media to be published from
the Asset list.
h. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "(Preview) Defining a Publish Publishing Output Action" on page 407.
26. (Wolftech News, Production Management) Change the parameters of a Send to Playback action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Send to Playback.
b. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID (Sequence ID) from the Source Event is used.
c. Select another STP profile from the Profile list.
d. Enable or disable the Burn Graphics/High Priority/Overwrite toggles as needed.
e. Change the name of the process to be shown in the Process app.
f. (Wolftech News, optional) Change the Publishing destination settings (Publishing destination, Template, Asset).
g. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Send to Playback Action" on page 408.
27. Change the parameters for an Update Metadata action.
a. In the Define Rule panel, select the action Update Metadata.
b. In the Details panel, select another Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. (optional) Change the default behavior how the entity to be used is retrieved. By default, the entity ID from the Source Event is used.
d. Change the values of added properties.
e. Add properties and values.
f. Remove added properties.
g. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Update Metadata Action" on page 410.
28. Click the Save button to save the rule.
If the rule shows validation issues, a Validation dialog box opens that summarizes the validation issues. Click Save Anyway to save the rule with all validation issues, or click Cancel and resolve the listed issues before saving again.
29. (if paused) Resume event processing in the Rules Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Rules Engine
Event Processing" on page 345.
Duplicating a Rule As an alternative to building an entirely new rule, the Rules Editor app allows you to duplicate an existing rule to expedite the rule creation process.
> **Note:** The Duplicate feature is only activated for a rule that has no unsaved change.
15 Using the Rules Editor App To duplicate a rule:
1. In the Rules and Items panel, open the RULES tab.
2. Do one of the following:
- Right-click a rule in the rules table and select Duplicate in the context-menu that opens.
- Double-click a rule in the rules table, and in the Details panel click the Duplicate button.
A new duplicated rule is created and loaded into the Define Rule and Details panels.
  - The name of the rule is "Copy <original rule name>".
  - A new Rule ID is assigned to the rule.
  - The rule is disabled.
  - The rule is not saved yet.
3. (optional) Rename the rule and edit the items of the rule. See "Editing a Rule" on page 427.
4. Enable the rule.
5. Click the Save button to save the rule.
If you click Cancel, the duplicated rule is discarded.
Copying and Moving Rules The Rules Editor app allows you to move or copy a rule from one Production Management or Asset Management module to another module. This feature might be helpful when a rule is imported and references a module which is not available in your setup.
Note the following limitations:
- Copying and moving rules is supported for Production Management and Asset Management
modules.
You can copy and move rules only between modules of the same type (Production Management to
- Production Management, Asset Management to Asset Management).
You can copy or move rules from an offline module, but you cannot copy or move rules to an offline
- module.
After you moved the last rule from an offline module, the entry of the unavailable module is removed from the sidebar.
- You cannot copy or move a rule that has unsaved changes; in this case, you will be prompted to save
or discard your changes first.
To copy or move rules:
1. Select an Asset Management or Production Management module from the app’s sidebar.
2. In the Rules and Items panel, do one of the following:
- Select an individual rule.
- Use Ctrl+click to select several non-consecutive rules in the table.
- Use Shift+click to select several consecutive rules in the table.
- Select a rule and use Shift+ Arrow Up or Arrow Down key to select several consecutive rules in
the table.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
3. Drag and drop your selection to the target module (that has the same system type) in the sidebar.
The Move/Copy to dialog box opens.
4. Do one of the following:
- To move the rules to the target module, click the Move button.
- To copy the rules to the target module, click the Copy button.
A progress indicator shows the progress of the move or copy operation.
If an error occurs during the operation, a message, as shown in the following example, opens.
In this case, the Move button is replaced by a Retry button. Click the Retry button to retry the copy or move operation that has failed. The Move/Copy to dialog box stays open as long as the issues persists. Click the Cancel button to close the dialog box if you do not want to retry further.
After the move or copy operation has finished successfully, the Move/Copy to dialog box closes.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
  - On Move: The Rules Editor UI still shows the Rules and Items panel of the source module. The
rules table is reloaded. In each moved rule, the System ID is adapted.
  - On Copy: The Rules Editor UI switches to the Rules and Items panel of the target module. In
each copied rule, the System ID is adapted and a new Rule ID is set.
Exporting Rules The Rules Editor app provides options to export rules. You can export the following:
