An individual rule selected in the Rules and Items panel or the rule that is currently being loaded into
- the Define Rule and Details panels.
The export creates a single JSON file.
- Several rules selected in the Rules and Items panel.
The export creates a Zip file containing the selected rules as a separate JSON file.
- All rules.
The export creates a Zip file containing each rule of the system selected in the sidebar as a separate JSON file.
Depending on your web browser's settings the following happens during the export operation:
- The rules are directly downloaded to the download location specified in your web browser’s settings.
- The Save As dialog box opens that lets you save the files to a selected directory.
> **Note:** The Export feature is only activated for rules that have no unsaved changes.
To export an individual rule:
1. Open the Rules and Items panel and make sure that no rule has unsaved changes.
2. Do one of the following:
- In the rules table, right-click a rule and select Export from the context-menu.
- Double-click a rule in the rules table to open it in the Define Rule panel and click the Export rule
button on the Define Rule panel header.
The file is saved in JSON format. The file name is "rules-<time stamp>.json".
To export several rules at the same time:
1. Open the Rules and Items panel and make sure that no rule has unsaved changes.
2. In the rules table, do one of the following:
- Use Ctrl+click to select several non-consecutive rules in the table.
- Use Shift+click to select several consecutive rules in the table.
- Select a rule and use Shift+ Arrow Up or Arrow Down key to select several consecutive rules in
the table.
3. Right-click the selection and select Export in the context-menu that is shown.
The selected rules are downloaded as a Zip archive. The file name is "rules-<time stamp>.zip". Each exported rule is included as a separate *.json file in the Zip archive. For the rules in the Zip archive, the rule name, as set in Rules Editor, is used as file name. Invalid characters are replaced with underscores in the file name.
15 Using the Rules Editor App To export all rules:
1. Open the Rules and Items panel and make sure that no rule has unsaved changes.
The Export rule button is activated.
2. In the Rules and Items panel, click the Export all rules button.
All rules are downloaded as a Zip archive. The file name is "rules-<time stamp>.zip". Each exported rule is included as a separate *.json file in the Zip archive. For the rules in the Zip archive, the rule name, as set in Rules Editor, is used as file name. Invalid characters are replaced with underscores in the file name.
Importing Rules The Rules Editor app provides options to import previously exported rules. You can import the following:
Individual rules (JSON files).
- Rules included in a Zip file (containing each rule as a separate JSON file).
- During the import, you have options to define "enabled" and "overwrite" settings for the import rules.
> **Note:** The Import Rules feature is context-independent, not restricted to the currently selected module. Rules
are imported according to the system type/system ID information stored in the exported JSON file.
To import rules:
1. (optional) Temporarily stop event processing in the Rules Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Rules
Engine Event Processing" on page 345.
2. Select a rule type from the app’s sidebar.
3. In the Rules Editor header, click the Import Rules button.
The Import Rules dialog box opens.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
4. Click the Browse button.
The Open dialog box opens.
5. Select the files you want to import: individual rules (*.json files) or rules in a *.zip file or a combination
of both. Multi-selection is supported.
6. Click Open.
The Open dialog box closes. The names of the files you selected are displayed in the Select Files field.
7. Define the "enabled" status of new rules (rules with new Rules ID).
- To disable new rule(s), leave the blue "Disable new rules" toggle enabled (default).
- To enable new rule(s), click the blue "Disable new rules" toggle so it turns gray.
8. Define if the imported rule(s) should overwrite a rule with the same Rule ID.
- To keep all rules and do not overwrite any rule with an imported rule, leave the gray "Overwrite
existing rules" toggle disabled (default).
If a rule with the same ID already exists, the rule will be imported as a new rule with a new ID.
- To overwrite each rule with the imported rule that has the same Rule ID, click the gray
"Overwrite existing rules" toggle so it turns blue.
If a rule with the same ID already exists, the rule will be overwritten by the imported rule.
In both cases, the name of the imported rule will be changed (appended by a counter in brackets) if the rule name already exists.
9. Define the "enabled" status of existing rules (rules with no new Rules ID).
> **Note:** The "Keep enabled status of existing rules" toggle can only be used as long as the "Overwrite
existing rules" button is enabled.
- To keep the "enabled" status as present in existing rules, leave the blue "Keep enabled status
of existing rules" toggle enabled (default).
- To overwrite the "enabled" status of existing rules, click the blue "Keep enabled status of
existing rules" toggle so it turns gray.
10. Click Import.
The Import Rules dialog box is closed. The rules are imported. If the currently selected rule type/system is affected by the import, the Rules and Items panel is refreshed.
After the import, the properties of the rules might be altered as follows:
  - Name: Appended by a counter in brackets if a rule with the same name exists.
  - Rule ID: Kept or changed depending on the "Overwrite" setting during the import.
  - Created on: will be set to the time of the import.
  - Created by: will be set to the user who imported the rule.
  - Modified on: will be set to the time of the import.
  - Modified by: will be set to the user who imported the rule.
  - Enabled status: Kept or changed depending on the "Disable new rules" and "Keep enabled
status of existing rules" settings during import.
11. (if paused) Resume event processing in the Rules Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Rules Engine
Event Processing" on page 345.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Deleting Rules The Rule Editor app allows you to delete rules at any time. Note that there is no undo for the delete operation. Consider disabling the rule instead of finally deleting it.
To delete an individual rule:
1. In the Rules and Items panel, open the RULES tab.
2. In the rules table, do one of the following:
- Hover the cursor over the rule to be deleted and click the Trash icon that is shown.
- Right-click the rule to be deleted and select Delete in the context-menu that is shown.
A Delete Rule security prompt opens.
3. Do one of the following:
- Click the Delete button to permanently delete the rule.
The rule is removed from the RULES tab table. The Define Rule and Details panels are reset to initial state.
- Click the Cancel button or press the Esc key to exit the deletion process.
To delete several rules at the same time:
1. In the Rules and Items panel, open the RULES tab.
2. In the rules table, do one of the following:
- Use Ctrl+click to select several non-consecutive rules in the table.
- Use Shift+click to select several consecutive rules in the table.
- Select a rule and use Shift+ Arrow Up or Arrow Down key to select several consecutive rules in
the table.
3. Right-click the selection and select Delete in the context-menu that is shown.
A Delete Rules security prompt opens.
15 Using the Rules Editor App
4. Do one of the following:
- Click the Delete button to permanently delete the rules.
The rules are removed from the RULES tab table.
- Click the Cancel button or press the Esc key to exit the deletion process.
Rules Validation The Rules Editor back-end validates rules consistency on different levels. Validation information is provided in all panels of the app. A rule is validated in the following cases:
- When you create or edit the rule.
- When the rules table in the Rules and Items panel's RULES tab is reloaded.
- When you save the rule.
For more information, see the following topics:
- "Rules and Items Panel: Rules Tab" below
- "Define Rule Panel" on the next page
Rules and Items Panel: Rules Tab In the left column of the RULES tab table a warning icon is shown for a rule in the following cases:
- The rule is missing a trigger.
- The rule is missing an entity.
- The rule is missing an action.
When you hover your cursor over the icon, a tool-tip shows the missing items.
Auto-Fixing Changed Action Specifications When you select a rule in the Rules and Items panel's RULES tab and changes in an action specification of the rule are detected (for example, a property has been added to or removed from the action specification) an “Action Specification(s) Changed” window will be displayed. The window lists all changes, as shown in the following example.
15 Using the Rules Editor App The rule will be automatically adapted and saved. After that, the action specification in the rule is aligned again. However, additional manual steps might be necessary to make the action valid, such as setting a new mandatory parameter.
Click OK to close the “Action Specification(s) Changed” window.
Define Rule Panel When you hover the cursor over a warning icon in the Define Rule panel, a tool-tip informs you about required configuration steps that have not finished in the Details panel yet.
Item Message Entity Summarizes warnings from all its conditions. For Asset Management, an additional warning related to asset or sequence types can be shown.
Action Notification l The mandatory field Title is not set.
- The mandatory field Destination is not set.

Playback
- The system is not selected.
- The profile is not selected.

- The configured action is no longer in line with the action specification.
- The action specification is not available.

- The configured action is no longer in line with the action specification.
- An action specification is not available.

of the invalid folder.
The Folder Name is not provided.

- Folder values cannot be loaded.

- The values of Properties cannot be loaded.
Details Panel The Details panel shows warning icons in the item-specific sections. When you hover your cursor over the icon, a tool-tip shows the reason for the warning.
Item Message Individual Condition l The condition is incomplete (mandatory fields are not set).
- Attributes used in the condition do not exist any longer.
- A drop-down entry of an attribute does not exist any longer in the list of drop-
down values.

header Entity - Condition Shows warnings for the individual condition, such as missing mandatory values.

For more information, see "Troubleshooting" on page 451.
Validate on Save When you save the rule it is validated and parsed. If issues are detected, the results of the validation are summarized in a Validation dialog box. Validation issues are shown as warnings, parsing issues are shown as "incomplete".
Do one of the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
- Click Cancel and resolve the listed issues before saving again.
- Click Save Anyway to save the rule with validation issues. Items shown as incomplete, are completely
removed from the rule.
Using Rules of Unavailable Systems If a Production Management or Asset Management system is offline or temporarily not available, its entry in the sidebar is dimmed and its icon shows a red dot to the upper left side; instead of the name, the system ID is shown. An entry of a unavailable system can also appear when an imported rule references an unavailable system.
When you select an unavailable system in the sidebar, its rules are loaded in the Rules and Items tab but an error message is shown. Click anywhere in the UI to close the message.
The Data Models pill in the header at the top of the Rules Editor app is colored red, and its health indicator tool-tip shows the message"Data model information could not be retrieved for <Production/Asset Management system ID>".
15 Using the Rules Editor App However, you can review the details of the rules and apply some limited actions.
UI for Rules of an Unavailable System When you select the rule of an unavailable system, several controls are disabled.
- Rules and Items panel:
  - RULES tab: Shows the full table with all columns; the Create a rule and Import Default Rules
features are disabled. In the context-menu of a rule, the Duplicate option is deactivated.
  - ITEMS tab: Is disabled
Define Rule panel:
  - Shows the items of the rule in read-only mode.
  - The Export rule button is enabled when you select a rule in the Rules and Items panel.
  - The Save and Cancel buttons will be enabled after the "Enable" status of the rule has changed.
  - The Duplicate button is disabled.
Details panel:
  - Shows the general section that lets you change the Enable status of the rule.
  - Shows the Trigger/Entity/Actions sections in read-only mode.
Options for Rules of an Unavailable System You have the following options for a rule of an unavailable system:
- Enable/disable the rule
- Display the rule's details in the Details panel
- Export rule(s)
- Copy or Move rule(s). See "Copying and Moving Rules" on page 434.
- Delete rule(s)
To change the Enable status of a rule:
1. In the Rule Type panel, select the unavailable system.
2. In the Rules and Items panel, select a rule.
3. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- To enable a disabled rule, click the gray Enable toggle so it turns blue.
- To disable an enabled rule, click the blue Enable toggle so it turns gray.
4. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button.
15 Using the Rules Editor App To export rules:
1. To export an individual rule, do one of the following:
- In the Rules and Items panel, right-click a rule and select Export from the context-menu.
- Load a rule in the Define Rule panel and click the Export rule button on the Define Rule panel
header.
The file is saved in JSON format. The file name is "<rule name>.json".
2. To export several rules at the same time, do the following:
a. In the Rules and Items panel, do one of the following:
- Use Ctrl+click to select several non-consecutive rules in the table.
- Use Shift+click to select several consecutive rules in the table.
- Select a rule and use Shift+ Arrow Up or Arrow Down key to select several consecutive
rules in the table.
b. Right-click the selection and select Export in the context-menu that is shown.
The selected rules are downloaded as a Zip archive.
3. To export all rules, do the following:
- In the Rules and Items panel, click the Export all rules button.
All rules are downloaded as a Zip archive.
To delete rules:
1. To delete an individual rule, do the following:
a. In the Rules and Items panel, hover the cursor over the rule to be deleted and click the Trash icon that is shown.
b. Click Delete in the Delete Rule security prompt.
2. To delete several rules at the same time, do the following:
a. Do one of the following:
- Use Ctrl+click to select several non-consecutive rules in the table.
- Use Shift+click to select several consecutive rules in the table.
- Select a rule and use Shift+ Arrow Up or Arrow Down key to select several consecutive
rules in the table.
b. Right-click the selection and select Delete in the context-menu that is shown.
c. Click Delete in the Delete Rule security prompt.
Using the Category Other The rule type Other shows rules uploaded to the Rules Engine that are unknown for the Rules Editor but can be executed by the Rules Engine; for example, rules that have been created via the API.
> **Note:** Usually this rule type is empty. Rules that are unknown to the Rules Editor are exceptions. However, if
existing, the Rules Editor makes these rules visible.
UI for Rules in the Category Other When you select the rule type Other, the Rules Editor app shows a simplified UI.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Rules and Items panel:
  - RULES tab: Shows a simplified table with less columns (Name, Enabled, Modification Date); the
features Create a rule and Import default rules are disabled.
  - ITEMS tab: Is disabled
Define Rule panel:
  - Does not show the items of the rule.
  - The Export rule button is enabled when you select a rule in the Rules and Items panel.
  - The Save and Cancel buttons will be enabled after the "Enable" status of the rule has changed.
  - The Duplicate button is disabled.
- Details panel:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
  - Shows the general section that lets you change the Enable status of the rule.
  - Shows a specific, read-only section JSON Data.
Options for Rules in the Category Other You have the following options for a rule of type "Other":
Enable/disable the rule
- Export rule(s)
- Display the rule's JSON in the Details panel
- Delete rule(s)
- To change the Enable status of a rule:
1. In the Rule Type panel, select Other.
2. In the Rules and Items panel, select a rule.
3. In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- To enable a disabled rule, click the gray Enable toggle so it turns blue.
- To disable an enabled rule, click the blue Enable toggle so it turns gray.
4. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button.
To export rules:
1. To export an individual rule, do one of the following:
- In the Rules and Items panel, right-click a rule and select Export from the context-menu.
- Load a rule in the Define Rule panel and click the Export rule button on the Define Rule panel
header.
The file is saved in JSON format. The file name is "<rule name>.json".
2. To export several rules at the same time, do the following:
a. In the Rules and Items panel, do one of the following:
- Use Ctrl+click to select several non-consecutive rules in the table.
- Use Shift+click to select several consecutive rules in the table.
- Select a rule and use Shift+ Arrow Up or Arrow Down key to select several consecutive
rules in the table.
b. Right-click the selection and select Export in the context-menu that is shown.
The selected rules are downloaded as a Zip archive.
3. To export all rules, do the following:
- In the Rules and Items panel, click the Export all rules button.
All rules are downloaded as a Zip archive.
To show the JSON data of a rule:
1. In the Rule Type panel, select Other.
2. In the Rules and Items panel, select a rule.
3. In the Details panel, click the Expand toggle button in front of the JSON Data section.
15 Using the Rules Editor App To delete rules:
1. To delete an individual rule, do the following:
a. In the Rules and Items panel, hover the cursor over the rule to be deleted and click the Trash icon that is shown.
b. Click Delete in the Delete Rule security prompt.
2. To delete several rules at the same time, do the following:
a. Do one of the following:
- Use Ctrl+click to select several non-consecutive rules in the table.
- Use Shift+click to select several consecutive rules in the table.
- Select a rule and use Shift+ Arrow Up or Arrow Down key to select several consecutive
rules in the table.
b. Right-click the selection and select Delete in the context-menu that is shown.
c. Click Delete in the Delete Rule security prompt.
Troubleshooting The purpose of this appendix is to provide information on tools and processes that can be used to identify and resolve issues for Rules Editor, Rules Engine and Action Engines. See the following topics:
"Issues Indicated by Rules Editor Health Indicators" on the next page 15 Using the Rules Editor App Issues Indicated by Rules Editor Health Indicators The header at the top of the Rules Editor app provides you with a health check of the back-end components that enable the functionality in the Rules Editor app. Issues are indicated by yellow or red color, depending on the severity of the issue.
Yellow Rules Engine health indicator Issue: The Rules Engine health indicator in the Rules Editor header is yellow.
Possible reason: Event processing in the RulesEngine has been paused.
Solution/Action: To resume event processing in the Rules Engine, hover the cursor over the orange Rules Engine pill and click the Resume Rules Engine processing button in the tool-tip.
Red Rules Engine health indicator Issue: The Rules Engine health indicator in the Rules Editor header is red.
Possible reason: The Rules Engine is not accessible.
Solution/Action: In Kubernetes dashboard search for the deployment avid-platform-rulesengine- rulesengine and check the status and log files. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 467.
Yellow Action Engines health indicator Issue: The Action Engines health indicator in the Rules Editor header is yellow.
Possible reason: One of the registered Action Engines is in error state.
Solution/Action: In Kubernetes dashboard search for the deployment of the Action Engine and check the status and log files. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 467.
Red Action Engines health indicator Issue: The Action Engines health indicator in the Rules Editor header is red. The fly-out menu shows "No Action Engine found".
Possible reason:
- The Rules Engine is not accessible and thus cannot provide information on the registered Action
Engines.
- Not all required platform automation components are deployed and are running properly.
Solution/Action: Check the deployment and status of the involved components in Kubernetes dashboard.
See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 467.
Red Data Models health indicator Issue: The Data Models health indicator is red.
Possible reason: Attributes cannot be retrieved for the selected module.
Solution/Action: Check if the Asset Management/Production Management module is up and running.
15 Using the Rules Editor App For Asset Management, you might want to check the Services view in the Asset Management Control
- Center. See "Working with the Services View" in the MediaCentral | Asset Management Control
Center User's Guide.
Unavailable Modules Shown in the Rules Editor Sidebar The following issue might be indicated in the Rules Editor sidebar.
Asset Management/Production Management: Modules shown as unavailable Issue: The Rules Editor sidebar shows the module as unavailable (dimmed entry, ID shown instead of the system name). The Data Models pill in the Rules Editor header shows a “Data model could not be retrieved for <system ID>” error.
Possible reason:
The module is offline or temporarily not available.
- One or more imported rules reference an unavailable module.
- Solution/Action: Consider moving the rules to an available module or copying the rules and later deleting
the rules from the unavailable module.
> **Note:** Elements of the rule (properties, system references) might not be available on the target module.
Therefore, a copied/moved rule might become invalid. Check the rules after copying/moving.
1. Copy or move the rules to an available module. See"Copying and Moving Rules" on page 434.
After you moved the last rule from an unavailable module, the unavailable module entry is removed from the sidebar.
2. Check the Conditions of the copied/moved rules:
a. Properties used for Conditions might not be available in the data model of the target module.
Check and modify if necessary.
b. Asset Management only: An Asset Type used for the Entity might not be available in the data model of the target module. Check and modify if necessary.
3. Check and modify the Actions of the copied/moved rules if needed:
a. Some actions for Asset Management/Production Management are related to dedicated module instances (Send to Playback, AM Orchestration, Create Entity, Create Folder, Update Metadata): check and modify the used module references.
b. Some actions for Asset Management/Production Management might use properties that are not available in the data model of the target module (AM Orchestration, Create Entity, Update Metadata): check and modify the properties if needed.
c. AM Orchestration actions require that specific Action (process creation) templates have been defined in Asset Management Datamodel Administrator: check the Asset Management data model on the target module if the corresponding ACTION_<process class name> templates are available. See "Action Specifications" on page 384.
Sidebar shows an unavailable module with ID consisting only of zeros Issue: An unavailable Production Management or Asset Management module with a dummy ID 00000000- 0000-0000-0000-000000000000 is shown in the sidebar.
Possible reason: This is expected behavior after the import of Production Management or Asset Management default rules. See "Importing Default Rules" on page 343.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Solution/Action: Move the default rule to an available Production Management or Asset Management module. The unavailable module entry 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 is then removed from the sidebar.
Issues Shown for Actions in Rules Editor The Rules Editor back-end validates rules consistency on different levels. Rules validation information is provided in all panels of the app. The following issues might be indicated.
Action AM Orchestration System not available Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective service MAMOrchestrationCTC on the Asset Management module (Control Center > Services view).
Action Specification not available Issue: The Action shows an error “Action default specification not available” (tool-tip) resp. "Failed to load specification" (details).
Possible reason: For actions of type AM Orchestration, predefined action specifications are not provided by Avid but must be configured on each connected Asset Management module. On the referenced Asset Management module, no action specification has been configured.
Solution/Action: Configure at least one AM Orchestration action specification. See "Action Specifications" on page 384.
Action Create Entity No systems available Issue: The Action shows a “There are no systems available” error.
Possible reason: The respective CTC services of all connected Asset Management modules and Wolftech News are not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective CTC services:
- Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
Center > Services view).
- Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
System not available Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective CTC service.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
- Center > Services view).
- Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
Action Specification not available Issue: The Action shows a "Failed to load specification" error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the respective CTC services are in proper state:
Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management system (Control
- Center > Services view).
Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Values of list property cannot be loaded
Issue: The Action shows a “Failed to load values” error for a list property.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the respective CTC services are in proper state:
- Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
Center > Services view).
Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Action Create Folder
No systems available Issue: The Action shows a “There are no systems available” error.
Possible reason: The respective CTC service of all connected Asset Management and Production Management modules are not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective CTC services:
- Asset Management: Check the status of the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management
modules (Control Center > Services view).
Production Management: Check the status of the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral
- platform.
System not available Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective CTC service:
15 Using the Rules Editor App Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
- Center > Services view).
- Production Management: Check the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
Invalid value for Parent Folder Issue: The Action Create Folder shows “Invalid value. Please select a new value” for the Parent Folder.
Possible reason: The folder has been deleted or moved.
Solution/Action: Select a valid folder from the Parent Folder control.
Failed to load Parent Folder values Issue: The Action Create Folder shows “Failed to load values” for the Parent Folder.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the respective CTC services are in proper state:
Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module(Control
- Center > Services view).
- Production Management: Check the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
Action Media Analytics Action Specification not available Issue: The Action shows a "Failed to load specification" error.
Possible reason: The Action Engine or required action provider Avid Ada is not deployed or not running.
Solution/Action: Check the deployment and status of the involved components in Kubernetes dashboard.
See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 467.
Action Publish Failed to load publish templates Issue: The Action shows a "Failed to load" error for the Publish Template field.
Possible reason: The configured publish templates could not be retrieved from the Publisher SAAS platform.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check the logs of Action Engine Publisher, which might give information about the cause of the issue. Potentially, the configuration of the Publisher connection or the Action Engine Publisher or both is incomplete or invalid.
Action Send to Playback System not available Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
Values of profile cannot be loaded Issue: The Action shows a “The values of the selected profile cannot be loaded” error.
Possible reason:
1. The service providing the Send to Playback profiles is not deployed or running.
2. The Send to Playback profile does not exist or has errors.
Solution/Action:
1. Check the service ‘Avid Media Services’ in Kubernetes. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for
Troubleshooting" on page 467.
2. Check the profile in the MediaCentral Cloud UX Administration > Workflow Settings > Send to
Playback. For more information, see "Working with the Send to Playback Settings" on page 242.
Action Send to Playback (Wolftech) Action Specification not available Issue: The Action shows a "Failed to load specification" error.
Possible reason: The Action Engine PM-STP is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the deployment and status of the Action Engine PM STP in the Kubernetes dashboard.
Failed to load Publishing destination values Issue: The action shows “Failed to load values” for the Publishing destination, Template or Assets fields.
Possible reason: The Wolftech News CTC service is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the wolftech-news-ctc service on the MediaCentral platform is in proper state.
System not available Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
Values of profile cannot be loaded Issue: The Action shows a “The values of the selected profile cannot be loaded” error.
Possible reason:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
1. The Action Engine PM-STP is not available.
2. The service providing the Send to Playback profiles is not deployed or running.
3. The Send to Playback profile does not exist or has errors.
Solution/Action:
1. Check the deployment and status of the Action Engine PM STP in the Kubernetes dashboard.
2. Check the service ‘Avid Media Services’ in Kubernetes. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for
Troubleshooting" on page 467.
3. Check the profile in the MediaCentral Cloud UX Administration > Workflow Settings > Send to
Playback. For more information, see "Working with the Send to Playback Settings" on page 242.
Action Update Metadata No systems available Issue: The Action shows a “There are no systems available” error.
Possible reason: The respective CTC service of all connected Asset Management and Production Management modules and Wolftech News are not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective CTC services:
- Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
Center > Services view).
Production Management: Check the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- System not available
Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective CTC service:
Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
- Center > Services view).
- Production Management: Check the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
Action Specification not available Issue: The Action shows a "Failed to load specification" error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the respective CTC services are in proper state:
- Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management system (Control
Center > Services view).
- Production Management: Check the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Values of list property cannot be loaded
Issue: The Action shows a “Failed to load values” error for a list property.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the respective CTC services are in proper state:
- Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
Center > Services view).
Production Management: Check the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Action Create News Sequence
System not available Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the Production Management module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
Failed to load Folder values Issue: The action shows “Failed to load values” for the Folder.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the Production Management module is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the avid-pam-ctc service on the MediaCentral platform is in proper state.
Action Create Sequence System not available Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the Prodcution Management module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
Failed to load Folder values Issue: The action shows “Failed to load values” for the Folder.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the Production Management module is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the avid-pam-ctc service on the MediaCentral platform is in proper state.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Action Create Publishing Output Failed to load Publishing destination values Issue: The action shows “Failed to load values” for the Publishing destination field.
Possible reason: The Wolftech News CTC service is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the wolftech-news-ctc service on the MediaCentral platform is in proper state.
Action Publish Publishing Output Action Specification not available Issue: The Action shows a "Failed to load specification" error.
Possible reason: The Action Engine Publisher is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the deployment and status of the Action Engine Publisher in the Kubernetes dashboard.
Failed to load publish templates Issue: The Action shows a "Failed to load" error for the Publish Template field.
Possible reason: The configured publish templates could not be retrieved from the Publisher SAAS platform.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check the logs of Action Engine Publisher, which might give information about the cause of the issue. Potentially,.the configuration of the Publisher connection or the Action Engine Publisher or both is incomplete or invalid.
Failed to load Properties or Publishing destination values Issue: The action shows “Failed to load values” for the Properties, Publishing destination, Template or Assets fields.
Possible reason: The Wolftech News CTC service is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the wolftech-news-ctc service on the MediaCentral platform is in proper state.
The Rules Editor does not show the expected action Issue: In the Rules Editor's ITEMS pane, I do not see all actions.
Possible reason:
1. The selected rule type does not support this action type.
2. The necessary Action Engine is not deployed or running correctly. Consider especially the following
Action Engines that are only deployed with optional feature packs:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
  - Action Engine PM-STP (feature pack ae-pm-stp): required if you want to use Send to Playback
actions (in Production Management or Wolftech News rules or both).
  - Action Engine AM-Orchestration (feature pack: ae-am-orchestration): required if you want to
use AM Orchestration actions.
  - Action Engine Wolftech News (feature pack: wolftech): required if you want to use Wolftech
News-related actions (Add to Story, Add to Publishing Output, Create Publishing Output, Publish Publishing Output, Create Sequence, Create News Sequence, Send to Playback).
  - Action Engine Publisher (feature pack: publisher): required if you want to use Publish and
Publish Publishing Output actions.
Solution/Action:
1. Check if the rules type supports this action. See "Defining Actions" on page 382.
2. Check in Kubernetes if all Action Engines are running correctly. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard
for Troubleshooting" on page 467.
What to Check if an Expected Rule or Action is Not Executed In case a rule or an action, as part of an Platform Automation rule, is not executed, the following section might be helpful to resolve the issue.
Check the Rule in the Rule Editor Review the following:
Is the rule enabled?
- The Enabled status (green check mark) is directly shown on the Rules and Items panel table.
Is the rule still valid?
- Check if warnings are shown for elements of the rule.
Are configured conditions valid?
- Check if warnings are shown for conditions in the Details panel.
Are used expressions valid?
- Use the Rules Simulator to check the expression against a defined change event. See "Evaluating an
Expression" on page 423.
Rules always trigger based on events. Use the Rules Simulator to test if the rule would trigger on a
- defined change event. See "Evaluating Rules" on page 425.
- Was the rule imported or moved and references an unavailable module?
Check the Rules Editor sidebar for unavailable modules and if the rule belongs to such a module.
If the rule was imported or moved, check the Action of the rule if the referenced module is valid.
Check the Action Execution in the Process App The Rules Engine and Schedule Engine expose rule and job execution to the Action Monitor so that the execution of actions triggered by rules and scheduled jobs can be monitored in the Process app.
Consider reviewing the following:
- Is the corresponding rule type shown in the Process app or not?
- Is the specific rule shown in the Process app or not?
15 Using the Rules Editor App Is the corresponding action type shown in the Process app or not?
- Is the specific action shown in the Process app or not?
- Is the action execution "hanging" (stays in status Created, Pending or Running for a long time)?
- See "Monitoring Platform Automation Actions in the Process App" on page 546.
Such issues might indicate that the corresponding Action Engine is not running. See "Check for Incorrect or not Working Services" below.
Check Events Consider reviewing the following:
The rule has not been executed because no change events to trigger the rule are being created.
- Check the Kafka topic "avid.changes" if change events are being created. See "Checking Kafka
Topics for Troubleshooting" on page 470.
Check the log files of the related Change Event Agents. See "Upstream Source System Services"
- below.
- For Production Management and Asset Management rules: Check the mode of the Change Event
Agent (platform service avid-change-event-agent-search), which recognizes events and creates entries in the Kafka topic avid.changes. The Rules Engine triggers actions based on those entries. By default, the Change Event Agent operates in “send all” mode.
For Production Management and Asset Management rules: Check if the Search is working properly:
- For example, if your rule is triggered by the creation of an entity, go to the MediaCentral UX Search
app and submit a search with filter “Created” on the respective Production Management/Asset Management system. Then, check if the entity that triggers your rule show up in the search results.
Check for Incorrect or not Working Services Possibly a relevant service in the “messaging chain” is not working properly and prevents the execution of the respective action. To resolve this issue, review the following.
Rules Engine and Respective Action Engines Are the services (avid-platform-rulesengine, avid-platform-ae-*) up and running?
- Check their status in Kubernetes Dashboard. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for
Troubleshooting" on page 467.
- Are the services connected to Kafka?
If there are issues, normally you will see this in the log files. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 467.
Alternatively, the connectivity status can also be viewed by using a Kafka Client.
Upstream Source System Services Are the services up and running?
- Check their status in Kubernetes Dashboard. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for
Troubleshooting" on page 467.
Are the services connected to Kafka?
- If there are issues, normally you will see this in the log files. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for
Troubleshooting" on page 467.
Alternatively, the connectivity status can also be viewed by using a Kafka Client.
15 Using the Rules Editor App For Production Management and Asset Management: Check the configuration of the source system
- for search connection and the MediaCentral platform service avid-change-event-agent.
  - Asset Management service SyncCentralIndex: This is a service running on the Asset
Management host, not on the MediaCentral Platform. Publishes information about entity changes to the search topic avid.search.interplay--mam.<SYSTEM-ID>.
  - Production Management: platform service avid-pam-search-connector-production: Publishes
information about entity changes to the search topic avid.search.interplay--pam.<SYSTEM-ID>
  - Platform Service avid-change-event-agent-search: Transforms and republishes the event to
the Kafka topic avid.changes. Operates by default in “send all” mode.
Can be controlled by adding the configMap setting AVID_CHANGE_EVENT_AGENT_SEND_ ONLY_PHONETIC [true|false].
Using the Process App for Monitoring Platform Automation Actions The Rules Engine and Schedule Engine expose rule and job execution to the Action Monitor so that the execution of actions triggered by rules and scheduled jobs can be monitored in the Process app.
The Action Monitor does the following:
Reads action commands from topic "avid.actionengine.commands" and publish these commands
- with status “pending”.
Reads action status from topic "avid.actionengine.status" and
  - updates commands with “in progress”, when starting with the action processing.
  - updates commands with final state “completed” or “failed”, when done.
Reports the action status to the Job Monitor (Process app backend) so that actions become visible in
- the Process app.
In the Process app, the following are shown:
- The execution of rules
- The execution of scheduled jobs (when they were triggered)
- Actions triggered by rules and scheduled jobs
Use the Process app to monitor the execution of platform automation actions. See the following topic:
"Monitoring Platform Automation Actions in the Process App" below
- The Process additionally provides options for manual and scheduled retries of failed platform automation
actions. See the following topic:
Monitoring Platform Automation Actions in the Process App Use the Process app to monitor the execution of platform automation actions, for example, to check if an action is shown or the action execution is "hanging".
> **Note:** Only administrators or users of a group with Master of Jobs entitlement can monitor the execution of
actions triggered by rules and scheduled jobs in the Process app.
By default, processes and jobs of all types currently being used are shown as a list in the Process app. You can modify the Process List to show only rules, actions and scheduled jobs by using the following filters.
Note that all filters are cumulative.
15 Using the Rules Editor App Control Action Groups filter (sidebar) The following Groups filters can be used for monitoring the execution of individual rules, actions and scheduled jobs:
You can filter actions by applying the sidebar filter
- Groups > "Action: <action type>"
Example: "Action: am-orchestration" or "Action: ctms- addtofolder"
- You can filter rules by applying the sidebar filter
Groups > "Rule: <rule name>" You can filter scheduled jobs by applying the sidebar filter
- Groups > "Schedule: <scheduled job name>"
System filter (sidebar) The following System filter can be used for monitoring the execution of all rules, actions and scheduled jobs:
- Automation
Status filter (sidebar and toolbar) Use the Status filter to show only actions of a specific status (such as Running or Failed).
Show Hierarchy toggle (toolbar) To visualize the hierarchy of the actions triggered by a rule or scheduled job, select the Show Hierarchy toggle button in the toolbar.
The actions are shown as children of the triggering rule or scheduled job.
In the Process List, rules, actions and scheduled jobs are shown according to the following naming convention:
Type Naming convention Rules The name is provided in the following form: <rule name>:<entity in the form system type/asset type> - [trigger] Examples:
- My Rule1: wolftech-news/wtn-story - [Updated]
- My Rule2: interplay-pam/masterclip - [Created]
- My Rule3: interplay-mam/asset.VIDEO - [Deleted]
Scheduled jobs The name is provided in the following form: <scheduled job name (Job ID)> Example: My Job (66c2f22c-ea22-66b6-22d2-2b6fb6ac6b62) Actions The name is provided in the form <technical action type name> Example: Action: mediaanalytics Special cases:
- am-orchestration action names are appended with the name of the process that
is triggered. Example: am-orchestration/MyExportProcess If an action was triggered on the results of a Search entity in a scheduled job, an
- action "search" will be shown.
15 Using the Rules Editor App To monitor rules, actions and scheduled jobs in the Process app:
1. Click the Process button on the Fast Bar.
2. Open the Filters sidebar.
3. To show only rules, actions and scheduled jobs by using the "System" filter:
a. Expand the System list in the Filters sidebar if needed.
b. Select the Automation check box.
The Process app shows you only rules, actions triggered by rules/scheduled jobs and scheduled jobs triggered by the Schedule Engine.
4. To show only specific rules, actions or scheduled jobs by using the "Groups" filter:
a. Expand the Groups list in the Filters sidebar if needed.
b. Click the "show all" button.
c. In the break-out window that opens, select the rules, actions or scheduled jobs you want to display and then click the Apply button.
The Process app shows you only the corresponding items.
5. To group the displayed rules, actions, and scheduled jobs:
a. Click the Display Group button so it turns blue.
Rules, actions and scheduled jobs are grouped by their type in the Process List.
b. Click the Expand/Collapse toggle button of each type to show its items.
6. (optional) To visualize the hierarchy:
a. Click the Show Hierarchy button in the Process app toolbar so it turns blue.
Actions are arranged below their triggering rule or scheduled job in the Process List. If the action was triggered on search results by a scheduled job, the hierarchy is scheduled job > search > action.
b. Click the Expand button to the left side of a rule or scheduled job to show the triggered actions.
7. (optional) Apply additional filter criteria.
a. (Filters sidebar) To filter the shown items by creation time ranges, expand the Creation Time list and select one of the predefined time ranges.
b. (Filters sidebar and Toolbar) To show only items of a specific status, expand the Status list and select the status you want to show or click the corresponding Status button in the toolbar.
Multi-selection is supported. You can use the following filters:
Icon Status Created (Should be present only for a very short time) Pending (Should be present only for a very short time) Running (Should be present only for a very short time) Completed Failed 15 Using the Rules Editor App Retrying Failed Platform Automation Actions in the Process App You can use the Process app to not only monitor the execution of platform automation actions, but also to monitor and initiate retry actions for failed platform automation actions.
For failed platform automation actions, by default a Retry option is provided. Additionally, scheduled retry might by possible if configured in the Action Monitor config map. This configuration also defines what happens with scheduled retries after a manual retry has been applied. For more information, see "Configuring Long-Term Retry for Platform Automation Actions" on page 600.
In the Process app, the following controls are shown for retries:
Button Meaning Lets you retry a failed platform automation action.
The manual Retry button is shown for failed platform automation actions and after all scheduled retries have been executed for a failed action. When you click the Retry button it is disabled and turns blue for 5 seconds.
Indicates that an auto-retry is scheduled for the failed action.
The Name of each retried action is appended by the "<Retry [counter] (auto)>" suffix.
When you hover the mouse pointer over the auto-retry button, a tool-tip shows the scheduled retry date and time (format according to the time zone and time format set in your User Settings).
You can also click the auto-retry button to initiate a manual retry before the scheduled retry is executed. When you click the button it is disabled and turns blue for 5 seconds.
Lets you cancel a scheduled retry. If you cancel a scheduled retry action, the following happens:
- In the backend, the one time job is removed from the Schedule Engine and the Job Monitor
job is updated.
- In the Process app, the Auto-retry and Cancel buttons are removed, and the manual Retry
button is shown.
When you click the button it is disabled and turns blue for 5 seconds.
To use retry options in the Process app:
1. Click the Process button on the Fast Bar.
2. Apply filters as needed.
3. To initiate a manual retry, click the Retry button of the action.
4. To use the auto-retry:
- Monitor the auto-retry actions. In the following example, a long-term retry for the action
wtnews-createpublishingpoint (action label: Create Publishing Output) has been configured with 5 retries and a delay of 30 seonds.
15 Using the Rules Editor App When the last scheduled retry is executed, the Auto-retry button is replaced by the manual Retry button.
- To initiate a manual retry before the next scheduled retry is executed, click the Auto-retry
button.
A manual retry is triggered and the Auto-retry button is replaced by the manual Retry button.
- To cancel a scheduled retry, click the Cancel button to the right side of a Auto-retry button.
The Auto-retry and Cancel buttons are removed, and the manual Retry button is shown.
Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting Use the Kubernetes Dashboard to check if a platform automation component is deployed and running, and to monitor the log files.
Components The following Platform Automation components are used by the Rules Editor:
Name Feature Deployment (Kubernetes) Rules Engine rulesengine avid-platform-rulesengine-rulesengine Action Engines The following Action Engines are part of the Platform Automation and are used by the Rules Engine and Rules Editor:
Name Actions Feature Deployment (Kubernetes) Core1 Action Engine <none> ae-core <none> Action Engine AM- AM Orchestration ae-am- avid-platform-ae-am- Orchestration (am-orchestration) orchestration orchestration-ae-am- orchestration Action Engine CTMS Create Entity ae-ctms avid-platform-ae-ctms-ae- (ctms-createentity) ctms Update Metadata (ctms-updatemetadata) Create Folder 15 Using the Rules Editor App Name Actions Feature Deployment (Kubernetes) (ctms-createfolder) Add To Folder (ctms-addtofolder) Action Engine Media Media Analytics ae-mediaanalytics avid-platform-ae- Analytics (mediaanalytics) mediaanalytics-ae- mediaanalytics Action Engine Notification ae-notification avid-platform-ae-notification- Notification (platform-notification) ae-notification Action Engine PM- Send to Playback ae-pm-stp avid-platform-ae-pm-stp-ae- STP (pm-stp) pm-stp Action Engine Add to Story wolftech avid-platform-ae-wolftech- Wolftech News (wtnews-addtostory) news-wolftech Add to Publishing Output (wtnews- addtopublishingpoint) Create Publishing Output (wtnews- createpublishingpoint) Publish Publishing Output (wtnews-publish) Create Sequence (wtnews-createsequence) Create News Sequence (wtnews- createnewssequence) Send to Playback (wtnews-stp) Action Engine Publish publisher avid-platform-ae-publisher- Publisher (publish) ae-publisher Central settings for Action Engines. Is deployed with first deployment of any Action Engine.
Change Event Agents The following Change Event Agents are used by the Platform Automation:
Name Feature Deployment (Kubernetes) Change Event Agent Search rulesengine avid-change-event-agent-search-rulesengine Change Event Agent Wolftech wolftech avid-change-event-agent-wolftech-wolftech The following Kafka plugin is used to collect messages from AWS SQS and publish them to a dedicated Kafka topic where they are processed by the Change Event Agent Wolftech :
15 Using the Rules Editor App Name Feature Kubernetes Pod Wolftech Event Connector wolftech kafka-connect-camel-connect To use the Kubernetes Dashboard:
1. Log in to the Kubernetes Dashboard of the platform host.
For more information, see "Accessing the Kubernetes Dashboard" on page 626.
2. Search for the Deployment you want to check (see tables above).
3. Check if the Deployment appears in the list.
If the Component is not shown under Deployments, the component is probably not deployed on this host.
4. Check if a specific service component is running: Search for the name, then check if the Deployment
and it’s instances (Pods) are running.
5. Check the log file that contains details about a component: Search for the Component and look for a
matching pod to show it’s logfile.
For more information, see "Collecting Logs Through the Dashboard" on page 628.
6. If an error is shown for the pod, consider deleting the pod.
Kubernetes recognizes the change and initiates the creation of a new pod to take its place.
For more information, see "Deleting a Pod" on page 626.
7. If the Deployment is marked red with an error, consider doing one of the following:
15 Using the Rules Editor App
- Restart the component (deployment).
- Delete all pods of the component. Kubernetes will start new instances of the service (as many
as defined in the Deployment).
Make sure that you only delete the pods. Do not delete the Config Maps, Deployments, or other.
Checking Kafka Topics for Troubleshooting The Platform Automation is designed as an asynchronous, event driven, messaging system. Services are communicating by exchanging messages via Kafka.
For troubleshooting it can help to evaluate the messages populated to the respective Kafka topic. A Kafka client can be used for this purpose. The following Kafka topics are relevant in context of the Platform Automation.
wt.events For change events in Wolftech News, entries in an Amazon SQS (Simple Queue Service) queue are created.
The Kafka plug-in "Wolftech Event Connector"(Kubernetes pod name: kafka-connect-camel-connect) reads these Wolftech News change events from Amazon SQS and writes them to the Kafka topic “wt.events” where they can be read by the Change Event Agent Wolftech (see avid.changes below).
To check if messages are created in the AWS SQS queue, you can use the AWS web interface to access AWS SQS (consult your AWS administrator for details):
1. Login to the AWS web interface with a user who can access AWS SQS.
2. Select “Simple Queue Service”.
3. Select the queue which is used for Wolftech News events.
4. In the details page of the queue, click on “Send and receive massages”.
5. In the “Send and receive massages” page, click on “Poll for messages”.
Only messages which have not been picked up yet will show up here. In a system which is running correctly, the messages are picked up by the Wolftech Event Connector very quickly.
If you do not get any new message in the topic “wt.events”:
- Check if the Change Event Agent Wolftech is working correctly (Kubernetes deployment: avid-
change-event-agent-wolftech-wolftech).
- Check if the Wolftech Event Connector is working correctly: In the Kubernetes Dashboard, check if at
least one pod with the name "kafka-connect-camel-connect-<number>" is running. If the pod is showing an error (for example, because it cannot access Amazon SQS), check the pod's log and consider deleting the pod.
- Check if you see messages in the AWS SQS query, as described above.
  - You see messages: The Wolftech Event Connector does not pick them up, try to delete the pod
(s) "kafka-connect-camel-connect-<number>" in Kubernetes dashboard.
  - You see no messages: Wolftech News is not creating messages in this SQS queue.
- In error case, it might also be helpful to check entries in the topic "wt.events.changeeventagent.dlq"
into which the Change Event Agent Wolftech writes if processing did not succeed.
avid.changes Source systems, such as Asset Management or Production Management, are signalizing changes by populating a “change event” to the avid.changes topic.
15 Using the Rules Editor App For Wolftech News, the Change Event Agent Wolftech reads Kafka messages from the Kafka topic “wt.events”, filters the messages for entities that are supported in the automation (Story, Pitch, Publishing Output), converts "wt events" into automation change events and writes Kafka messages to the Kafka topic avid.changes.
The Rules Engine picks up these events, checks for matching rules and triggers a respective action by sending an event to the avid.actionengine.commands topic.
Evaluating messages of the avid.changes topic can help to troubleshoot in case an expected rule action is not executed.
If the respective change event is present, check the rule configuration and downstream services
- (Rules Engine, Action Engines).
- If the respective change event is not present, check upstream services (avid-change-event-agent-
search, Asset Management, Production Management; avid-change-event-agent-wolftech).
avid.actionengine.commands Action Engine services are processing relevant messages of this topic.
avid.actionengine.status Action Engine services are populating the current status of an action command being executed.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App 16 Using the Schedule Editor App ​in The Schedule Editor app allows system administrators to define jobs that trigger schedule-based actions MediaCentral Cloud UX. A schedule defines a situation in which an action gets triggered. It combines a trigger (schedule), an optional search (entity) and one or more actions.
The Schedule Editor supports two different types of scheduled jobs:
- Scheduled jobs that trigger action(s) for a configured time and interval (no assets attached)
- Scheduled jobs that trigger action(s) on assets found by a saved search for a configured time and
interval Using the Schedule Editor app, an administrator can create, edit, enable/disable, duplicate, export, import and delete scheduled jobs.
The Schedule Editor saves scheduled jobs to and retrieves saved jobs from the Schedule Engine. The Schedule Engine service evaluates the scheduled jobs and in case a scheduled job matches it invokes the action in the corresponding Action Engine, which will orchestrate the action.
> **Note:** In this release, a maximum of 500 scheduled jobs is supported.
Sample Use Cases Consider the following sample uses cases that you can accomplish with scheduled jobs defined in Schedule Editor.
- Create your daily folder "Daily Edits"
- Re-run failed media analytics job over night
- Trigger Asset Management orchestration processes
Required Feature Packs There is no separate feature pack for the Schedule Engine and Schedule Editor App deployment:
The Schedule Engine is installed as core feature of the MediaCentral Cloud UX setup.
- The Schedule Editor app is a core administration app and deployed with the MediaCentral Cloud UX
- core app.
To use actions with scheduled jobs, Action Engines are required. Most Action Engines are installed as core features of the MediaCentral Cloud UX setup. Only a few optional Action Engines are installed with the MediaCentral Cloud UX Feature Packs ISO (mediacentral_feature_packs_<version>.iso). During the installation of the Feature Packs ISO, confirm the installation of the following optional feature packs if needed:
- ae-pm-stp (required if you want to use Send to Playback actions for Production Management assets)
- ae-am-orchestration (only required if you want to use AM Orchestration actions)
- publisher (required if you want to use Publish actions for Production Management or Asset
Management assets) wolftech (required if you want to use certain actions – AM Orchestration, Create Entity, Update
- Metadata – with Wolftech News assets)
See also "Running the Feature Pack Deployment Script" on page 134.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Prerequisites Before you start creating scheduled jobs in Schedules Editor, check the following prerequisites for action types:
Action Type Prerequisite Action Send to If you want to use Actions of type Send to Playback, Send to Playback profiles must have Playback been created for your Production Management system in the Workflow Settings app, Send to Playback section. For more information, see "Defining a Send to Playback Action" on page 516.
Action AM If you want to use Actions of type AM Orchestration, an administrator first must create Orchestration action specifications – specific Action templates for processes – in Asset Management and "Defining an AM Orchestration Action" on page 507.
Action Media If you want to use Actions of type Media Analytics the following are required:
Analytics
- Media Analytics Engine (feature pack: mediaanalytics-engine)
- Media Analytics Gateway (feature pack: mediaanalytics-gateway)
- An Analytics Provider. In this release, Avid Ada is the only supported analytics
provider (feature pack: Avid Ada (ml). Requires also the Speech To Text (stt) feature pack.
For more information, see "Avid Ada Transcribe" on page 30.
Action Publish If you want to use Actions of type Publish, an administrator first must do the following:
- Install the feature pack publisher and configure Publisher Settings. See "Configuring
the Publisher Settings" on page 229.
- Configure the credentials to be used to communicate with the Publisher backend.
See "Configuring Publisher Automation" on page 122.
- Configure a publisher automation setting in the Configuration Settings app, section
Publisher Automation. For more information, see "Configuring the Publisher Automation Setting" on page 231.
Schedules Processing: Included Back-End Components and Workflow Similar to the Rules Engine, the Schedule Engine triggers execution of Actions in Action Engines based on matching entities. The main difference is that entities are queried via MediaCentral Search from underlying systems (such as Production Management) based on schedules. In this way, search criteria act as conditions to select applicable entities.
For each found entity, the Schedule Engine triggers the execution of configured Actions. For this purpose Schedule Engine reads the Action Configuration as part of Schedule Engine job configuration, resolves expressions, and sends a corresponding Action Message to the responsible Action Engine via Kafka topic.
For more information, see "Actions: Configuration and Back-End Workflow" on page 503.
Depending on provided search criteria, MediaCentral Search may return multiple entities from different systems which may get processed in one go by triggered Actions. This opens great flexibility but also requires care when designing the search criteria in order to return only desired entities from supported systems so that the configured Actions can be processed appropriately.
For efficiency, the Schedule Engine tries to apply more than one entity to a single action execution. If and how many entities can be executed by a single Action is determined by the Action configuration (min/maxEntities parameters, can be shown in the Advanced properties of an action). Therefore, Schedule Engine might also trigger a single action execution for each found entity.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Note that some actions do not require an entity for execution at all. In this case just a scheduled job with a trigger is required without searching for entities.
Scheduled jobs support one-time triggers and triggers with various recurring patterns. Also a single Schedule Engine job may be configured with multiple triggers.
Accessing the Schedules Editor App You can access the Schedules Editor app through the administrator settings. For more information on accessing the administrator settings, see "Administrator App Overview" on page 143. For information on the Schedules Editor app layout, see "Understanding the Schedule Editor App Layout" below.
Creating a Schedule: Basic Steps The following procedure summarizes the main steps required for creating a scheduled job.
How to create a scheduled job:
1. In the Jobs panel, click the Add new job button.
2. Add a name for the scheduled job in the Details panel.
3. Add one or more triggers.
4. Define the trigger parameters.
5. (optional) Add an entity (search) and define the search parameters.
6. Add one or more Actions.
7. Define the parameters for the action(s).
8. Enable the scheduled job in the Details panel.
9. Save the scheduled job.
For more information, see the following topics:
- Understanding the Schedule Editor App Layout
The Schedule Editor app is divided between four primary areas, as described in the following illustration and table.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Section Description 1 Schedule The header at the top of the app provides you with a health check of the back-end Editor components that enable the functionality in the Schedule Editor app. If MediaCentral Header Cloud UX determines that the component is performing as expected, the header shows a green label for the component. If a problem is detected, the component might appear as yellow or red, depending on the severity of the issue. For more information, see "Reviewing Schedule Editor App Status Information" on page 487.
The Schedule Engine pill can also be used for pausing and resuming event processing in the Schedule Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Schedule Engine Job Processing" on page 487.
2 Jobs Information on the Jobs panel is arranged in two tabs:
- JOBS: Shows all created scheduled jobs in an overview table. Provides controls
to create new scheduled jobs as well as import, export and delete existing scheduled jobs.
- CONFIGURATION: Shows the items that can be added to a scheduled jobs
arranged in three sections: Trigger, Entities, Actions. The CONFIGURATION tab is automatically opened when you create a scheduled job, double-click on a scheduled job in the JOBS tab, or click on a placeholder in the Define Job panel.
For more information, see "The Jobs Panel" on the next page.
3 Define Job The Define Job area lets you add the items to the scheduled job: one or more triggers, one entity (search), and one or more actions. For more information, see "The Define Job Panel" on page 480.
4 Details This section of the app provides basic about the scheduled job and additional information about the item selected in the Define Job panel. This area allows an administrator to define the job name, enable and disable the scheduled job and to 16 Using the Schedule Editor App Section Description define search parameters and properties of actions. For more information, see "The Details Panel" on page 482.
The Jobs Panel Information on the Jobs panel is arranged in two tabs:
- JOBS: Shows all created scheduled jobs in an overview table. Use this tab to get an overview over the
existing scheduled jobs, create a new scheduled job, import and export scheduled jobs, delete existing scheduled jobs, and select a scheduled job for editing. See "Layout of the JOBS Tab" below.
After initial deployment, the JOBS tab is empty and shows the following message.
> **Note:** The Import Default Jobs feature is not functional yet and reserved for future use.
- CONFIGURATION: Shows the items that can be added to a scheduled job. The Items tab is
automatically opened when you create a scheduled job, double-click on a scheduled job in the JOBS tab, or click on a placeholder in the Define Job panel. It can also be accessed after selecting a scheduled job in the JOBS tab. See "Layout of the CONFIGURATION Tab" on page 479.
Layout of the JOBS Tab The JOBS tab displays all scheduled jobs that have been added to the Schedule Editor app in an alpha- numerically sorted list. You can click on any of the scheduled jobs to obtain more information about the scheduled job and its items and to edit the scheduled job. The JOBS tab provides also controls to create, import, export and delete scheduled jobs and to filter the scheduled jobs list — allowing you to quickly and easily identify a specific scheduled job. The following table lists the controls and information shown in the JOBS tab.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Item Description 1 Find a job The "Find a job" filter option allows you to enter a custom value to limit the Schedules list to show only those scheduled jobs that include your filter text.
See ."Filtering and Sorting the Schedules List" on the next page.
2 Import jobs The button lets you import previously exported scheduled jobs. See "Importing Scheduled Jobs" on page 530.
3 Export all jobs The button lets you export all scheduled jobs. See "Exporting Scheduled Jobs" on page 529.
4 Add new job The Add new job button allows you to create a new scheduled job. When you click the button the Jobs panel switches to the JOBS tab. See "Creating a Scheduled Job" on page 488.
5 Menu button Provides the Import Default Jobs option that lets you import default scheduled jobs.
> **Note:** The Import Default Jobs feature is not functional yet and reserved for
future use.
6 Warning icon A warning icon is shown to the left side of a scheduled job if the scheduled job is missing a trigger or action. When you hover your cursor over the icon, a tool-tip shows the missing item(s). See "Jobs Panel: JOBS Tab" on page 533.
7 Name Name of the scheduled job. When you hover the cursor over the scheduled job name a tool-tip displays basic scheduled job properties, as shown in the following example.
The same information is available in the Additional information section in the Details panel.
8 Trigger The number of triggers assigned to the scheduled job. The following triggers are available: Basic, One Time, and Advanced (CRON).
See "Trigger" on page 479.
9 Search (optional) Indicates if a search is assigned to the schedule. Only one search entity can be assigned to a scheduled job.
See "(Optional) Defining a Search Entity" on page 498.
10 Action The Action assigned to the scheduled job. The following actions are available:
AM Orchestration Send to Playback Create Entity Update Entity Create Folder Add to Folder 16 Using the Schedule Editor App Item Description Notification Media Analytics Publish You can assign a maximum of 10 actions to a scheduled job. When different actions are assigned to a scheduled job, the following icon is shown in the Actions column:
Icon indicating mixed action types When you hover the cursor over an action icon a tool-tip displays the name(s) of the assigned action(s).
11 Enabled Indicates if the scheduled job is enabled or not. If the green check mark icon is not shown, the scheduled job is disabled. You enable and disabled a scheduled job in the Details panel.
12 Modification Date Shows the date and time when the scheduled job was last modified.
13 Delete button Lets you delete the scheduled job. The Delete rule button is only shown when you hover the cursor over the scheduled job in the table. See "Deleting Scheduled Jobs" on page 532.
Information in the JOBS tab is updated when you save a new scheduled job, save changes made to an existing scheduled job, import scheduled jobs or delete a scheduled job.
Filtering and Sorting the Schedules List The toolbar at the top of the JOBS tab includes controls that allow you to execute certain actions that relate to the JOBS tab.
To sort the Jobs list:
1. In the Schedule Editor's JOBS tab, click on a column header to reorder the Jobs list. The warning icon
column does not support sorting.
An up arrow appears to the right of the column name to indicate that this column is used to sort the results in ascending order.
2. (optional) When you click on a column header, the column is sorted in ascending order. If you need to
sort the list in descending order, simply click on the column header again to reverse the order of the list.
A down arrow appears to the right of the column name to indicate that this column is used to sort the results in descending order.
To filter the Jobs list, do the following:
- Enter a custom value in the Find a job field.
The list of jobs is filtered to include only those whose name include your filter text.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App In the following example, an administrator has filtered the list to include only the jobs that include “dem” in the name.
Layout of the CONFIGURATION Tab The CONFIGURATION tab displays the items that can be added to a schedule, arranged in tree sections:
- Trigger
- Entities
- Actions
All items in the CONFIGURATION tab are represented by a card showing an icon and name.
Trigger The Trigger section shows all triggers that can be added to the schedule.
The following illustration shows the available triggers.
When you click on a trigger card, the trigger is added to the schedule and shown in the Define Job panel under TRIGGER. You can add a maximum of 5 triggers to a schedule.
Entities The Entities section shows the entity (Search) that can be added to the schedule.
When you click on the entity card, the entity is added to the schedule and shown in the Define Job panel under ENTITY. You can add one Search entity to a schedule.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Actions The Actions section shows all actions that can be added to the schedule. When you click on an action card, the action is added to the schedule and shown in the Define Job panel under ACTION. You can add a maximum of 10 actions to a schedule. Note that several actions require the Search entity being added to the schedule.
Action Requires Search Entity Add to Folder yes AM Orchestration optional1 Create Entity no Create Folder no Media Analytics yes optional2 Notification Publish yes Send to Playback yes Update Metadata yes 1 Adding the Search entity is required when the process that will be started by the action requires attached assets ; if the process does not require attached assets, the Search entity is not needed.
Adding the Search entity is required if you want MediaCentral Cloud UX to open the default application for the referenced asset when a user clicks the notification message.
The following illustration shows the available actions.
For more information, see "The Details Panel" on page 482 and "Defining Actions" on page 502.
The Define Job Panel The Define Job panel is opened when you create a new scheduled job and when you select a scheduled job in the Jobs panel's JOBS tab. The Define Job panel lets you add items to and remove items from the scheduled job, export the scheduled job, duplicate and save the new or edited scheduled job.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App The Define Job tab when starting to create a scheduled job (left) and editing an existing scheduled job with unsaved changes (right) Item Description 1 Item When you create a new scheduled job, the Define Job panel provides placeholders placeholder for the items you can add to the scheduled job: TRIGGER, ENTITY, ACTION. You add an item to the Define Job panel by clicking on it in the CONFIGURATION tab. The placeholder is then replaced by the name of the added item; for TRIGGER and ACTION, an additional placeholder is displayed.
Once you added a Search entity to the Define Job panel, the ACTION header changes to ACTION ON SEARCH RESULTS.
