2 Warning icon For any added Action, a warning icon will appear. When you hover the cursor over the warning icon, a tool-tip informs you which required configuration step in the Details panel still needs to be done. See "Scheduled Jobs Validation" on page 533.
3 Selected item When you select an item in the Define Job panel its details (properties) are shown in the Details panel.
4 Hovered item If you want to replace an already added item, you first need to remove the initial with Trash icon item: hover the cursor over the item and click the Trash icon that is shown. After that, click on the item you want to add in the CONFIGURATION tab.
5 Buttons Execute operations on the scheduled job currently being created or edited.
- Duplicate: Lets you duplicate the scheduled job currently being open in the
Define Job and Details panels. The button is not activated until you have saved all changes in the scheduled job. For more information, see "Duplicating a Scheduled Job" on page 528.
Save: Click the Save button to save the scheduled job.
- Cancel: When you click Cancel the following happens:
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Item Description
  - A new scheduled job is discarded, the Define Job and Details panels
are cleared, and in the Jobs panel the JOBS tab opens.
  - For an edited scheduled job, your changes are discarded. The Define
Job and Details panels stay in edit mode.
6 Export job Lets you export the scheduled job currently being created or edit. The button is not button activated until you have saved all changes in the scheduled job. For more information, see "Exporting Scheduled Jobs" on page 529.
The Details Panel Information on the Details panel is arranged in two sections:
- Job: Basic schedule information which is always displayed. See "The Job Section" below.
- Items specific: Provides controls and information that are related to the item selected in the Define
Job panel. See "The Item-specific Section" on the next page.
The Job Section The Job section is positioned at top of the Details panel and is always shown for a scheduled job. Its contents does not depend on the item selected in the Define Job panel.
Item Description 1 Job Name Lets you define the name of the scheduled job.
2 Enable toggle Lets you enable and disable the scheduled job. By default, a new scheduled job is disabled.
3 Additional Provides basic information about the scheduled job: Job ID, Created on, Created information by, Modified on, and Modified by. This information is generated by the backend and cannot be edited. You can enter a Comment (for internal use in the Schedule Editor app) in this section. The Auto Delete toggle is reserved for future use.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Item Description The Additional information can be collapsed and expanded. By default, Additional information is collapsed.
The Item-specific Section The item-specific section of the Details panel is displayed below the Job section. It shows different controls and information depending on the item selected in the Define Job tab:
Trigger
- Entity (Search)
- Action
- Trigger
The Triggers section lets you define the parameters for the selected trigger. All trigger types share a common set of fields but some fields are specific to individual trigger types, as shown in the following table.
Field One Time Basic Advanced (CRON) x1 Start Time x x End Time - x x Time Zone - x x1 x1 x1 Misfire Policy x Description x x x x1 Run every <interval> - - x1 CRON expression - - Mandatory field For more information, see "Defining Triggers" on page 492.
Entity (Search) Adding a Search entity to a schedule is optional.
You do not add a Search entity for scheduled jobs that trigger action(s) for a configured time and
- interval (no assets attached).
You must add a Search entity if the scheduled job should trigger action(s) on assets found by a saved
- search for a configured time and interval.
The Search section lets you define the parameters for the search.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Item Description 1 Section Header Shows "Search" to the left and might show a warning icon on the right side (a tool-tip shows the reason for the warning; for example, no search is defined yet). The Advanced toggle button lets you show an advanced setting to modify the default impersonation of the Search entity.
2 Size limit controls The spin boxes let you apply limitations to the number of search results and batch processing.
- Max hits of search: Maximum number of hits in the search results, when
the job is triggered (default: 100).
- Max. batch size: Maximum number of hits (assets) that will be attached
to a single action (default 50); if max. batch is smaller than max. hits of search, new actions will be triggered until all hits are processed.
3 Define a search/ Clicking the card opens the MediaCentral Search in a new window where you Modify the search can define and save a search or modify your saved search. As long as no card search is defined, the card reads "Define a search" and shows a dotted frame; once you save a search, the card reads "Modify the search" and shows a solid frame.
For more information, see "(Optional) Defining a Search Entity" on page 498.
Actions The action-specific section of the Details panel lets you define the parameters of the action to be executed, for example, an STP job, an Asset Management process, or a notification.
Which fields are shown is determined by the selected action specification. The Schedule Editor uses the action specification to generate the layout for a specific action. For more information, see "Action Specifications" on page 504. If provided by the action specification, a tool-tip might display a description for individual properties.
In error cases – for example, the Action Engine is not available or loading of the action specification fails – new actions cannot be configured, existing action data of the scheduled job are shown in read-only mode.
The following illustration shows examples for actions of type Send to Playback (left) and Notification (right).
Item Description 1 Header Shows the name of the action to the left and might show a warning icon on the right side (a tool-tip shows the reason for the warning).
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Item Description For many actions, you can click the Advanced toggle to show controls to configure the identity impersonation for action execution. For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 519.
The Advanced toggle also shows the Min. Number of Entities and Max. Number of Entities defined in the Action Specification. These settings specify how many entities can be processed min/max by a single ActionCommand and cannot be changed. For actions that include a System or Specification property or both, you first have to select these properties before the Min. Number of Entities and Max. Number of Entities fields can be shown by the Advanced toggle.
2 <Action- Add to Folder specific
- System: List of connected Production Management and Asset Management
fields> systems (mandatory).
- Folder: The folder to which the entity is to be added (mandatory).
- (optional) Advanced: Identity impersonation controls.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Folder Action" on page 504.
AM Orchestration System: List of available Asset Management Orchestration Engines
- (mandatory).
- Specification: List of available action specifications for the selected
Orchestration Engine (mandatory).
- Details (template) of the selected action specification. Which fields and controls
are shown and are editable depends on the action specification configuration in the Datamodel Administrator.
(optional) Advanced: Identity impersonation controls.
- For more information, see "Defining an AM Orchestration Action" on page 507.
Create Entity
- System: List of connected systems (Asset Management, Wolftech News)
(mandatory).
- Entity Type: List of entity types defined in the data model of the selected system
(Asset Management, Wolftech News) (mandatory).
Entity Name: The name of the entity to be created (mandatory).
- (optional): Properties added by using the Add/Remove attributes picker tool and
- provided values to be set on the created entity.
- (optional) Advanced: Identity impersonation controls.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Entity Action" on page 508.
Create Folder
- System: List of connected Production Management and Asset Management
systems (mandatory).
Parent Folder: Lets you select the parent folder in which the folder is to be
- created (mandatory).
- Folder Name: Name of the folder to be created (mandatory).
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Item Description
- (optional) Advanced: Identity impersonation controls.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Folder Action" on page 510.
Media Analytics
- Specification: The analytics provider, in this release only Avid Ada is supported
(mandatory).
- Features: In this release, only the feature Transcribe is supported (mandatory).
- Title: The title of the main Media Analytics job to be shown in the MediaCentral
Cloud UX Process app.
- Tracks: The audio tracks that are to be analyzed.
- Language: Reserved for future use.
- Result Name: The name of the transcript to be shown in the Transcript tab.
For more information, see "Defining a Media Analytics Action" on page 512.
Notification
- Title: Title that is to be shown for the notification (mandatory).
- Message: An optional message text that is to be displayed.
- Destinations: The user groups that should receive the notification (mandatory).
For more information, see "Defining a Notification Action" on page 514.
Publish
- Name: The title of the clip in Publisher.
- Publish Template: The publish template to be used.
- Publish clip when done: Defines if the clip is published after it is imported.
For more information, see "Defining a Publish Action" on page 515.
Send to Playback
- System: List of available STP Engines (mandatory).
- Profile: List of available STP profiles for the selected STP Engine (mandatory).
- Details of the selected STP profile. Which fields and controls are editable
depends on the actions specification.
- (optional) Advanced: Identity impersonation controls.
For more information, see "Defining a Send to Playback Action" on page 516.
Update Metadata System: List of connected systems (Production Management, Asset
- Management, Wolftech News) (mandatory).
- (optional): Properties added by using the Add/Remove attributes picker tool and
provided values to be updated on the entity.
- (optional) Advanced: Identity impersonation controls.
For more information, see "Defining an Update Metadata Action" on page 517.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Pausing and Resuming Schedule Engine Job Processing When you create, edit or import scheduled jobs, it might be useful that you temporarily pause the job processing in the Schedule Engine; for example, when you import a large number of scheduled jobs with "Enabled" status. This helps you to avoid unintended effects on your workflows because of missing or unintended scheduled job triggering and failed or unintended action execution.
Use the Schedule Engine pill in the Schedule Editor app header to stop and resume job processing in the Schedule Engine.
To pause and resume job processing in the Schedule Engine:
1. In the Schedule Editor app header, hover the cursor over the Schedule Engine pill.
A tool-tip opens.
2. Click the Pause Schedule Engine processing button in the tool-tip.
Job processing in the Schedule Engine is stopped. The color of the Schedule Engine pill switches to orange. In the tool-tip, the Resume Schedule Engine processing button is shown.
3. To resume job processing in the Schedule Engine, hover the cursor over the orange Schedule Engine
pill.
4. Click the Resume Schedule Engine processing button in the tool-tip.
Job processing in the Schedule Engine is resumed. The color of the Schedule Engine pill switches to green.
Reviewing Schedule Editor App Status Information The header at the top of the Schedule Editor app provides you with a health check of the back-end components that enable the functionality in the Schedule Editor app. If MediaCentral Cloud UX determines that the component is performing as expected, the header shows a green pill for the component. If a problem is detected, the component might appear as yellow or red, depending on the severity of the issue.
When status data is retrieved from the backend – you open the Schedule Editor app – a component might turn gray for a short period of time.
As shown in the following illustration, each component allows you to obtain additional status information by hovering your cursor over the health indicator. In the event of a warning or error, this tool-tip might help you identify the source of the problem. For more information, see "Issues Indicated by Schedule Editor Health Indicators" on page 538.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App The Schedule Engine pill can also be used for pausing and resuming job processing in the Schedule Engine.
See "Pausing and Resuming Schedule Engine Job Processing" on the previous page.
Component Description During normal operation, this health indicator should report that the “Schedule Engine is running”.
Job processing in the Schedule Engine has been paused.
The Schedule Engine health indicator turns red when the Schedule Engine is not accessible.
During normal operation, this health indicator should report that the “Action Engine Search is running”.
The Action Engine Search health indicator turns red when the Action Engine Search is not accessible.
Configuring scheduled jobs is still possible but any action that requires the Search entity will fail.
During normal operation, this health indicator should only show the connected Action Types without additional message.
The Action Engines health indicator turns yellow if one of the registered Action Engines is in error state.
The Action Engines health indicator turns red in the following cases:
The Schedule Engine is not accessible and thus cannot provide information on
- the registered Action Engines.
- The Schedule Engine is accessible but Action Types cannot be retrieved.
To update the health check information:
- Re-open the Schedule Editor app.
Creating a Scheduled Job During the scheduled job creation process, you will add multiple items to the scheduled job and configure property values. You can choose to either add items one at a time, configuring the properties for each as you add them, or you can add all of your items first and then configure the properties as a second step.
To create a scheduled job:
1. Click the Schedule Editor button in the Administrator Fast Bar.
2. (optional) Temporarily stop job processing in the Schedule Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming
Schedule Engine Job Processing" on the previous page.
3. In the Jobs panel, click the Add new job button.
The Jobs panel switches to the CONFIGURATION tab. The Define Job panel shows placeholders for the items you can add to the scheduled job. The Details panel shows a few basic controls.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
4. Add a name for the scheduled job: In the Details panel, replace the placeholder "New Job" in the Job
Name field.
Scheduled job names need to be unique. If you enter a name that is already used for another scheduled job, the name will be appended with a counter in brackets when you save the scheduled job.
5. (optional) Click the Expand toggle button in front of Additional information and do the following:
- Enter a comment in the Comment text box. The comment is only used within the Schedule
Editor app.
- (Reserved for future use) Change the status of the Auto Delete toggle button.
6. Add one or more Triggers: In the Jobs panel's CONFIGURATION tab, click the trigger you want to
add: One Time, Basic or Advanced (CRON). You can add up to 5 triggers.
7. Define trigger properties.
a. In the Define Job Panel, select the trigger.
b. In the Detail panel, provide information at least for the mandatory fields:
- One Time: Start Time
- Basic: Misfire Policy and Run every fields
- Advanced (CRON): Cron expression, Time Zone and Misfire Policy
For more information, see "Defining Triggers" on page 492.
8. (optional) Add one entity: In the Jobs panel's CONFIGURATION tab, click the Search entity.
9. Define the Search properties.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App a. In the Define Job panel, select the entity Search.
b. In the Details panel, select the "Define a search" card.
c. In the Search window that opens, define search settings and click OK.
d. (optional) In the Details panel, change the default attachment number settings (change the default values for "Max hits of search" and "Max. batch size" if needed).
For more information, see "(Optional) Defining a Search Entity" on page 498.
10. Add one or more Actions: In the Jobs panel's CONFIGURATION tab, click the action you want to add.
You can add up to 10 actions.
11. Define the parameters for an Add to Folder action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Add To Folder.
b. In the Details panel, select a Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select a folder from the Folder list.
d. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Folder Action" on page 504.
12. Define the parameters for an AM Orchestration action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action AM Orchestration.
b. In the Details panel, select the Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select an action specification from the Specification list.
d. Specify values for the selected specification.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an AM Orchestration Action" on page 507.
13. Define the parameters for a Create Entity action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Create Entity.
b. In the Details panel, select an Asset Management system or Wolftech News from the System list.
c. Select an entity type from the Entity Type list.
d. Type the name of the entity to be created in the Entity Name field.
e. (optional) Click the Add/Remove attributes button, select the properties to be added from the properties picker tool and provide values for the added properties.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Entity Action" on page 508.
14. Define the parameters for a Create Folder action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Create Folder.
b. In the Details panel, select a Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select the parent folder from the Parent Folder list.
d. Type the name of the folder to be created in the Folder Name field.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Folder Action" on page 510.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
15. Define the parameters for a Media Analytics action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Media Analytics.
b. In the Details panel, select an analytics provider from the Specification list. In this release, only Avid Ada is supported.
c. Select a feature from the Features list. In this release, only Transcribe is supported.
d. (optional) Define the title of the Media Analytics job to be shown in the MediaCentral Cloud UX Process app by using the Title field (type Expression).
e. Select the audio tracks that are to be analyzed. Default is A1,A2.
f. (optional) Define the name of the transcript to be shown in the Transcript tab by entering a name in the Result Name field.
For more information, see "Defining a Media Analytics Action" on page 512.
16. Define the parameters for a Notification action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Notification.
b. In the Details panel, type a name in the Title field.
c. (optional) Type a message text in the Message field.
d. Select one or more Platform User Management groups which should receive the notification from the Destinations list.
For more information, see "Defining a Notification Action" on page 514.
17. Define the parameters for a Publish action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Publish.
b. Provide the title of the clip to be created in Publisher.
c. Select the publish template to be used from the Publish Template list.
d. (optional) Change the status of the "Publish clip when done" toggle.
For more information, see "Defining a Publish Action" on page 515.
18. Define the parameters for a Send to Playback action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Send to Playback.
b. In the Details panel, select the Production Management module from the System list.
c. Select an STP profile from the Profile list.
d. (optional) If you do not want to keep the default Process Name, replace the default entry Action-PM.STP. This is the name that will be shown for the process in the Process app.
e. (optional) Enable the Burn Graphics/High Priority/Overwrite toggles as needed.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Send to Playback Action" on page 516.
19. Define the parameters for an Update Metadata action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Update Metadata.
b. In the Details panel, select a Production Management or Asset Management system or Wolftech News from the System list.
c. (optional) Click the Add/Remove attributes button, select the properties to be added from the properties picker tool and provide the values to be updated.
d. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Update Metadata Action" on page 517.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
20. Enable the schedule: In the Details panel, click the gray Enable toggle so it turns blue.
21. Click the Save button to save the schedule.
When you select another scheduled job in the Jobs panel or use the Add new job button while you are editing a scheduled job and have not saved your changes yet, you are prompted to save or discard the changes.
Do one of the following:
- Click Discard all changes.
- Click Save to save all changes.
When you save the scheduled job it is validated and parsed. If issues are detected, the results of the validation are summarized in a Validation dialog box. Validation issues are shown as warnings, parsing issues are shown as "incomplete".
Do one of the following:
- Click Cancel and resolve the listed issues before saving again.
- Click Save Anyway to save the scheduled job with validation issues. Items shown as
incomplete, are completely removed from the scheduled job.
22. (if paused) Resume job processing in the Schedule Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Schedule
Engine Job Processing" on page 487.
Defining Triggers When configuring a scheduled job, you need to define at least one trigger. A trigger is a schedule for when the configured action of the job is to be started. You can add a maximum of 5 triggers to a scheduled job.
The Schedule Editor provides the following trigger types:
- One Time: Can be used to specify a trigger that is executed once at a specified date.
- Basic: Can be used to specify the frequency of the scheduled job execution by using predefined time
values.
Advanced (CRON): Can be used to define specific points in time for job execution by using a Cron
- expression.
All trigger types share a common set of fields but some fields are specific to individual trigger types, as shown in the following table.
Field One Time Basic Advanced (CRON) Start Time x1 x x End Time - x x Time Zone - x x1 Misfire Policy x x1 x1 16 Using the Schedule Editor App Field One Time Basic Advanced (CRON) Description x x x x1 Run every <interval> - - x1 CRON expression - - Mandatory field For more information, see the following topics:
"Defining an One Time Trigger" below
- "Defining a Basic Trigger" on the next page
- Defining an One Time Trigger
Define an One Time trigger if the scheduled job is to be executed once at a specified date. Use One Time triggers for administrative tasks, or to test a scheduled job by execution it once or to send a notification to (all) user groups.
As soon as you modify the Start Time setting, information on the Next Execution Time(s) is shown below the configuration controls. The execution is in the user time zone, which is shown in brackets to the right side of Next Execution Time(s) and in the tool-tip. The user time zone is defined in the User Settings of the user; if no specific time zone is defined in the User Settings, the time zone of the local browser is used.
To define an One Time trigger:
1. In the Define Job panel, select a One Time trigger.
In the Details panel, the Trigger section is shown.
2. Provide the time when job execution is to be started in the Start Time (<user time zone>) field. The
format is DD/MM/YYYY hh:mm.
a. Click on a DD/MM/YYYY value and select a date from the calendar picktool that opens.
b. (optional) Type the hh:mm value. If you do not provide this value, 00:00 will be used.
Note that the start time is set in the user time zone. The user time zone is defined in the User Settings of the user.
seconds or more.
Select one of the following values from the Misfire Policy list:
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
- Do nothing: The misfire situation is ignored and the missed job execution is not triggered again.
This is the default value.
- Fire once: The underlying scheduler tries to trigger the execution of missed jobs once, and then
continues with the next configured execution times.
- Strict: The underlying scheduler does not try to evaluate the trigger for a misfire situation but
just tries to fire it as soon as possible, and continues with the next configured execution times.
All missed "firings“ will be fetched later as soon as possible, which might result in a high amount of triggered executions within a short period of time.
4. (optional) Enter a description in the Description text box. The description is only used within the
Schedule Editor app.
Defining a Basic Trigger Use the Basic trigger option if you want to specify the frequency of the scheduled job execution by using predefined time values.
When you add the trigger, the Details pane will show information on the Next Execution Time(s) related to the "Run every" setting below the configuration controls. As soon as you modify the Run every or Start Time setting, the Next Execution Time(s) are modified accordingly. Once schedule job execution started, additional information on the Previous Execution Time is shown below the configuration controls.
Note the following for the shown time zones:
Time Zone (Trigger): This is the time zone to be used by the trigger.
- It is used as a reference to calculate the execution time of the job; for example, it is used to correct
daylight saving time changes. It is also used to identify the days of a week or dates in a month (if Run every Weeks or Run every Month is selected).
By default, the Time Zone (Trigger) is set to the user time zone of the User Settings. You can change the used time zone for the trigger.
- Start Time (<User Time Zone>) and End Time (<User Time Zone>) of the trigger:
The start time and end time are in the user time zone. The user time zone is defined in the User Settings of the user; if no specific time zone is set in the User Settings, the time zone of the local browser is used. You cannot change the user time zone in the trigger.
Previous Execution Time (<User Time Zone>) and Next Execution Time (<User Time Zone>): The execution
- is in the user time zone.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App To define a Basic trigger:
1. In the Define Job panel, select a Basic trigger.
In the Details panel, the Trigger section is shown.
2. Use the "Run every" controls to define the frequency of the scheduled job execution. By default, once
per hour is selected.
a. Use the Up/Down arrows to define the Run every value. Valid values are 1-99.
b. Select a time value from the list: Minutes, Hours, Days, Weeks or Months.
- If you select Weeks, the days of the week appear.
If you want to repeat job execution on specific days of the week (or every day), click the days of the week you want to repeat job execution. The day(s) will appear highlighted as shown below.
- If you select Months, additional fields appear.
In the Run every <number> Months field, keep the default of 1 if you want job execution to repeat every month. Otherwise, change it to reflect the frequency (such as 2 for every two months, 3 for every three months, etc.). Valid values are 1 through 99.
Do one of the following:
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
- If you want the recording to repeat on a specific day, click the On day option
button, and select the day of the month (1 through 31) that you want the job execution to repeat.
- If you want more specific scheduling (for example, you want the job to run on the
last Wednesday of the month), click the On option button, then select the appropriate option from the list (First, Second, Third, Fourth, Fifth or Last), followed by the day of the week (for example, Wednesday).
3. Define the time zone to be used for the trigger: Select a value from the Time Zone (Trigger) list.
By default, the time zone defined in the User Settings is preselected in the Time Zone list.
4. Provide the time when job execution is to be started in the Start Time (<user time zone>) field. The
format is DD/MM/YYYY hh:mm.
a. Click on a DD/MM/YYYY value and select a date from the calendar picktool that opens.
b. (optional) Type the hh:mm value. If you do not provide this value, 00:00 will be used.
Note that the start time is set in the user time zone. The user time zone is defined in the User Settings of the user.
5. Provide the time when job execution ends in the End Time (<user time zone>) field. The format is
DD/MM/YYYY hh:mm.
a. Click on a DD/MM/YYYY value and select a date from the calendar picktool that opens.
b. (optional) Type the hh:mm value. If you do not provide this value, 00:00 will be used.
Note that the end time is set in the user time zone. The user time zone is defined in the User Settings of the user.
seconds or more.
Select one of the following values from the Misfire Policy list:
- Do nothing: The misfire situation is ignored and the missed job execution is not triggered again.
This is the default value.
- Fire once: The underlying scheduler tries to trigger the execution of missed jobs once, and then
continues with the next configured execution times.
- Strict: The underlying scheduler does not try to evaluate the trigger for a misfire situation but
just tries to fire it as soon as possible, and continues with the next configured execution times.
All missed "firings“ will be fetched later as soon as possible, which might result in a high amount of triggered executions within a short period of time.
7. (optional) Enter a description in the Description text box. The description is only used within the
Schedule Editor app.
Defining an Advanced (CRON) Trigger Use the Advanced (CRON) trigger option if you want to define specific points in time for job execution by using a Cron expression. A Cron expression is a string containing six or seven fields separated by blank spaces. Fields can contain values combined with special characters.
When you add the trigger, the Details pane will show information on the Next Execution Time(s) related to the "CRON expression" setting below the configuration controls. As soon as you modify the CRON expression or the Start Time setting, the Next Execution Time(s) are modified accordingly. Once schedule job execution started, additional information on the Previous Execution Time is shown below the configuration controls.
Note the following for the shown time zones:
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Time Zone (CRON expression): This is the time zone to be used by the CRON expression. By default,
- the CRON Expression time zone is set to the user time zone of the User Settings. You can change the
time zone for the CRON expression.
- Start Time (<User Time Zone>) and End Time (<User Time Zone>) of the trigger: The start time and end
time are set in the user time zone. The user time zone is defined in the User Settings of the user; if no specific time zone is set in the User Settings, the time zone of the local browser is used. You cannot change the user time zone in the trigger.
- Previous Execution Time (<User Time Zone>) and Next Execution Time (<User Time Zone>): The execution
is in the user time zone. As the time zone for Start Time/End Time, this is the time zone defined in the User Settings of the user.
To define an Advanced trigger:
1. In the Define Job panel, select an Advanced trigger.
In the Details panel, the Trigger section is shown.
2. To specify the point in time of the scheduled job execution, type a Cron expression in the CRON
expression text box.
The default Cron expression triggers job execution once a minute, at the beginning of the minute.
3. Define the time zone to be used by the CRON expression: Select a value from the Time Zone (CRON
Exp.) list.
This time zone can differ from the user time zone shown for Start Time/End Time. By default, the time zone defined in the User Settings is preselected the CRON (Exp.) list.
4. Provide the time when job execution is to be started in the Start Time (<user time zone>) field. The
format is DD/MM/YYYY hh:mm.
a. Click on a DD/MM/YYYY value and select a date from the calendar picktool that opens.
b. (optional) Type the hh:mm value. If you do not provide this value, 00:00 will be used.
Note that the start time is set in the user time zone. The user time zone is defined in the User Settings of the user.
5. Provide the time when job execution ends in the End Time (<user time zone>) field. The format is
DD/MM/YYYY hh:mm.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App a. Click on a DD/MM/YYYY value and select a date from the calendar picktool that opens.
b. (optional) Type the hh:mm value. If you do not provide this value, 00:00 will be used.
Note that the end time is set in the user time zone. The user time zone is defined in the User Settings of the user.
seconds or more.
Select one of the following values from the Misfire Policy list:
- Do nothing: The misfire situation is ignored and the missed job execution is not triggered again.
This is the default value.
- Fire once: The underlying scheduler tries to trigger the execution of missed jobs once, and then
continues with the next configured execution times.
- Strict: The underlying scheduler does not try to evaluate the trigger for a misfire situation but
just tries to fire it as soon as possible, and continues with the next configured execution times.
All missed "firings“ will be fetched later as soon as possible, which might result in a high amount of triggered executions within a short period of time.
7. (optional) Enter a description in the Description text box. The description is only used within the
Schedule Editor app.
(Optional) Defining a Search Entity Adding a Search entity to a schedule job is an optional step. The "attachment requirement" of the action you add to the scheduled job determines if you have to add a Search entity:
- You do not add a Search entity for scheduled jobs that trigger only action(s) for a configured time
and interval (no assets attached).
This applies to the following actions: Create Entity and Create Folder.
You must add a Search entity if the scheduled job is to trigger action(s) on assets found by a saved
- search for a configured time and interval.
This applies to the following actions: Add to Folder, Media Analytics, Publish, Send to Playback, and Update Metadata.
- Adding the Search entity is optional for the following actions:
  - AM Orchestration: Depending on the process that is triggered by the action, attached assets
might be required or not.
  - Notifications: Adding the Search entity is only required if you want MediaCentral Cloud UX to
open the default application for the referenced asset when a user clicks the notification message.
After you added the Search entity, you configure and save a search: When you click the Search entity in the Details panel, an instance of the MediaCentral Search app opens in a new window. The Action Engine Search will execute the saved search at the scheduled time and will perform the configured action(s) on the assets found by the search. For more information on the Search app, see the chapter "Searching for Assets" in the Avid MediaCentral Cloud UX User's Guide.
> **Note:** Each Action supports dedicated system types and asset types. To avoid misconfiguration, restrict the
search settings to the system and asset types supported by the action(s) you add to the scheduled job.
If you use more than one action in the scheduled job, the search results ideally only contain assets that are supported by all actions of the scheduled job. If you want to use actions that support different system and asset types, create separate scheduled jobs.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Action Search Settings - Restrict System to: Search Settings - Restrict Type to:
Add to Folder Production Management (local and Masterclip, Subclip, Sequence remote modules) Asset Management (local and remote <any> modules) Media Analytics Production Management (local module) Masterclip Publish Production Management (local module) Masterclip, Subclip, Sequence Asset Management (local module) Video-type assets, EDLs, Images Send to Playback Production Management (local module) Masterclip, Subclip, Sequence Update Metadata Wolftech News Story, Publishing Output, Pitch Production Management (local and Masterclip, Subclip, Sequence remote modules) Asset Management (local and remote <any> modules) To add and configure the Search entity:
1. In the Jobs panel's CONFIGURATION tab, click the Search entity.
2. In the Define Job panel, select the entity Search.
In the Details panel, the Search section is shown. The section header shows a warning because no search has been saved yet.
3. In the Details panel, select the Define a search card.
The Search app opens in a new window.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
4. Define search settings.
> **Note:** The search settings need to reflect the system and asset types supported by the action(s)
included in the scheduled job.
- (optional) Use advanced options to formulate your search, such as Recent Searches and
Search Favorites.
For more information, see the "Creating and Using Search Favorites" and "Using Recent Searches" in the Avid MediaCentral Cloud UX User's Guide.
- Restrict the systems to be included in the search: In the Search sidebar, go to the System
section and select the systems that are to be included in the search.
If the system is not shown, click the "show <number> more" link to show additional systems.
For more information, see the "Using Filters" in the Avid MediaCentral Cloud UX User's Guide.
- Restrict the asset type to be included in the search: In the Search sidebar, go to the Type
section and select the asset types that are to be included in the search.
If the required asset type is not shown, click the "show all" link and in the fly-out window that opens, select all required asset types and click the Apply icon.
- Enter a search term.
For more information, see the "Searching for Assets" and "Additional Search Options" in the Avid MediaCentral Cloud UX User's Guide.
5. Click OK.
All search settings are saved. The Search window is closed.
The warning icon is removed from the Search section header. The label of the "Define a search" card now reads "Modify the search".
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
6. (optional) Change the default attachment number settings.
  - Define the maximum number of hits in the search results when the job is triggered by entering
or selecting another value in the Max. hits of search field. Valid values are 1 through 1000; the default value is 100.
  - Define the maximum number of hits (assets) that will be attached to a single action by entering
or selecting another value in the Max. batch size field. Valid values are 1 through 100; the default value is 50.
How many actions will actually be triggered is determined by the Min. and Max. Number of Entities settings in the Action Specification of the action. See "Showing the Min. and Max.
Number of Entities Values" on page 520.
Example: The search finds 89 assets and Max batch size is "20".
  - If the configured action (such as AM Orchestration) allows up to 50 attached assets (the
entities and 1 action with 9 entities will be executed.
  - If the configured action allows only 1 entity, 89 actions each with 1 action will be
executed.
7. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and change the default Impersonation setting for the
Search entity.
Each MediaCentral search needs a valid account. This account is used to check permissions; depending on the used account, the search can return different results.
By default, the Action Engine Search uses the System Identity (service account). You can change this default behavior by selecting one of the following from the Identity list:
- System
The Action Engine Search uses the System Identity (service account) to execute the search on run-time of the scheduled job.
- User
Select a MediaCentral User Management (IAM) user from the User list.
The Action Engine Search uses the specified user account to execute the search on run-time of the scheduled job.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App To modify saved Search settings:
1. In the Jobs panel's JOBS tab, select a scheduled job that has a Search entity.
2. In the Define Job panel, select the entity Search.
3. In the Details panel, click the Modify a search card.
The Search app opens in a new window.
4. Modify the saved search settings as needed.
5. Click OK.
The Search window is closed, your changes made to the search are saved.
Defining Actions Define the parameters of the action to be executed in the action-specific section of the Details panel. Note that some actions require attached assets; the assets to be attached are defined by the Search entity configuration of the scheduled job. Other actions cannot be applied to assets. The following table shows the dependency between actions and asset attachments defined by the Search entity.
Actions Requires Search Search Entity Is Does Not Support Search Entity Optional Entity Add to Folder x – – Media Analytics x – – Publish x – – Send to Playback x – – Update Metadata x – – x1 AM Orchestration – – Notification – x2 – Create Entity – – x Create Folder – – x Adding the Search entity is required when the process that will be started by the action requires attached assets ; if the process does not require attached assets, the Search entity is not needed.
2 Adding the Search entity is required if you want MediaCentral Cloud UX to open the default application for the referenced asset when a user clicks the notification message.
If you configure an action that requires attached assets but the Search entity has not been added to the scheduled job, a corresponding warning appears, as shown in the following illustration.
> **Note:** Each Action supports dedicated system types and asset types. To avoid misconfiguration, restrict the
settings of the Search entity to the system and asset types supported by the action(s) you add to the scheduled job. If you use more than one action in the scheduled job, the search result should ideally only contain assets that are supported by all actions of the scheduled job. If you want to use actions that support different system and asset types, create separate scheduled jobs.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App See the following topics:
- "Defining an Add to Folder Action" on the next page
Action Engine calls against the downstream system run under a dedicated identity (account). You can modify the default impersonation setting if needed. See the following topic:
- Each action has Min. and Max. Number of Entities settings which determine how many actions will actually
be triggered. See the following topic:
For the input fields of the action specifications, you have specific options. See the following topics:
Actions: Configuration and Back-End Workflow The Schedule Engine retrieves all available Action Engines. For each Action Engine, the Schedule Editor retrieves the Action Types to populate the list of available actions in the Schedule Editor UI. An Action Type provides basic information of an action along with a list of system types plus entity types the action can be applied to. If an action depends on systems, a system selection is displayed in the Schedule Editor app’s Details panel for the Action.
When the user selects a system, the Schedule Editor retrieves the Action Specification(s) for the selected system and action type. For some actions types, such as Send to Playback, always a single Action Specification is returned; for others, such as AM Orchestration, several Action Specifications can be returned. In this case, the user will select one of the provided Actions Specifications.
An Action Specification defines the visual elements by providing labels, value types, and potentially default values. With this information, the Schedule Editor can display the layout of the Action’s section in the Details panel.
When a user selects a control representing a dynamic property (such as Profile), the Schedule Editor retrieves the related properties. In a case of STP profiles, not only the profile names but also their attributes (Device, Resolution, …) get retrieved.
After a user populated all mandatory fields, the Schedule Editor is able to save the Action configuration in the schedule. The Schedule Editor only validates the mandatory flag of properties but does not validate dependent fields, for example, if some of Boolean values (check boxes) need to appear in certain combinations only. Such invalid combinations can only be detected by the Action Engines, when the Action is executed.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App When triggering the scheduled job the Schedule Engine reads the related Action Configuration, resolves expressions and sends a corresponding Action Message to the responsible Action Engine. The Action executes the action, for example by creating an STP job or Asset Management process.
Action Specifications An Action Specification defines the visual elements by providing labels, value types, and potentially default values. With this information, the Schedule Editor can display the layout of the Action’s section in the Details panel.
For all action types – except for AM Orchestration –, a predefined action specification is provided by Avid.
These action specifications cannot be altered. The Create Entity and Update Metadata action specifications additionally provide a properties picker tool that lets you dynamically add properties to the template.
For actions of type AM Orchestration, predefined action specifications are not provided by Avid. In case of AM Orchestration, the Action Engine generates dynamically action specifications for each applicable Asset Management process. The name of such an action specification corresponds to the Asset Management process label and gets displayed in the Specification drop-down list. AM Orchestration action specifications need to be configured. The following are required to create an action specification:
- A process model (created in Process Modeler, uploaded and activated in the Process Engine).
- All scripts that are required by the process model (uploaded into the script repository in
Orchestration Monitor).
The corresponding process class with attributes in Datamodel Administrator.
- A specific Action (process creation) template defined in Datamodel Administrator.
  - Template name: ACTION_<process class name>
  - Supported properties: boolean, duration, float, integer, text, timecode, legal list
Not supported: date, datetime, time; multi-value, multi-value compound; thesaurus, master data The corresponding process creation right (Processes/Create_<process class name>) assigned in Asset
- Management User Manager to the user groups that are to be allowed to create the process.
For more information, see "Creating Action Templates" in the Avid MediaCentral | Asset Management Process Reference.
Auto-Fixing Changed Action Specifications When you select a schedule in the Jobs panel's JOBS tab and changes in an action specification of the scheduled job are detected (for example, a property has been added to or removed from the action specification) an “Action Specification(s) Changed” window will be displayed. The window lists all changes.
The scheduled job will be automatically adapted and saved. After that, the action specification in the scheduled job is aligned again. However, additional manual steps might be necessary to make the action valid, such as setting a new mandatory parameter.
Click OK to close the “Action Specification(s) Changed” window.
Defining an Add to Folder Action Action Requires Search Entity Search Settings (Restrict Search to) Add to Folder Yes System: Production Management (local module)
- Asset Types: Masterclip, Subclip, Sequence
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Action Requires Search Entity Search Settings (Restrict Search to)
- System: Asset Management (local module)
Asset Types: <any>
> **Note:** Each Action supports dedicated system types and asset types. To avoid misconfiguration, restrict the
search settings to the system and asset types supported by the action(s) you add to the scheduled job.
If you use more than one action in the scheduled job, the search results ideally only contain assets that are supported by all actions of the scheduled job. If you want to use actions that support different system and asset types, create separate scheduled jobs.
Using the Add to Folder action you can define a schedule that triggers adding the assets found by the specified search to the specified folder in the Production Management or Asset Management module.
To define an Add to Folder action:
1. In the Define Job panel, select the action Add to Folder.
In the Details panel, the Add to Folder section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, select the Production Management or Asset Management module from the
System list. The System list shows all connected local and remote Production Management and Asset Management modules.
The template defined by the action specification is loaded.
3. To select a folder, do the following:
a. Click on the Folder list.
The list shows all top-level folders of the selected module as shown in the following illustration.
b. Select a parent folder from the Folder list.
The Folder control shows the folders of the selected top-level folder.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App c. Select a subfolder from the control. You can use the Filter control to filter the list of folders.
The control shows one of the following:
  - The subfolders of the selected folder.
  - The No values found message if the folder does not contain subfolders.
d. To navigate back, do on of the following:
- To navigate back to the top-level folders, click the X button in the control.
- To navigate upwards in the folder hierarchy, click behind the last folder name and delete
the subfolder name(s) by pressing the backspace key.
e. To confirm your selection, click outside the Folder selection control.
The control is closed and the selected folder is set as Folder.
f. To replace a set folder, click on the Folder list, then click the X button in the control and select another folder.
4. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 519.
Above the identity impersonation setting, the Advanced toggle also shows the Min. Number of Entities and Max. Number of Entities defined in the Action Specification. These settings specify how many entities can be processed min/max by a single ActionCommand and cannot be changed. See "Showing the Min. and Max. Number of Entities Values" on page 520.
5. In the Define Job panel, click the Save button to save the scheduled job.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Defining an AM Orchestration Action Action Requires Search Entity Search Settings (Restrict Search to) AM Orchestration Optional (required in cases l System: Wolftech News where the process that will be Asset Types: Story, Pitch, Publishing Output triggered requires attached
- System: Production Management (local
assets) module) Asset Types: Masterclip, Subclip, Sequence System: Asset Management (local module)
- Asset Types: <any>
> **Note:** Which system/asset types are supported
depends on the configuration of the process that will be started by the AM Orchestration action.
> **Note:** Each Action supports dedicated system types and asset types. To avoid misconfiguration, restrict the
search settings to the system and asset types supported by the action(s) you add to the scheduled job.
If you use more than one action in the scheduled job, the search results ideally only contain assets that are supported by all actions of the scheduled job. If you want to use actions that support different system and asset types, create separate scheduled jobs.
In case of AM Orchestration, the Action Engine dynamically generates action specifications for each applicable Asset Management process – each process for which a specific Action (process creation) template has been defined in Datamodel Administrator. The names of these action specifications correspond to the Asset Management process labels and get displayed in the Specification drop-down list.
Which fields are shown depends on the Action template configured for the specification. The following procedure therefore only details the general steps that are required for all AM Orchestration actions.
The attributes that are shown when clicking the Advanced toggle button, are not part of the Action template created in Datamodel Administrator but are added by the Action Engine to the action specification.
> **Note:** In this release, only actions for local modules are supported.
To define an AM Orchestration action:
1. In the Define Job panel, select the action AM Orchestration.
In the Details panel, the AM Orchestration section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, select the Asset Management module from the System list.
The Specification list is shown.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
3. Select an action specification from the Specification list.
4. Specify values for the selected specification.
Which fields are shown depends on the Action template configured for the selected specification.
5. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 519.
Above the identity impersonation setting, the Advanced toggle also shows the Min. Number of Entities and Max. Number of Entities defined in the Action Specification. These settings specify how many entities can be processed min/max by a single ActionCommand and cannot be changed. See "Showing the Min. and Max. Number of Entities Values" on page 520.
6. In the Define Job panel, click the Save button to save the scheduled job.
Defining a Create Entity Action Action Requires Search Entity Create Entity No Using the Create Entity action you can define a scheduled job that triggers the creation of an entity (metadata object) in the database of the selected Asset Management module or in Wolftech News.
This action lets you dynamically add properties once the template defined by the action specification is loaded. A properties picker tool provides the following:
- All properties of all asset and sequence types (object and EDL classes) defined in the data model of
the selected Asset Management module.
All properties of all asset types defined in the data model of Wolftech News.
- You can add the properties and provide their values as you want them to be populated on the new entity.
Note the following limitations:
- The properties picker tool is not restricted to the properties of the selected asset type but shows all
properties of all asset types defined in the data model of the respective system; misconfiguration is not prevented.
Independent of system type, read-only attributes will be filtered out and not provided in the
- properties picker. This applies also to compound properties and multi-value properties (except list
properties and thesaurus properties of system type Wolftech, which are supported).
- The section only provides text fields or lists for providing input for added properties. You need to know
which kind of value (for example, milliseconds, frames, SMPTE TC) the system expects for the used property. Refer to the CTMS properties documentation at https://developer.avid.com/ctms/api/aa/resources/attributes.html.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App To define a Create Entity action:
1. In the Define Job panel, select the action Create Entity.
In the Details panel, the Create Entity section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, select a system from the System list.
The System list shows all connected local and remote Asset Management modules and Wolftech News.
The template defined by the action specification is loaded. Note the Add/Remove button that is shown at the bottom right corner of the section.
3. Select an entity type from the Entity Type list.
The list shows the following:
  - For a selected Asset Management module: All asset and sequence types (object and EDL
classes) defined in the data model of the selected Asset Management module.
  - For Wolftech News: Story and Pitch.
4. Type the name of the entity to be created in the Entity Name field.
The name you provide will populate the common Name attribute of the CTMS interface, which will be mapped to the Maintitle property of the created asset or sequence or Name property of the created Wolftech News entity.
5. Add properties:
a. Click the Add/Remove attributes button shown at the bottom right side of the Details panel.
b. In the property picker tool that opens, check the check boxes of the properties you want to add.
You can use the Filter control at the top of the property picker to filter the list of displayed properties.
c. Click outside the property picker tool.
d. Provide values for the added properties.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
6. (optional) Remove added properties:
a. Click the Add/Remove attributes button shown at the bottom right side of the Details panel.
b. In the property picker tool that opens, deselect the check boxes of the added properties.
c. Click outside the property picker tool.
Alternatively, right-click an added property and select Remove from the context-menu that opens.
7. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 519.
Above the identity impersonation setting, the Advanced toggle also shows the Min. Number of Entities and Max. Number of Entities defined in the Action Specification. These settings specify how many entities can be processed min/max by a single ActionCommand and cannot be changed. See "Showing the Min. and Max. Number of Entities Values" on page 520.
8. In the Define Job panel, click the Save button to save the scheduled job.
Defining a Create Folder Action Action Requires Search Entity Create Folder No Using the Create Folder action you can define a scheduled job that triggers the creation of a folder in the database of the selected Production Management or Asset Management module. In case the folder with the name specified in the scheduled job already exists, no action is performed.
Note the following:
- If you have selected a Parent Folder and switch to another System, the parent folder will be cleared
and reset to initial state.
If a folder saved in the Action has become invalid, the Parent Folder control shows the corresponding
- message; the tool-tip of the warning icon in the Action header shows the parentFolderId of the invalid
folder.
To define a Create Folder action:
1. In the Define Job panel, select the action Create Folder.
In the Details panel, the Create Folder section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, select the Production Management or Asset Management module from the
System list. The System list shows all connected local and remote Production Management and Asset Management modules.
The template defined by the action specification is loaded.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
3. To select a folder, do the following:
a. Select the parent folder from the Parent Folder list. The list shows all top-level folders of the selected module. The following illustration shows the top-level folders of a selected Production Management module.
The Parent Folder control shows the folders of the selected top-level folder.
b. Select a subfolder from the control. You can use the Filter control to filter the list of folders.
The control shows one of the following:
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
  - The subfolders of the selected folder.
  - The No values found message if the folder does not contain subfolders.
c. To navigate back, do on of the following:
- To navigate back to the top-level folders, click the X button in the control.
- To navigate upwards in the folder hierarchy, click behind the last folder name and delete
the subfolder name(s) by pressing the backspace key.
d. To confirm your selection, click outside the Parent Folder control.
The control is closed and the selected folder is set as Parent Folder.
e. To replace a set parent folder, click on the Parent Folder list, then click the X button in the control and select another folder.
4. Type the name of the folder to be created in the Folder Name field.
5. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 519.
Above the identity impersonation setting, the Advanced toggle also shows the Min. Number of Entities and Max. Number of Entities defined in the Action Specification. These settings specify how many entities can be processed min/max by a single ActionCommand and cannot be changed. See "Showing the Min. and Max. Number of Entities Values" on page 520.
6. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the scheduled job.
Defining a Media Analytics Action Action Requires Search Entity Search Settings (Restrict Search to) Media Analytics Yes l System: local Production Management module
- Asset Type: Masterclip
> **Note:** Each Action supports dedicated system types and asset types. To avoid misconfiguration, restrict the
search settings to the system and asset types supported by the action(s) you add to the scheduled job.
If you use more than one action in the scheduled job, the search results ideally only contain assets that are supported by all actions of the scheduled job. If you want to use actions that support different system and asset types, create separate scheduled jobs.
Using the Media Analytics action you can define a schedule that triggers a Media Analytics job, concrete: a Transcribe operation.
Note the following:
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Only Production Management master clips are supported.
- Only the provider Avid Ada and the feature Transcribe are supported.
- Triggering Transcribe jobs with Avid Ada requires an Avid Ada Transcribe (formally named
MediaCentral | STT Base Package) license.
Since the Transcribe license is a quota license that tracks the number of hours of transcribed audio, formulate the schedule carefully.
To define a Media Analytics action:
1. In the Define Job panel, select the action Media Analytics.
In the Details panel, the Media Analytics section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, select an analytics provider from the Specification list: In this release, only Avid
Ada is supported.
3. Select the features that are to be processed from the Features list. In this release, only Transcribe is
supported.
4. Define the title of the Media Analytics job to be shown in the MediaCentral Cloud UX Process app by
using the Title field (type Expression). If you do not change the default, the Media Analytics job title will be Analytics: <name of analyzed asset> To change the default expression, do the following:
a. Hover the cursor over the Title field and click the Edit expression button that is shown.
Analytics b. In the Edit Expression - Title dialog box edit the expression. For example, change to Transcribe.
c. Click OK to close the dialog box.
The changed expression is shown as content of the Title field.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
5. Select the audio tracks that are to be processed from the Tracks list. The list shows the maximum
number of 16 audio tracks (A1-A16). By default, A1 and A2 are preselected.
> **Note:** If you select more than one audio track, the tracks will be mixed down into one track for
transcription and a transcript for the mixed down audio track is created.
6. Do not use the Language list, which is reserved for future use.
In this release, the media analytics jobs run with language auto detection.
7. Enter a name for the transcript in the Result Name field. This name will be shown as transcript name
in the Transcript tab. If you do not provide a result name, the transcript will be named according to the audio track(s) for which it is created.
8. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button to show the Min. Number of Entities and Max. Number of
Entities defined in the Action Specification. These settings specify how many entities can be processed min/max by an single ActionCommand and cannot be changed. See "Showing the Min.
and Max. Number of Entities Values" on page 520.
9. In the Define Job panel, click the Save button to save the scheduled job.
Defining a Notification Action Action Requires Search Entity Notification Optional: Adding the Search entity is required if you want MediaCentral Cloud UX to open the default application for the referenced asset when a user clicks the notification message.
Using the (platform) Notification action you can configure notifications and define which user groups of the MediaCentral platform User Management (IAM) should receive the notifications.
> **Note:** By default, up to 10 groups can be added to an individual notification action. This number might be
altered by the setting GROUP_MAX_SIZE of the configMap "avid-push-notifications-notification- service-core". See "Configuring the Maximum of Groups for Notification Actions" on page 602.
When the scheduled job is executed and a platform Notification is sent, the configured users will receive a Platform notification, as shown in the following example. If you added a Search entity to the scheduled job, the notification includes a deep link to the found asset: When a user clicks the notification, the default app of the asset opens in MediaCentral Cloud UX. For more information, see "The Notifications App" in the MediaCentral Cloud UX User's Guide. Alternatively, an activity notification email can be send to the users.
Sending platform notifications and emails can be enabled and disabled for scheduled jobs in MediaCentral Cloud UX > User Settings > Notifications by using the "System independent job applies" toggle button in the "Platform Automation" section. Note that this only applies to notifications that do not include a deep link to an asset. For more information, see "Configuring Notifications" in the MediaCentral Cloud UX User's Guide.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App To define a Notification action:
1. In the Define Job panel, select the action Notification.
In the Details panel, the Notification section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, type a name in the Title field.
3. (optional) Type a message text in the Message field.
4. Select one or more groups which should receive notifications from the Destinations list. By default,
you can select up to 10 groups.
The Destinations list shows all user groups that are retrieved from the MediaCentral IAM.
5. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button to show the Min. Number of Entities and Max. Number of
Entities defined in the Action Specification. These settings specify how many entities can be processed min/max by an single ActionCommand and cannot be changed. See "Showing the Min.
and Max. Number of Entities Values" on page 520.
6. In the Define Job panel, click the Save button to save the scheduled job.
Defining a Publish Action Action Requires Search Entity Search Settings (Restrict Search to) Publish Yes l System: Production Management (local module) Asset Types: Masterclip, Subclip, Sequence
- System: Asset Management (local module)
Asset Types: Video-type assets, EDLs, Images
> **Note:** Each Action supports dedicated system types and asset types. To avoid misconfiguration, restrict the
search settings to the system and asset types supported by the action(s) you add to the scheduled job.
If you use more than one action in the scheduled job, the search results ideally only contain assets that are supported by all actions of the scheduled job. If you want to use actions that support different system and asset types, create separate scheduled jobs.
Using the Publish action you can define a scheduled job that triggers publishing with Publisher without using the MediaCentral Publisher app. The following can be published:
- Production Management: master clips, subclips, sequences
- Asset Management: video-type assets, EDLs, images
16 Using the Schedule Editor App To define a Publish action:
1. In the Define Job panel, select the action Publish.
In the Details panel, the Publish section is shown.
2. Provide the title of the clip to be created in Publisher.
By default, the field is of type Property and the "common|name" property is preselected; thus, the value of the name property will be used as clip title in Publisher. You cannot select another property.
Note that the name property can be overwritten by a value set for the title on the used publish template in Publisher (field TITLE: DEFAULT VALUE in the Publisher app).
3. Select the publish template to be used from the Publish Template list.
The list shows all templates of type Video and Image.
4. (optional) Change the status of the "Publish clip when done" toggle.
- If toggled on, the asset is published to the defined targets of the selected template after it is
imported to the Publisher backend.
- If toggled off (default), the asset is not published after it is imported to the Publisher backend.
5. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button to show the Min. Number of Entities and Max. Number of
Entities defined in the Action Specification. These settings specify how many entities can be processed min/max by an single ActionCommand and cannot be changed. See "Showing the Min.
and Max. Number of Entities Values" on page 520.
6. In the Define Job panel, click the Save button to save the scheduled job.
Defining a Send to Playback Action Action Requires Search Entity Search Settings (Restrict Search to) Send to Playback Yes l System: Production Management (local module)
- Asset Type: Masterclip, Subclip, Sequence
> **Note:** Each Action supports dedicated system types and asset types. To avoid misconfiguration, restrict the
search settings to the system and asset types supported by the action(s) you add to the scheduled job.
If you use more than one action in the scheduled job, the search results ideally only contain assets that are supported by all actions of the scheduled job. If you want to use actions that support different system and asset types, create separate scheduled jobs.
Using the Send to Playback action you can define a schedule that triggers sending the asset to a playback destination. The action Send to Playback can be used for Production Management assets.
The Action pm-stp of the Action Engine STP triggers the Send to Playback activity for the asset specified by the Entity property (by default, the asset of the source event).
> **Note:** In this release, only actions for one local system are supported.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App To define a Send to Playback action:
1. In the Define Job panel, select the action Send to Playback.
In the Details panel, the Send to Playback section is shown.
2. Select the Production Management module from the System list.
3. Select an STP profile from the Profile list.
The list shows all profiles that are created for the system in the Workflow Settings app, Send to Playback section. For more information, see "Working with the Send to Playback Settings" on page 242.
Data in the read-only fields Device, Resolution, Video, Audio, and Workspace is read from the selected profile.
4. (optional) Enable the following toggles if needed:
  - Burn Graphics: Enable the toggle if your MediaCentral Cloud UX system is integrated with a
graphics management system, and you are sending an asset to playback that includes timed graphic elements that are to be embedded (“burn-in”) into the video stream.
  - High Priority: Enable the toggle if you need the asset to be transferred as quickly as possible to
the playback device.
  - Overwrite: Enable the toggle if you want to overwrite any asset with the same VideoID already
sent to the playback device.
5. (optional) If you do not want to keep the default Process Name, replace the default entry Action-
PM.STP (Production Management). This is the name that will be shown for the process in the Process app.
6. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" on page 519.
Above the identity impersonation setting, the Advanced toggle also shows the Min. Number of Entities and Max. Number of Entities defined in the Action Specification. These settings specify how many entities can be processed min/max by a single ActionCommand and cannot be changed. See "Showing the Min. and Max. Number of Entities Values" on page 520.
7. In the Define Job panel, click the Save button to save the scheduled job.
Defining an Update Metadata Action Action Requires Search Entity Search Settings (Restrict Search to) Update Metadata Yes System: Wolftech News
- Asset Types: Story, Pitch, Publishing Output
System: Production Management (local module)
- Asset Types: Masterclip, Subclip, Sequence
System: Asset Management (local module)
- Asset Types: <any>
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
> **Note:** Each Action supports dedicated system types and asset types. To avoid misconfiguration, restrict the
search settings to the system and asset types supported by the action(s) you add to the scheduled job.
If you use more than one action in the scheduled job, the search results ideally only contain assets that are supported by all actions of the scheduled job. If you want to use actions that support different system and asset types, create separate scheduled jobs.
Using the Update Metadata action you can define a scheduled job that triggers the update of selected properties with specified values in the database of a local or remote Production Management or Asset Management module or in Wolftech News.
This action lets you dynamically add properties once the template defined by the action specification is loaded. A properties picker tool provides all properties of all asset types (object classes) defined in the data model of the selected Asset Management module resp. all properties of the selected Production Management module or Wolftech News. You can add the properties and provide their values as you want them to be updated on the entity.
Note the following limitations:
- The properties picker tool is not restricted to the properties of the selected asset type but shows all
properties of all asset types defined in the data model of the respective system; misconfiguration is not prevented.
Independent of system type, read-only attributes will be filtered out and not provided in the
- properties picker. This applies also to compound properties and multi-value properties (except list
properties and thesaurus properties of system type Wolftech, which are supported).
- Description property in Wolftech News entities: a description can be set/updated for Story and Pitch
entities but not for a Publishing Output entity.
The section only provides text fields or lists for providing input for added properties. You need to know
- which kind of value (for example, milliseconds, frames, SMPTE TC) the system expects for the used
property. Refer to the CTMS properties documentation at https://developer.avid.com/ctms/api/aa/resources/attributes.html.
To define an Update Metadata action:
1. In the Define Job panel, select the action Update Metadata.
In the Details panel, the Update Metadata section is shown.
2. In the Details panel, select a system from the System list.
The System list shows all connected local and remote Production Management and Asset Management modules and Wolftech News.
The template defined by the action specification is loaded. Note the Add/Remove button that is shown at the bottom right corner of the section.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
3. Add properties:
a. Click the Add/Remove attributes button shown at the bottom right side of the Details panel.
b. In the property picker tool that opens, check the check boxes of the properties you want to add.
You can use the Filter control at the top of the property picker to filter the list of displayed properties.
c. Click outside the property picker tool.
d. Provide values for the added properties.
4. (optional) Remove added properties:
a. Click the Add/Remove attributes button shown at the bottom right side of the Details panel.
b. In the property picker tool that opens, deselect the check boxes of the added properties.
c. Click outside the property picker tool.
Alternatively, right-click an added property and select Remove from the context-menu that opens.
5. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and modify the identity impersonation setting if needed.
For more information, see "Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls" below.
Above the identity impersonation setting, the Advanced toggle also shows the Min. Number of Entities and Max. Number of Entities defined in the Action Specification. These settings specify how many entities can be processed min/max by a single ActionCommand and cannot be changed. See "Showing the Min. and Max. Number of Entities Values" on the next page.
6. In the Define Rule panel, click the Save button to save the rule.
Modifying the Default Impersonation Setting for Action Engine Calls Action Engine calls against the system defined by the Entity ID run under a dedicated identity (account). By default, the user identity of the trigger (schedule) is used. You can change this default behavior, as follows.
The impersonation setting is provided for most actions, except for Media Analytics, Notifications and Publish.
To change the default impersonation setting:
1. In the Define Job panel, select an action.
2. In the Details panel, click the Advanced toggle button shown in the action header.
3. Modify the identity impersonation setting by selecting one of the following:
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
- System
The Action Engine uses the System Identity (service account) to trigger actions on the selected system.
- Trigger (default)
The Action Engine uses the user identity of the scheduled job to trigger actions on the selected system.
