Enable the Fallback to System Identity toggle for cases where the trigger does not provide a user identity. Then, the system identity is used as a fallback. If the Fallback to System Identity toggle is disabled, the Action will fail if the trigger does not provide a user identity.
- User
Select a MediaCentral User Management (IAM) user from the User list.
The Action Engine uses the specified user account to trigger actions on the selected system.
4. Save the scheduled job.
Showing the Min. and Max. Number of Entities Values If your scheduled job includes a Search entity, the "Max hits of search" and "Max batch size" of the Search define the maximum number of hits in the search results when the job is triggered and the maximum number of hits that will be attached to a single action. See "(Optional) Defining a Search Entity" on page 498.
But, how many actions will actually be triggered is determined by the Min. and Max. Number of Entities settings in the Action Specification of the action.
Example: The search finds 89 assets and Max batch size is "20".
If the configured action (such as AM Orchestration) allows up to 50 attached assets (the Max.
- Number of Entities in the Action Specification is set to 50), 4 actions each with 20 entities and 1 action
with 9 entities will be executed.
- If the configured action allows only 1 entity, 89 actions each with 1 action will be executed.
You can show the Min. Number of Entities and Max. Number of Entities for each action by using the Advanced toggle button. You cannot change these settings.
To show the Min. and Max. Number of Entities values:
1. In the Define Job panel, select an action.
2. (if the System field is shown) In the Details panel, select the system.
3. (if the Specification field is shown) Select the specification.
4. Click the Advanced toggle button.
The Min. and Max Number of Entities values are shown above the identity impersonation settings.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Action Min. / Max. Number of Entities Values -* AM Orchestration Min. Number of Entities:
-* Max. Number of Entities:
*No default, depends on the selected custom action specification Defining the Input Type: Literal, Property or Expression In contrast to the input fields in other sections of the Details panel, the actions section allows you to switch the input type of the displayed fields.
Sample Use Case for Different Input Types Example: For a notification, the Message field by default is a text field; here you can type a message that is to be shown as notification text. If you do not want to provide a "hard-coded" message, you can change the Message field to the following types:
"Property"
> **Note:** Although you can select the Property type, do not use it for Schedule Editor actions: Due to
lacking system context, for a property only an empty list will be shown.
"Expression" and formulate an expression that injects the value of a specified attribute as contents of
- the notification message. An expression allows also to combine hard-coded text with values from
attributes.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Switching the Input Type When you hover the cursor over an input field or its label, to the left side of the label an icon indicates the input type:
- Literal
- Property
- Expression
By default, input fields are shown as type "Literal." This means that the input control defined in the action specification is shown for each field. You can change the input type for a field by using the field's context menu.
In some cases, the context menu shows an additional entry:
- If a default value is available for a field, the field label is displayed in italic font. In this case, the
context menu additionally shows the option "Reset to Default" (for example, for the toggles and Process Name field in a Send to Playback action).
Double-clicking an italic label resets the field to its default value and type.
In actions where you can dynamically add properties (Create Entity and Update Metadata), the
- context menu of any added property shows the "Remove" option.
> **Note:** The option to switch the input type is only available if the action specification supports this feature.
When you change the input type of a field, you are not prevented from creating an invalid input.
To change the input type:
1. Right-click on a label or input field.
2. Select the input type from the context menu.
The currently applied input type is dimmed in the context menu.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
  - Literal: The input control defined in the action specification for the field is used.
  - Property: The input field switches to a drop-down list.
> **Note:** Although you can select the Property type, do not use it for Schedule Editor actions: Due
to lacking system context, for a property only an empty list will be shown.
  - Expression: The input field switches to a read-only box. To enter an expression, click the Edit
expression button to the right. See "Entering an Expression" below.
To restore the original input type and default value:
- Right-click on an italic label and select Reset to Default from the context menu.
- Double-click on an italic label.
Using Expressions Expressions let you inject the value of a specified attribute as contents of another attribute.
Entering an Expression Before you can enter an expression, you must switch the input type of the desired field to type "Expression." To enter an expression:
1. Right-click on a label or input field.
2. Select Expression from the context menu.
The input type for the field is set to Expression. An "Open dialog to enter an expression" placeholder is shown in the field.
3. Hover the cursor over the field.
To the right side, an "Edit expression" button is shown.
4. Click the Edit expression button.
The Edit Expression - <field name> dialog box opens.
5. Enter or paste an expression in the field, as shown in the following example.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
6. Click OK.
The Edit Expression dialog box is closed. The expression is shown in the field. If the expression text is too long, its display is cut and ends in an ellipsis. Hover the cursor over the field to show the entire expression in a tool-tip.
7. To remove an expression:
a. Hover the cursor over the field and click the Edit expression button.
b. In the Edit Expression dialog box, delete the expression text and click OK.
Expressions: General Examples The following table lists some general expression examples that can be used in Action fields.
Sample Expressions Meaning ${common|name} Fetch the value of the common property Name from the search results ${custom|myprop} Fetch the value of the custom property "myprop" from the search results "job for [" + ${common|title} + "]" Combined string expression ${custom|myprop} == "abc" ? "ABC" : "something else" Expression with preprocessing token ${custom|myprop.curr} + " - " + ${custom|otherprop} "Changed from " + ${custom|myprop.curr} + " -> " + ${custom|myprop.prev} ((string)${status}) == "Ready for Review" && If the status is Ready For Review and the due ((DateTime?)${custom|dueDateTime})<= date and time is 15 minutes or less, set the DateTime.Now.AddMinutes(15)?"Urgent":"Normal" urgency to normal Expression Context Search results access The search results is available as variable "this". The properties of the search results can also be accessed implicitly without the "this." prefix.
Execution context The internal execution context (ExpressionContext) is available as variable _context.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App String Extension Methods Methods Meaning <input>.Matches(<pattern>) Regular expression match <input>.Like(<pattern>) Wildcard match <input>.StringContains(<substr1>) String match <input>.StringContains([<substr1>, <substr2> ...]) String match Editing a Scheduled Job You can edit the items and properties of a scheduled job at any time. The following process details all editing options for all scheduled job items.
To edit a scheduled job:
1. (optional) Temporarily stop job processing in the Schedule Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming
Schedule Engine Job Processing" on page 487.
2. In the Jobs panel's JOBS tab, select the scheduled job to be edited.
3. Change the name of the scheduled job: In the Details pane, replace the current name in the Job
Name field.
Scheduled job names need to be unique. If you enter a name that is already used for another scheduled job, the name will be appended with a counter in brackets when you save the scheduled job.
4. Change the status of the scheduled job: In the Details panel, do one of the following:
- To enable a disabled scheduled job, click the gray Enable toggle so it turns blue.
- To disable an enabled scheduled job, click the blue Enable toggle so it turns gray.
5. Click the Expand toggle button in front of Additional information and do the following:
- Enter a new comment in the Comment text box. The comment is only used within the Schedule
Editor app.
- (Reserved for future use) Change the status of the Auto Delete toggle button.
6. Add an additional trigger.
a. In the Jobs panel's CONFIGURATION tab, click the trigger you want to add.
b. Set the properties of the trigger in the Details panel's Trigger section.
For more information, see"Defining Triggers" on page 492.
7. Modify a trigger.
a. Select the trigger in the Define Job panel.
b. Change the properties of the trigger in the Details panel's Trigger section.
For more information, see"Defining Triggers" on page 492.
8. Delete a trigger.
a. In the Define Job panel, hover the cursor over the trigger, and click the Delete button that is shown.
b. Confirm the security prompt by clicking Delete.
9. (If required by a used action) Add a Search entity.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App a. In the Jobs panel's CONFIGURATION tab, click the Search entity card.
b. In the Details panel, define the Max. hits of search and Max. batch size settings.
c. In the Details panel, click the Define a search card.
d. In the Search window that opens, define your search settings and click the OK button.
For more information, see "(Optional) Defining a Search Entity" on page 498.
10. Modify the Search entity.
a. Select the Search entity in the Define Job panel.
b. In the Details panel, change the Max. hits of search and Max. batch size settings.
c. In the Details panel, change the search settings: Click the Modify the search card, and in the Search window that opens, define new search settings and click the OK button.
For more information, see"(Optional) Defining a Search Entity" on page 498.
11. Delete the Search entity.
a. In the Define Job panel, hover the cursor over the Search entity, and click the Delete button that is shown.
b. Confirm the security prompt by clicking Delete.
12. Add an action.
a. In the Jobs panel's CONFIGURATION tab, click the action you want to add.
b. Define at least the mandatory properties of the action.
13. Delete an action.
a. In the Define Job panel, hover the cursor over the action.
b. Click the Delete button that is shown.
c. Click Delete in the security prompt.
14. Change the parameters for an Add to Folder action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Add To Folder.
b. In the Details panel, select another Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select another folder from the Folder list.
d. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Add to Folder Action" on page 504.
15. Change the parameters for an AM Orchestration action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action AM Orchestration.
b. In the Details panel, select another Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select another action specification from the Specification list.
d. Specify values for the selected specification.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an AM Orchestration Action" on page 507.
16. Change the parameters for a Create Entity action.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Create Entity.
b. In the Details panel, select another Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select another asset or sequence type from the Entity Type list.
d. Provide another entity name in the Entity Name field.
e. Change the values of added properties.
f. Add properties and values.
g. Remove added properties.
h. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Entity Action" on page 508.
17. Change the parameters for a Create Folder action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Create Folder.
b. In the Details panel, select another Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Select another parent folder from the Parent Folder list.
d. Type the name of another folder to be created in the Folder Name field.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Create Folder Action" on page 510.
18. Change the parameters of a Media Analytics action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Media Analytics.
b. Change the title of the Media Analytics job to be shown in the MediaCentral Cloud UX Process app by using the Title field (type Expression).
c. Select other audio tracks from the Tracks list.
d. Change the name of the transcript to be shown in the Transcript tab by entering a new name in the Result Name field.
For more information, see "Defining a Media Analytics Action" on page 512.
19. Change the parameters for a Notification action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Notification.
b. In the Details panel, type another name in the Title field.
c. Type another message text in the Message field.
d. Select one or more Platform User Management groups which should receive the notification from the Destinations list.
For more information, see "Defining a Notification Action" on page 514.
20. Change the parameters for a Publish action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Publish.
b. Change the title of the Publisher clip in the Name field.
c. Select another publish template to be used from the Publish Template list.
d. Change the status of the "Publish clip when done" toggle.
For more information, see "Defining a Publish Action" on page 515.
21. Change the parameters of a Send to Playback action.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Send to Playback.
b. Select another STP profile from the Profile list.
c. Enable or disable the Burn Graphics/High Priority/Overwrite toggles as needed.
d. Change the name of the process to be shown in the Process app.
e. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining a Send to Playback Action" on page 516.
22. Change the parameters for an Update Metadata action.
a. In the Define Job panel, select the action Update Metadata.
b. In the Details panel, select another Production Management or Asset Management system from the System list.
c. Change the values of added properties.
d. Add properties and values.
e. Remove added properties.
f. (optional) Click the Advanced toggle button and specify identity impersonation settings.
For more information, see "Defining an Update Metadata Action" on page 517.
23. Click the Save button to save the scheduled job.
If the scheduled job shows validation issues, a Validation dialog box opens that summarizes the validation issues. Click Save Anyway to save the scheduled job with all validation issues, or click Cancel and resolve the listed issues before saving again.
24. (if paused) Resume job processing in the Schedule Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Schedule
Engine Job Processing" on page 487.
Duplicating a Scheduled Job As an alternative to building an entirely new scheduled job, the Schedule Editor app allows you to duplicate an existing scheduled job to expedite the job creation process.
> **Note:** The Duplicate feature is only activated for a scheduled job that has no unsaved changes.
To duplicate a scheduled job:
1. In the Jobs panel, open the JOBS tab.
2. Do one of the following:
- Right-click a scheduled job in the jobs table and select Duplicate in the context-menu that
opens.
- Double-click a scheduled job in the job table, and in the Define Job panel click the Duplicate
button.
A new duplicated scheduled job is created and loaded into the Define Job and Details panels.
  - The name of the scheduled job is "Copy <original schedule name>".
  - A new Job ID is assigned to the scheduled job.
  - The scheduled job is disabled.
  - The scheduled job is not saved yet.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
3. (optional) Rename the scheduled job and edit the items of the scheduled job. See "Editing a
Scheduled Job" on page 525.
4. Enable the scheduled job.
5. Click the Save button to save the scheduled job.
If you click Cancel, the duplicated job is discarded.
Exporting Scheduled Jobs The Schedule Editor app provides options to export scheduled jobs. You can export the following:
- An individual scheduled job selected in the Jobs panel or the scheduled job that is currently being
loaded into the Define Job and Details panels.
The export creates a single JSON file.
Several scheduled jobs selected in the Jobs panel.
- The export creates a Zip file containing the selected scheduled jobs as a separate JSON file.
- All scheduled jobs.
The export creates a Zip file containing each scheduled job as a separate JSON file.
Depending on your web browser's settings the following happens during the export operation:
The scheduled jobs are directly downloaded to the download location specified in your web browser’s
- settings.
The Save As dialog box opens that lets you save the files to a selected directory.
> **Note:** The Export feature is only activated for scheduled jobs that have no unsaved changes.
To export an individual scheduled job:
1. Open the Jobs panel and make sure that no scheduled job has unsaved changes.
2. Do one of the following:
- In the jobs table, right-click a scheduled job and select Export from the context-menu.
- Select a scheduled job in the jobs table to open it in the Define Job panel and click the Export
job button on the Define Job panel header.
The file is saved in JSON format. The file name is "<scheduled job name>.json".
To export several scheduled jobs at the same time:
1. Open the Jobs panel and make sure that no scheduled job has unsaved changes.
2. In the jobs table, do one of the following:
- Use Ctrl+click to select several non-consecutive scheduled jobs in the table.
- Use Shift+click to select several consecutive scheduled jobs in the table.
- Select a scheduled job and use Shift+ Arrow Up or Arrow Down key to select several
consecutive scheduled jobs in the table.
3. Right-click the selection and select Export in the context-menu that is shown.
The selected scheduled jobs are downloaded as a Zip archive. The file name is "jobs-<time stamp>.zip". Each exported scheduled job is included as a separate *.json file in the Zip archive. For the scheduled jobs in the Zip archive, the scheduled job name, as set in Schedule Editor, is used as file name. Invalid characters are replaced with underscores in the file name.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App To export all schedules:
1. Open the Jobs panel and make sure that no schedule has unsaved changes.
The Export all jobs button is activated.
2. In the Jobs panel, click the Export all jobs button.
All schedules are downloaded as a Zip archive. The file name is "jobs-<time stamp>.zip". Each exported schedule is included as a separate *.json file in the Zip archive. For the schedules in the Zip archive, the schedule name, as set in Schedule Editor, is used as file name. Invalid characters are replaced with underscores in the file name.
Importing Scheduled Jobs The Schedule Editor app provides options to import previously exported scheduled jobs. You can import the following:
- Individual scheduled jobs (JSON files).
- Scheduled jobs included in a Zip file (containing each scheduled job as a separate JSON file).
During the import, you have options to define "enabled" and "overwrite" settings for the import scheduled jobs.
To import scheduled jobs:
1. (optional) Temporarily stop job processing in the Schedule Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming
Schedule Engine Job Processing" on page 487.
2. In the Jobs panel, click the Import Jobs button.
The Import Jobs dialog box opens.
3. Click the Browse button.
The Open dialog box opens.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
4. Select the files you want to import: individual scheduled jobs (*.json files) or scheduled jobs in a *.zip
file or a combination of both. Multi-selection is supported.
5. Click Open.
The Open dialog box closes. The names of the files you selected are displayed in the Select Files field.
6. Define the "enabled" status of new scheduled jobs (scheduled jobs with new Job ID).
- To disable new scheduled job(s), leave the blue "Disable new jobs" toggle enabled (default).
- To enable new scheduled job(s), click the blue "Disable new jobs" toggle so it turns gray.
7. Define if the imported scheduled job(s) should overwrite a scheduled job with the same Job ID.
- To keep all scheduled jobs and do not overwrite any scheduled job with an imported scheduled
job, leave the gray "Overwrite existing jobs" toggle disabled (default).
If a scheduled job with the same Job ID already exists, the scheduled job will be imported as a new scheduled job with a new ID.
- To overwrite each schedule with the imported schedule that has the same Job ID, click the gray
"Overwrite existing jobs" toggle so it turns blue.
If a schedule with the same Job ID already exists, the schedule will be overwritten by the imported schedule.
In both cases, the name of the imported schedule will be changed (appended by a counter in brackets) if the schedule name already exists.
8. Define the "enabled" status of existing schedules (schedules with no new Job ID).
> **Note:** The "Keep enabled status of existing jobs" toggle can only be used as long as the "Overwrite
existing jobs" button is enabled.
- To keep the "enabled" status as present in existing schedules, leave the blue "Keep enabled
status of existing jobs" toggle enabled (default).
- To overwrite the "enabled" status of existing schedules, click the blue "Keep enabled status of
existing schedules" toggle so it turns gray.
9. Click Import.
The Import Jobs dialog box is closed. The schedules are imported, and the Jobs panel is refreshed.
After the import, the properties of the schedules might be altered as follows:
  - Job Name: Appended by a counter in brackets if a schedule with the same name exists.
  - Job ID: Kept or changed depending on the "Overwrite" setting during the import.
  - Created on: will be set to the time of the import.
  - Created by: will be set to the user who imported the schedule.
  - Modified on: will be set to the time of the import.
  - Modified by: will be set to the user who imported the schedule.
  - Auto Delete status: not evaluated, reserved for future use.
  - Enabled status: Kept or changed depending on the "Disable new jobs" and "Keep enabled
status of existing jobs" settings during import.
10. (if paused) Resume job processing in the Schedule Engine. See "Pausing and Resuming Schedule
Engine Job Processing" on page 487.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Deleting Scheduled Jobs The Schedule Editor app allows you to delete scheduled jobs at any time. Note that there is no undo for the delete operation. Consider disabling the scheduled job instead of finally deleting it.
To delete an individual scheduled jobs:
1. In the Jobs panel, open the JOBS tab.
2. In the jobs table, do one of the following:
- Hover the cursor over the scheduled job to be deleted and click the Trash icon that is shown.
- Right-click the scheduled job to be deleted and select Delete in the context-menu that is
shown.
A Delete Job security prompt opens.
3. Do one of the following:
- Click the Delete button to permanently delete the scheduled job.
The scheduled job is removed from the JOBS tab table. The Define Job and Details panels are reset to initial state.
- Click the Cancel button or press the Esc key to exit the deletion process.
To delete several scheduled jobs at the same time:
1. In the Jobs panel, open the JOBS tab.
2. In the jobs table, do one of the following:
- Use Ctrl+click to select several non-consecutive scheduled jobs in the table.
- Use Shift+click to select several consecutive scheduled jobs in the table.
- Select a scheduled job and use Shift+ Arrow Up or Arrow Down key to select several
consecutive scheduled jobs in the table.
3. Right-click the selection and select Delete in the context-menu that is shown.
A Delete Jobs security prompt opens.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App
4. Do one of the following:
- Click the Delete button to permanently delete the scheduled jobs.
The scheduled jobs are removed from the JOBS tab table.
- Click the Cancel button or press the Esc key to exit the deletion process.
Scheduled Jobs Validation The Schedule Editor back-end validates jobs consistency on different levels. Validation information is provided in all panels of the app. A scheduled job is validated in the following cases:
When you create or edit the scheduled job.
- When the jobs table in the Job Panel's JOBS tab is reloaded.
- When you save the scheduled job.
- For more information, see the following topics:
- "Jobs Panel: JOBS Tab" below
- "Define Job Panel" on the next page
Jobs Panel: JOBS Tab In the left column of the JOBS tab table a warning icon is shown for a scheduled job in the following cases:
The scheduled job is missing a trigger.
- The scheduled job is missing an action.
- When you hover your cursor over the icon, a tool-tip shows the missing items.
Auto-Fixing Changed Action Specifications When you select a schedule in the Jobs panel's JOBS tab and changes in an action specification of the scheduled job are detected (for example, a property has been added to or removed from the action specification) an “Action Specification(s) Changed” window will be displayed. The window lists all changes.
The scheduled job will be automatically adapted and saved. After that, the action specification in the scheduled job is aligned again. However, additional manual steps might be necessary to make the action valid, such as setting a new mandatory parameter.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Click OK to close the “Action Specification(s) Changed” window.
Define Job Panel When you hover the cursor over a warning icon in the Define Job panel, a tool-tip informs you about required configuration steps that have not finished in the Details panel yet.
Item Message Trigger Shows a warning if a mandatory field is empty.
- Basic: Interval not set (no week days selected)
- One Time: Start time not set
- Advanced: Cron expression not set
Entity Search Shows a warning if search parameters are not defined.
Action Notification The mandatory field Title is not set.
- The mandatory field Destination is not set.
- Action Send to l The Action requires attached assets but the Search entity was not added to the
Playback scheduled job yet.
- There are no systems available.
- The system is not selected.
- The profile is not selected.
- The configured action is no longer in line with the action specification.
- The action specification is not available.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Item Message Action AM The Action requires attached assets but the Search entity was not added to the
- Orchestration scheduled job yet (if the selected custom action specification requires attached
assets).

- The action specification is not selected.

Action Create l There are no systems available.
Entity
- The system is not selected.
- The action specification is not available.
- The Entity Type is not selected.
- The Entity Name is not provided.
Action Update l The Action requires attached assets but the Search entity was not added to the Metadata scheduled job yet.
- The system is not selected.
- The action specification is not available.
Action Create l The system is not selected.
Folder
- The action specification is not available.
- The Parent Folder is not selected.
- The saved Parent Folder has become invalid. The tool-tip shows the parentFolderId
of the invalid folder.
- The Folder Name is not provided.
Action Add to l The Action requires attached assets but the Search entity was not added to the Folder scheduled job yet.
- The system is not selected.
- The Folder is not provided.
Action Media l The Action requires attached assets but the Search entity was not added to the Analytics scheduled job yet.
- The action specification is not selected.
- The action specification is not available.
- The Features are not selected.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Item Message Action Publish l The Action requires attached assets but the Search entity was not added to the scheduled job yet.
- The title is not set (if default was removed)
- The Publish Template is not selected.
- The values of Publish Template cannot be loaded.
Details Panel The Details panel shows warning icons in the item-specific sections. When you hover your cursor over the icon, a tool-tip shows the reason for the warning.
Item Message Trigger header Shows a warning if a mandatory field is not set:
- Basic: Interval not set (no week days selected)
- One Time: Start time not set
- Advanced: Cron expression not set
Entity (Search) Shows a warning if search parameters are not defined.
header Action Notification The mandatory field Title is not set.

Playback scheduled job yet.

Action Create l There are no systems available.

Validate on Save When you save the scheduled job it is validated and parsed. If issues are detected, the results of the validation are summarized in a Validation dialog box. Validation issues are shown as warnings, parsing issues are shown as "incomplete".
Do one of the following:
- Click Cancel and resolve the listed issues before saving again.
- Click Save Anyway to save the scheduled job with validation issues. Items shown as incomplete, are
completely removed from the scheduled job.
Troubleshooting The purpose of this appendix is to provide information on tools and processes that can be used to identify and resolve issues for Schedule Editor, Schedule Engine and Action Engines. See the following topics:
- "Issues Indicated by Schedule Editor Health Indicators" below
- "Issues Shown for Actions in Schedule Editor" on the next page
Issues Indicated by Schedule Editor Health Indicators The header at the top of the Schedule Editor app provides you with a health check of the back-end components that enable the functionality in the Schedule Editor app. Issues are indicated by yellow or red color, depending on the severity of the issue.
Yellow Schedule Engine health indicator Issue: The Schedule Engine health indicator in the Schedule Editor header is yellow.
Possible reason: Job processing in the ScheduleEngine has been paused.
Solution/Action: To resume event processing in the Schedule Engine, hover the cursor over the orange Schedule Engine pill and click the Resume Schedule Engine processing button in the tool-tip.
Red Schedule Engine health indicator Issue: The Schedule Engine health indicator in the Schedule Editor header is red.
Possible reason: The Schedule Engine is not accessible.
Solution/Action: In Kubernetes dashboard search for the deployment avid-platform-scheduleengine- scheduleengine and check the status and log files. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 550.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Red Action Engine Search health indicator Issue: The Action Engine Search health indicator in the Schedule Editor header is red.
Possible reason:
- The Action Engine Search is not accessible.
- The Action Engine Search cannot retrieve search results from MediaCentral Search.
Solution/Action: In Kubernetes dashboard search for the deployment avid-platform-ae-search-ae-search and check the status and log files. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 550.
Yellow Action Engines health indicator Issue: The Action Engines health indicator in the Schedule Editor header is yellow.
Possible reason: One of the registered Action Engines is in error state.
Solution/Action: In Kubernetes dashboard search for the deployment of the Action Engine and check the status and log files. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 550.
Red Action Engines health indicator Issue: The Action Engines health indicator in the Schedule Editor header is red. The fly-out menu shows "No Action Engine found".
Possible reason:
- The Schedule Engine is not accessible and thus cannot provide information on the registered Action
Engines.
Not all required platform automation components are deployed and are running properly.
- Solution/Action: Check the deployment and status of the involved components in Kubernetes dashboard.
See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 550.
Issues Shown for Actions in Schedule Editor The Schedule Editor back-end validates rules consistency on different levels. Schedule validation information is provided in all panels of the app. The following issues might be indicated.
Action AM Orchestration System not available Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective service MAMOrchestrationCTC on the Asset Management module (Control Center > Services view).
Action Specification not available Issue: The Action shows an error “Action default specification not available” (tool-tip) resp. "Failed to load specification" (details).
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Possible reason: For actions of type AM Orchestration, predefined action specifications are not provided by Avid but must be configured on each connected Asset Management module. On the referenced Asset Management module, no action specification has been configured.
Solution/Action: Configure at least one AM Orchestration action specification. See "Action Specifications" on page 504.
Action Create Entity No systems available Issue: The Action shows a “There are no systems available” error.
Possible reason: The respective CTC services of all connected Asset Management modules and Wolftech News are not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective CTC services:
Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
- Center > Services view).
- Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
System not available Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective CTC service.
- Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
Center > Services view).
Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Action Specification not available
Issue: The Action shows a "Failed to load specification" error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the respective CTC services are in proper state:
- Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management system (Control
Center > Services view).
- Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
Values of list property cannot be loaded Issue: The Action shows a “Failed to load values” error for a list property.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the respective CTC services are in proper state:
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
- Center > Services view).
- Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
Action Create Folder No systems available Issue: The Action shows a “There are no systems available” error.
Possible reason: The respective CTC service of all connected Asset Management and Production Management modules are not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective CTC services:
Asset Management: Check the status of the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management
- modules (Control Center > Services view).
- Production Management: Check the status of the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral
platform.
System not available Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective CTC service:
Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
- Center > Services view).
Production Management: Check the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Invalid value for Parent Folder
Issue: The Action Create Folder shows “Invalid value. Please select a new value” for the Parent Folder.
Possible reason: The folder has been deleted or moved.
Solution/Action: Select a valid folder from the Parent Folder control.
Failed to load Parent Folder values Issue: The Action Create Folder shows “Failed to load values” for the Parent Folder.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the respective CTC services are in proper state:
Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module(Control
- Center > Services view).
Production Management: Check the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Action Media Analytics Action Specification not available Issue: The Action shows a "Failed to load specification" error.
Possible reason: The Action Engine or required action provider Avid Ada is not deployed or not running.
Solution/Action: Check the deployment and status of the involved components in Kubernetes dashboard.
See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 467.
Action Publish Failed to load publish templates Issue: The Action shows a "Failed to load" error for the Publish Template field.
Possible reason: The configured publish templates could not be retrieved from the Publisher SAAS platform.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check the logs of Action Engine Publisher, which might give information about the cause of the issue. Potentially, the configuration of the Publisher connection or the Action Engine Publisher or both is incomplete or invalid.
Action Send to Playback System not available Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
Values of profile cannot be loaded Issue: The Action shows a “The values of the selected profile cannot be loaded” error.
Possible reason:
1. The service providing the Send to Playback profiles is not deployed or running.
2. The Send to Playback profile does not exist or has errors.
Solution/Action:
1. Check the service ‘Avid Media Services’ in Kubernetes. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for
Troubleshooting" on page 550.
2. Check the profile in the MediaCentral Cloud UX Administration > Workflow Settings > Send to
Playback. For more information, see "Working with the Send to Playback Settings" on page 242.
Action Update Metadata No systems available Issue: The Action shows a “There are no systems available” error.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Possible reason: The respective CTC service of all connected Asset Management and Production Management modules and Wolftech News are not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective CTC services:
Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
- Center > Services view).
Production Management: Check the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- System not available
Issue: The Action shows a “Selected system not available” error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Check the status of the respective CTC service:
- Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
Center > Services view).
- Production Management: Check the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
Action Specification not available Issue: The Action shows a "Failed to load specification" error.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the respective CTC services are in proper state:
- Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management system (Control
Center > Services view).
Production Management: Check the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Values of list property cannot be loaded
Issue: The Action shows a “Failed to load values” error for a list property.
Possible reason: The CTC service of the source module is not available.
Solution/Action: Click the Reload button to the right side of the message.
If this does not help, check if the respective CTC services are in proper state:
- Asset Management: Check the service MAMAssetsCTC on the Asset Management module (Control
Center > Services view).
- Production Management: Check the service avid-pam-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
- Wolftech News: Check the service wolftech-news-ctc on the MediaCentral platform.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App The Schedule Editor does not show the expected action Issue: In the Schedule Editor's JOBS pane, I do not see all actions.
Possible reason:
1. The necessary Action Engine is not deployed or running correctly. Consider especially the following
Action Engines that are only deployed with optional feature packs:
  - Action Engine PM-STP (feature pack ae-pm-stp): required if you want to use Send to Playback
actions.
  - Action Engine AM-Orchestration (feature pack: ae-am-orchestration): required if you want to
use AM Orchestration actions.
  - Action Engine Publisher (feature pack: publisher): required if you want to use Publish actions.
Solution/Action:
1. Check in Kubernetes if all Action Engines are running correctly. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard
for Troubleshooting" on page 550.
What to Check if an Expected Schedule or Action is Not Executed In case a scheduled job or an action, as part of an Platform Automation scheduled job, is not executed, the following section might be helpful to resolve the issue.
Check the Scheduled Job in the Schedule Editor Review the following:
Is the scheduled job enabled?
- The Enabled status (green check mark) is directly shown on the Jobs panel table.
Is the scheduled job still valid?
- Check if warnings are shown for elements of the scheduled job.
Was the scheduled job imported and references an unavailable module?
- Check in the Actions of the scheduled job if the referenced modules are valid.
Have all required items been added to the scheduled job?
  - Check if at least one trigger and one action are included.
  - Check if the trigger settings are valid (such as Start Time).
  - Check if the used action(s) are properly configured.
Does the scheduled job include a Search entity?
  - Check if the used Identity of the Search is correct: click the Advanced toggle button of the
Search entity in the Details panel and check the selected Identity value. Each MediaCentral search needs a valid account. This account is used to check permissions; depending on the used account, the search can return different results or no results at all.
  - Check if the Search settings match the attachment requirements of the used action.
Action Search Settings - Restrict System to: Search Settings - Restrict Type to:
Add to Folder Production Management (local and Masterclip, Subclip, remote modules) Sequence 16 Using the Schedule Editor App Action Search Settings - Restrict System to: Search Settings - Restrict Type to:
Asset Management (local and remote <any> modules) Media Analytics Production Management (local module) Masterclip Publish Production Management (local module) Masterclip, Subclip, Sequence Asset Management (local module) Video-type assets, EDLs, Images Send to Playback Production Management (local module) Masterclip, Subclip, Sequence Update Metadata Wolftech News Story, Publishing Output, Pitch Production Management (local and Masterclip, Subclip, remote modules) Sequence Asset Management (local and remote <any> modules) Check the Action Execution in the Process App The Rules Engine and Schedule Engine expose rule and job execution to the Action Monitor so that the execution of actions triggered by rules and scheduled jobs can be monitored in the Process app.
Consider reviewing the following:
- Is the corresponding scheduled job type shown in the Process app or not?
- Is the specific scheduled job shown in the Process app or not?
- Is the corresponding action type shown in the Process app or not?
- Is the specific action shown in the Process app or not?
- Is the action execution "hanging" (stays in status Created, Pending or Running for a long time)?
See "Monitoring Platform Automation Actions in the Process App" on the next page.
Such issues might indicate that the corresponding Action Engine is not running. See "Check for Incorrect or not Working Services" below.
Check for Incorrect or not Working Services Possibly a relevant service in the “messaging chain” is not working properly and prevents the execution of the respective action. To resolve this issue, review the following.
Schedule Engine and Respective Action Engines
- Are the services (avid-platform-scheduleengine, avid-platform-ae-*) up and running?
Check their status in Kubernetes Dashboard. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 550.
Are the services connected to Kafka?
- If there are issues, normally you will see this in the log files. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Troubleshooting" on page 550.
Alternatively, the connectivity status can also be viewed by using a Kafka Client.
Upstream Source System Services
- Are the services up and running?
Check their status in Kubernetes Dashboard. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 550.
- Are the services connected to Kafka?
If there are issues, normally you will see this in the log files. See "Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting" on page 550.
Alternatively, the connectivity status can also be viewed by using a Kafka Client.
Using the Process App for Monitoring Platform Automation Actions The Rules Engine and Schedule Engine expose rule and job execution to the Action Monitor so that the execution of actions triggered by rules and scheduled jobs can be monitored in the Process app.
The Action Monitor does the following:
Reads action commands from topic "avid.actionengine.commands" and publish these commands
- with status “pending”.
- Reads action status from topic "avid.actionengine.status" and
  - updates commands with “in progress”, when starting with the action processing.
  - updates commands with final state “completed” or “failed”, when done.
- Reports the action status to the Job Monitor (Process app backend) so that actions become visible in
the Process app.
In the Process app, the following are shown:
The execution of rules
- The execution of scheduled jobs (when they were triggered)
- Actions triggered by rules and scheduled jobs
- Use the Process app to monitor the execution of platform automation actions. See the following topic:
"Monitoring Platform Automation Actions in the Process App" below
- The Process additionally provides options for manual and scheduled retries of failed platform automation
actions. See the following topic:
Monitoring Platform Automation Actions in the Process App Use the Process app to monitor the execution of platform automation actions, for example, to check if an action is shown or the action execution is "hanging".
> **Note:** Only administrators or users of a group with Master of Jobs entitlement can monitor the execution of
actions triggered by rules and scheduled jobs in the Process app.
By default, processes and jobs of all types currently being used are shown as a list in the Process app. You can modify the Process List to show only rules, actions and scheduled jobs by using the following filters.
Note that all filters are cumulative.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Control Action Groups filter (sidebar) The following Groups filters can be used for monitoring the execution of individual rules, actions and scheduled jobs:
You can filter actions by applying the sidebar filter
- Groups > "Action: <action type>"
Example: "Action: am-orchestration" or "Action: ctms- addtofolder"
- You can filter rules by applying the sidebar filter
Groups > "Rule: <rule name>" You can filter scheduled jobs by applying the sidebar filter
- Groups > "Schedule: <scheduled job name>"
System filter (sidebar) The following System filter can be used for monitoring the execution of all rules, actions and scheduled jobs:
- Automation
Status filter (sidebar and toolbar) Use the Status filter to show only actions of a specific status (such as Running or Failed).
Show Hierarchy toggle (toolbar) To visualize the hierarchy of the actions triggered by a rule or scheduled job, select the Show Hierarchy toggle button in the toolbar.
The actions are shown as children of the triggering rule or scheduled job.
In the Process List, rules, actions and scheduled jobs are shown according to the following naming convention:
Type Naming convention Rules The name is provided in the following form: <rule name>:<entity in the form system type/asset type> - [trigger] Examples:
- My Rule1: wolftech-news/wtn-story - [Updated]
- My Rule2: interplay-pam/masterclip - [Created]
- My Rule3: interplay-mam/asset.VIDEO - [Deleted]
Scheduled jobs The name is provided in the following form: <scheduled job name (Job ID)> Example: My Job (66c2f22c-ea22-66b6-22d2-2b6fb6ac6b62) Actions The name is provided in the form <technical action type name> Example: Action: mediaanalytics Special cases:
- am-orchestration action names are appended with the name of the process that
is triggered. Example: am-orchestration/MyExportProcess If an action was triggered on the results of a Search entity in a scheduled job, an
- action "search" will be shown.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App To monitor rules, actions and scheduled jobs in the Process app:
1. Click the Process button on the Fast Bar.
2. Open the Filters sidebar.
3. To show only rules, actions and scheduled jobs by using the "System" filter:
a. Expand the System list in the Filters sidebar if needed.
b. Select the Automation check box.
The Process app shows you only rules, actions triggered by rules/scheduled jobs and scheduled jobs triggered by the Schedule Engine.
4. To show only specific rules, actions or scheduled jobs by using the "Groups" filter:
a. Expand the Groups list in the Filters sidebar if needed.
b. Click the "show all" button.
c. In the break-out window that opens, select the rules, actions or scheduled jobs you want to display and then click the Apply button.
The Process app shows you only the corresponding items.
5. To group the displayed rules, actions, and scheduled jobs:
a. Click the Display Group button so it turns blue.
Rules, actions and scheduled jobs are grouped by their type in the Process List.
b. Click the Expand/Collapse toggle button of each type to show its items.
6. (optional) To visualize the hierarchy:
a. Click the Show Hierarchy button in the Process app toolbar so it turns blue.
Actions are arranged below their triggering rule or scheduled job in the Process List. If the action was triggered on search results by a scheduled job, the hierarchy is scheduled job > search > action.
b. Click the Expand button to the left side of a rule or scheduled job to show the triggered actions.
7. (optional) Apply additional filter criteria.
a. (Filters sidebar) To filter the shown items by creation time ranges, expand the Creation Time list and select one of the predefined time ranges.
b. (Filters sidebar and Toolbar) To show only items of a specific status, expand the Status list and select the status you want to show or click the corresponding Status button in the toolbar.
Multi-selection is supported. You can use the following filters:
Icon Status Created (Should be present only for a very short time) Pending (Should be present only for a very short time) Running (Should be present only for a very short time) Completed Failed 16 Using the Schedule Editor App Retrying Failed Platform Automation Actions in the Process App You can use the Process app to not only monitor the execution of platform automation actions, but also to monitor and initiate retry actions for failed platform automation actions.
For failed platform automation actions, by default a Retry option is provided. Additionally, scheduled retry might by possible if configured in the Action Monitor config map. This configuration also defines what happens with scheduled retries after a manual retry has been applied. For more information, see "Configuring Long-Term Retry for Platform Automation Actions" on page 600.
In the Process app, the following controls are shown for retries:
Button Meaning Lets you retry a failed platform automation action.
The manual Retry button is shown for failed platform automation actions and after all scheduled retries have been executed for a failed action. When you click the Retry button it is disabled and turns blue for 5 seconds.
Indicates that an auto-retry is scheduled for the failed action.
The Name of each retried action is appended by the "<Retry [counter] (auto)>" suffix.
When you hover the mouse pointer over the auto-retry button, a tool-tip shows the scheduled retry date and time (format according to the time zone and time format set in your User Settings).
You can also click the auto-retry button to initiate a manual retry before the scheduled retry is executed. When you click the button it is disabled and turns blue for 5 seconds.
Lets you cancel a scheduled retry. If you cancel a scheduled retry action, the following happens:
- In the backend, the one time job is removed from the Schedule Engine and the Job Monitor
job is updated.
- In the Process app, the Auto-retry and Cancel buttons are removed, and the manual Retry
button is shown.
When you click the button it is disabled and turns blue for 5 seconds.
To use retry options in the Process app:
1. Click the Process button on the Fast Bar.
2. Apply filters as needed.
3. To initiate a manual retry, click the Retry button of the action.
4. To use the auto-retry:
- Monitor the auto-retry actions. In the following example, a long-term retry for the action
wtnews-createpublishingpoint (action label: Create Publishing Output) has been configured with 5 retries and a delay of 30 seonds.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App When the last scheduled retry is executed, the Auto-retry button is replaced by the manual Retry button.
- To initiate a manual retry before the next scheduled retry is executed, click the Auto-retry
button.
A manual retry is triggered and the Auto-retry button is replaced by the manual Retry button.
- To cancel a scheduled retry, click the Cancel button to the right side of a Auto-retry button.
The Auto-retry and Cancel buttons are removed, and the manual Retry button is shown.
Using the Kubernetes Dashboard for Troubleshooting Use the Kubernetes Dashboard to check if a platform automation component is deployed and running, and to monitor the log files.
Components The following Platform Automation components are used by the Schedule Editor:
Name Feature Deployment (Kubernetes) Schedule Engine scheduleengine avid-platform-scheduleengine-scheduleengine Action Engines The following Action Engines are part of the Platform Automation and are used by the Schedule Engine and Schedule Editor:
Name Actions Feature Deployment (Kubernetes) Core1 Action Engine <none> ae-core <none> Action Engine AM- AM Orchestration ae-am- avid-platform-ae-am- Orchestration (am-orchestration) orchestration orchestration-ae-am- orchestration Action Engine CTMS Create Entity ae-ctms avid-platform-ae-ctms-ae-ctms (ctms-createentity) Update Metadata (ctms- updatemetadata) 16 Using the Schedule Editor App Name Actions Feature Deployment (Kubernetes) Create Folder (ctms-createfolder) Add To Folder (ctms-addtofolder) Action Engine Media Media Analytics ae-mediaanalytics avid-platform-ae-mediaanalytics- Analytics (mediaanalytics) ae-mediaanalytics Action Engine Notification ae-notification avid-platform-ae-notification-ae- Notification (platform-notification) notification Action Engine PM-STP Send to Playback ae-pm-stp avid-platform-ae-pm-stp-ae-pm- (pm-stp) stp Action Engine Publish publisher avid-platform-ae-publisher-ae- Publisher (publish) publisher 1 Central settings for Action Engines. Is deployed with first deployment of any Action Engine.
The following Action Engine is part of the Platform Automation but only used by the Schedule Engine:
Name Actions Feature Deployment (Kubernetes) Action Engine <none, but is called by the Schedule Engine for ae-search avid-platform-ae- Search scheduled jobs that include a Search entity> search-ae-search To use the Kubernetes Dashboard:
1. Log in to the Kubernetes Dashboard of the platform host.
For more information, see "Accessing the Kubernetes Dashboard" on page 626.
2. Search for the Deployment you want to check (see tables above).
3. Check if the Deployment appears in the list.
If the Component is not shown under Deployments, the component is probably not deployed on this host.
4. Check if a specific service component is running: Search for the name, then check if the Deployment
and it’s instances (Pods) are running.
5. Check the log file that contains details about a component: Search for the Component and look for a
matching pod to show it’s logfile.
For more information, see "Collecting Logs Through the Dashboard" on page 628.
6. If an error is shown for the pod, consider deleting the pod.
16 Using the Schedule Editor App Kubernetes recognizes the change and initiates the creation of a new pod to take its place.
For more information, see "Deleting a Pod" on page 626.
7. If the Deployment is marked red with an error, consider doing one of the following:
- Restart the component (deployment).
- Delete all pods of the component. Kubernetes will start new instances of the service (as many
as defined in the Deployment).
Make sure that you only delete the pods. Do not delete the Config Maps, Deployments, or other.
Checking Kafka Topics for Troubleshooting The Platform Automation is designed as an asynchronous, event driven, messaging system. Services are communicating by exchanging messages via Kafka.
For troubleshooting it can help to evaluate the messages populated to the respective Kafka topic. A Kafka client can be used for this purpose. The following Kafka topics are relevant in context of the Platform Automation.
avid.actionengine.commands Action Engine services are processing relevant messages of this topic.
avid.actionengine.status Action Engine services are populating the current status of an action command being executed.
17 Avid MediaCentral | Cloud UX Mobile App 17 Avid MediaCentral | Cloud UX Mobile App The Avid MediaCentral Cloud UX mobile app enables users of iOS devices to extend MediaCentral Cloud UX workflows to the mobile platform. This app enables direct, secure access to your station’s Newsroom Management and / or Production Management systems.
The Avid MediaCentral Cloud UX mobile app connects to your environment in one of two ways:
- Wi-Fi
- Carrier-specific cellular service— for example, 3G, 4G or Edge
> **Note:** For information on the MediaCentral Cloud UX app for Android, see v2021.11 or earlier of the Avid
MediaCentral | Cloud UX Installation Guide.
Before You Begin Before using the MediaCentral Cloud UX app, verify the following tasks have been completed:
Confirm that the mobile device can access the MediaCentral Cloud UX server using its Fully Qualified
- Domain Name (FQDN).
It is vital that the fully qualified domain names (FQDN) for all MediaCentral Cloud UX servers are resolvable by the domain name server (DNS) tasked with doing so. This is particularly important when the MediaCentral Cloud UX servers are accessed from the Cloud UX mobile app. Mobile devices that cannot resolve the FQDN of the servers experience issues connecting to the player service. Hostname resolution issues might allow mobile users to sign into MediaCentral Cloud UX, but media playback might be intermittent or not possible. While advanced users might be able to adjust the network settings directly on a mobile device, manual adjustments should not be required and are not the recommended best practice.
> **Note:** When connecting to a MediaCentral Cloud UX server or cluster through the mobile app’s sign-in
window, Avid recommends using the server’s FQDN (fully qualified domain name).
- Confirm that your server has been licensed with the appropriate number of Client Licenses and
features for your workflow. For more information, see "Using the License App" on page 176.
If you plan on connecting to MediaCentral Cloud UX remotely, you must establish a VPN connection
- between your mobile device and your corporate network.
For more information, see "Remote Client Connections" on page 26.
Installing MediaCentral | Cloud UX for iOS The following procedure assumes that licensing, setup, and configuration of the MediaCentral Cloud UX, Newsroom Management, and / or Production Management servers have already been completed
> **Note:** If you are using the MediaCentral Cloud UX Mobile app, you must import a valid certificate into your
mobile device. For more information, see "Importing TLS Certificates" on page 604.
To install the mobile app on an iPad or iPhone:
1. Open the iOS App Store.
2. Locate the Avid MediaCentral Cloud UX mobile application.
3. Select the application and tap Download.
17 Avid MediaCentral | Cloud UX Mobile App When the MediaCentral Cloud UX mobile application is installed on your device, an icon representing the application appears on the home screen. You can move it elsewhere like the icons for other applications.
A direct link to the Avid MediaCentral Cloud UX app on the App Store is provided here (link current at time of publication):
4. https://apps.apple.com/us/app/mediacentral-cloud-ux/id1280863531
For additional information on the configuration and usage of the MediaCentral Cloud UX mobile app, see the Avid MediaCentral | Cloud UX User’s Guide.
Upgrading the Mobile App If your iOS device is configured to automatically update the MediaCentral Cloud UX app, newer versions of the software are installed automatically as they become available.
If you have deselected the option to auto-update, you must manually upgrade the app by selecting the Update button on the app from within the App Store.
The App Store limits developers, such as Avid, to have only the most recent version of the app available for download. Prior to upgrading the MediaCentral Cloud UX app, you might want to create a backup of the current version of the app. A backup enables you to restore an earlier version of the app in the event that you are unsatisfied with the updates made in the new version.
To back up an app:
Visit the Apple website for detailed instructions on how to back up apps on your iOS device:
https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT203977 To restore an older version of an app:
1. Uninstall the current version of the app from your iPhone or iPad.
2. Connect the device to a desktop workstation that has iTunes installed.
3. In iTunes, click on the “Apps” link from the sidebar.
This menu displays the apps that were on your device at the time of the last backup.
4. Select the previous version of the MediaCentral Cloud UX app from the Apps pane and drag it to the
device (iPhone or iPad) section of the sidebar.
5. Sync your device with iTunes.
The previous version of the app is reinstalled on your device.
For more information on syncing your iOS device with Apple Cloud, see the following link on the Apple website: https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT201253 A Additional Topics A Additional Topics The purpose of this appendix is to provide additional information and detail on topics included in the main body of the MediaCentral Cloud UX Installation Guide.
The following main topics are described in this chapter:
"Power Cycling and Maintenance Mode" below
- Power Cycling and Maintenance Mode
As you know, the Kubernetes software installed on a MediaCentral Cloud UX server manages resources to ensure continual operation of the system. If a highly available service goes down for any reason, Kubernetes identifies the missing service and restarts it.
In most cases, you should not need to reboot or shut down a MediaCentral Cloud UX server. Avid does not recommend performing a full shutdown of the system — especially in clusters where a full shut down or reboot is often unnecessary as services are distributed across multiple nodes. If you need to complete a task on a particular node that might result in an interruption of service to the users, Kubernetes includes a command that allows you to put a node into a maintenance mode. This command drains the Kubernetes managed resources from the node so that you can perform any required maintenance tasks. After the maintenance is complete, you add the node back as a managed resource.
If you must shut down or reboot a node, you must put the Kubernetes controlled resources into maintenance mode first. This is required for both single-server and cluster configurations, but it is especially important in cluster configurations. This avoids the possibility of Kubernetes attempting to restart a service that was intentionally shut down.
A Additional Topics This section includes information on how to put a server into maintenance mode and how to reboot or shut down a MediaCentral Cloud UX server. These processes apply to both single-server and cluster configurations. For additional information on rebooting a clustered node, see "Rebooting a MediaCentral Cloud UX Cluster" on page 578.
If your installation includes MediaCentral Phonetic Index, the processes described in this section do not apply to the Search Grid server. You are not required to shut down or boot the Search Grid server at any specific point in the MediaCentral Cloud UX power-up or power-down cycle. When both systems are fully available (after a power-up), they begin to communicate automatically.
For information on additional Kubernetes commands and cluster management, see:
https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/ minutes depending on your configuration and deployed feature packs) before attempting to access the MediaCentral Cloud UX through your web browser. This allows time for Kubernetes to restart all the pods and services on the required node or nodes.
MediaCentral Production Management Note While you can restart a MediaCentral Cloud UX server without an impact to the Production Management, the reverse is not true. If you need to reboot the MediaCentral | Production Index (Media Indexer) server(s), you must sign in to Kubernetes and delete the pods related to playback. Complete the following process to restart these pods:
1. Sign into Kubernetes.
For more information, see "Accessing the Kubernetes Dashboard" on page 626.
2. Enter “playback” in the search tool at the top of the screen.
avid-playback-<value> One or more pods are listed in the search. If you have a single-server, you should see only one pod. If you are running a clustered configuration, you should see multiple pods.
3. Click on the menu to the right of the pod to delete each of the playback pods.
The Kubernetes management system detects that the pod has been deleted and automatically creates a new pod to replace it.
4. Repeat the steps above to delete the avid-render-<value> pod.
Putting a Node in Maintenance Mode The following process applies to single-server configurations or to a single node in a MediaCentral Cloud UX cluster.
To put a single-server or cluster node into maintenance mode:
1. Use an SSH client such as PuTTY to access the node that you want to put into maintenance mode. For
details, see "Logging in to Ubuntu for the First Time" on page 47.
2. Enter the following command to put the node into maintenance mode:
sudo avidctl node offline This command drains all services from the node and prevents Kubernetes from scheduling new services on it. This command also shuts down host services and deletes local data (e.g. caches) on the node, but it does not delete Database information.
3. Perform any maintenance tasks on the node as needed.
4. Bring the node back online using the following command:
sudo avidctl node online A Additional Topics
5. Wait a few minutes and then enter the following command to obtain the status of the Kubernetes
node or nodes:
sudo kubectl get nodes Each server in your MediaCentral Cloud UX configuration should be listed under the name column and the status of each node should report as Ready. The following example text shows a three node cluster configuration:
NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION wavd-mcux01 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux02 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux03 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux04 Ready <none> 10m v1.32.4+k3s1
6. After the nodes report as Ready, enter the following command to obtain the status of the individual
pods:
sudo kubectl get pods Before you release the system back to the users, you should verify that all pods report as Running.
Although these status values do not guaranty that all pods are started and ready, it is a good indicator of the system health.
For more information, see "Working with Kubernetes" on page 623.
Rebooting or Shutting Down a Node The following process applies to single-server configurations or to a single node in a MediaCentral Cloud UX cluster.
To reboot or shutdown a single-server or cluster node:
1. Use an SSH client such as PuTTY to access the node that you want to put into maintenance mode. For
details, see "Logging in to Ubuntu for the First Time" on page 47.
2. Enter the following command to put the node into maintenance mode:
sudo avidctl node offline This command drains all services from the node and prevents Kubernetes from scheduling new services on it. This command also deletes local data (e.g. caches) on the node, but it does not delete Database information.
3. Enter one of the following commands to reboot or shut down the node:
- To reboot a node, type the following:
sudo reboot
- To shut down a node, type the following:
sudo shutdown -h now
4. After you reboot or power-on the server, you must enter the following command to bring the
Kubernetes resources back online:
sudo avidctl node online
5. Wait a few minutes and then enter the following command to obtain the status of the Kubernetes
node or nodes:
sudo kubectl get nodes Each server in your MediaCentral Cloud UX configuration should be listed under the name column and the status of each node should report as Ready. The following example text shows a three node cluster configuration:
A Additional Topics NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION wavd-mcux01 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux02 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux03 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux04 Ready <none> 10m v1.32.4+k3s1
6. After the nodes report as Ready, enter the following command to obtain the status of the individual
pods:
sudo kubectl get pods Before you release the system back to the users, you should verify that all pods report as Running.
Although these status values do not guaranty that all pods are started and ready, it is a good indicator of the system health.
For more information, see "Working with Kubernetes" on page 623.
Power Cycling a MediaCentral Cloud UX Cluster
> **Note:** If you have an unexpected shut down due to an event such as a power outage where all nodes are
powered-down simultaneously, Avid recommends that you power-up all nodes at the same time and allow the cluster to manage the role assignments. Note that an event of this nature might require you to perform additional tasks to ensure that the system returns to normal operation.
To shut down all nodes in a MediaCentral Cloud UX cluster:
1. Enter the following command from any node to drain the Kubernetes controlled resources on all
nodes simultaneously:
sudo avidctl node drain --all
2. Wait a few minutes and then enter the following command to obtain the status of the Kubernetes
nodes:
sudo kubectl get nodes When all nodes have been drained of resources, this command should report information similar to the following:
NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION wavd-mcux01 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux02 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux03 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux04 Ready <none> 10m v1.32.4+k3s1
3. After all nodes report a Ready status, enter the following command on all nodes to shut down the
Cloud UX servers:
sudo shutdown -h now
4. Avid recommends that you power-down the nodes in descending order. For example if you have a
four node cluster, you would shut down the nodes in the following order: 4 (worker), 3 (control plane), 2 (control plane), 1 (control plane primary).
To power-on all nodes in a MediaCentral Cloud UX cluster:
1. Power on all nodes in any order.
You do not need to power-on all nodes simultaneously, but all nodes at approximately the same time is recommended.
2. Wait a few minutes and then enter the following command to obtain the status of the Kubernetes
nodes:
sudo kubectl get nodes A Additional Topics This command should report information similar to the following:
NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION wavd-mcux01 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux02 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux03 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux04 Ready <none> 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 Repeat this command as necessary. After all nodes report a Ready status, continue to the next step.
3. Enter the following command from any node to bring the cluster out of maintenance mode:
sudo avidctl node uncordon --all
4. Wait a few minutes and then enter the following command to obtain the status of the Kubernetes
nodes:
sudo kubectl get nodes Each server in your MediaCentral Cloud UX configuration should be listed under the name column and the status of each node should report as Ready. The following example text shows a three node cluster configuration:
NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION wavd-mcux01 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux02 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux03 Ready control-plane,etcd,master 10m v1.32.4+k3s1 wavd-mcux04 Ready <none> 10m v1.32.4+k3s1
5. After the nodes report as Ready, enter the following command to obtain the status of the individual
pods:
sudo kubectl get pods Before you release the system back to the users, you should verify that all pods report as Running.
Although these status values do not guaranty that all pods are started and ready, it is a good indicator of the system health.
For more information, see "Working with Kubernetes" on page 623.
MediaCentral Cloud UX System Maintenance Avid builds MediaCentral Cloud UX on the Linux (Ubuntu) operating system with server-class hardware and multiple redundant systems, making it a very robust product. As such, MediaCentral Cloud UX requires very little routine maintenance as compared to some other systems. The following are just some of the elements that work together to reduce or eliminate standard maintenance procedures:
- Linux cron jobs manage log files, automatically archiving, rotating, and deleting older logs as newer
log files are created.
Self monitoring of the Data volume ensures that you do not fill the drive to capacity with cached
- media assets. After the system meets a certain threshold, older assets are deleted automatically. If a
user needs access to an asset whose proxy has been deleted, the system creates new proxy media automatically — “on the fly”. The same can be said for the MP4 proxy files used in the Hoverscrub workflow.
Kubernetes monitors pods to automatically restart systems in the event of a failure. For more
- information on this sub-system, see "Working with Kubernetes" on page 623.
(optional) Clustering multiple MediaCentral Cloud UX servers not only increases the potential user
- and stream counts, but enables high availability of many systems in the event of a hardware failure.
A Additional Topics While many of these systems are fully automated, Avid highly recommends that system administrators deploy the optional system monitoring services to help ensure that any potential issues are found and resolved quickly. Once installed, Administrators can use Grafana actively monitor memory usage, disk space, and other system basics. For more information, see "Enabling System Monitoring" on page 124.
Per "Power Cycling and Maintenance Mode" on page 555, Avid does not recommend performing a full shutdown of the system — especially in clusters where a full shut down or reboot is often unnecessary as services are distributed across multiple nodes. Avid also does not recommend “forced failovers” for clustered configurations.
Avid recommends the following regular maintenance steps:
- Create periodical backups of the MongoDB database. For details, see "Backing up the Mongo
Database" on page 595.
- Organizations that deploy self-signed certificates need to plan on recreating their certificates on a
recurring basis. The maximum validity period for these certificates is 397 days. After that period expires, the certificate is considered invalid and a new one would need to be created. This same guidance might also apply to organizations that create internally-signed certificates. For more information, see "Creating Certificate Files" on page 78.
- If you are using Hoverscrub, run the proxy storage report to verify if you have an excessive amount of
small (<750k) proxy files. The frequency at which you run this script is heavily dependent on your workflow (number of users, number of modules, etc). For more information, see "Cleaning the Hoverscrub Cache" on page 582.
Copying Software to the MCUX Server When working with MediaCentral Cloud UX, you might be required to copy software to or from the MediaCentral Cloud UX server. This section details two methods for completing this task:
"Copying Software Using an SFTP Client" below
- While the SFTP method might be preferred for ease of access, the USB method might be required for some
operations.
